yemeni.meaning: Evokes the elevated dignity and authority of one born to command with grace.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Amira, meaning 'princess' or 'leader.' Widely used in Yemen; reflects the Arabic tradition of names celebrating feminine nobility.

yemeni.name: Walid
yemeni.meaning: Carries the fresh vitality of a newborn, the very first breath of a life just beginning.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Walid, meaning 'newborn.' Common in Yemen; reflects the Arabic tradition of names celebrating the moment of birth.

yemeni.name: Muna
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of the heartfelt wishes and deep desires one holds close and hopes to see fulfilled.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Muna, meaning 'wishes' or 'desires.' Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; a name of longing and aspiration.

yemeni.name: Hamdan
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of one greatly praised, whose deeds inspire acknowledgment on every side.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Hamdan, meaning 'the praised one.' Very common in Yemen; associated with the ancient Hamdanid dynasty and deep Yemeni tribal heritage.

yemeni.name: Samira
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the warmth of evening company, conversations flowing freely under the stars.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Samira, meaning 'evening companion.' Common in Yemen and across North Africa and the Levant; widely recognized across the Arab world.

yemeni.name: Mansour
yemeni.meaning: Carries the triumphant quality of one aided by the divine and helped to final victory.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Mansur, meaning 'victorious' or 'divinely aided.' Very common in Yemen; reflects the Islamic tradition of names expressing divine support.

yemeni.name: Fatima
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of one who holds herself apart with sacred dignity and quiet inner grace.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Fatima. One of the most common female names in Yemen; the name of the Prophet Muhammad's daughter, deeply venerated in Islamic tradition.

yemeni.name: Yasir
yemeni.meaning: Reflects the ease and gentleness of one who takes the soft path without needless hardship.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Yasir, meaning 'easy' or 'flexible.' Common in Yemen; reflects the Islamic value of ease and kindness in daily life.

yemeni.name: Najla
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the wide and beautiful eyes whose gaze draws attention and holds it with quiet power.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Najla, meaning 'wide-eyed.' Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the poetic Arabic tradition of celebrating the eyes as beauty's measure.

yemeni.name: Saleh
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of one who is righteous and upright, living correctly in all their dealings.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Salih, meaning 'righteous' or 'good.' Very common in Yemen; the name of a Quranic prophet associated with the ancient people of Thamud.

yemeni.name: Layla
yemeni.meaning: Carries the mystery and beauty of the night, dark and still and full of unspoken things.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Layla, meaning 'night.' Very common in Yemen and across the Arab world; immortalized in the classical Arabic love poetry tradition.

yemeni.name: Qahtan
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the ancient and proud lineage of a great patriarch, the ancestor of a people.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name associated with Qahtan, the legendary ancestor of the South Arabian peoples. Deeply embedded in Yemeni tribal identity; one of the most distinctively Yemeni male names.

yemeni.name: Wafa
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of loyalty and the faithful keeping of every promise made with a sincere heart.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Wafa', meaning 'loyalty' or 'faithfulness.' Common in Yemen; reflects the Yemeni cultural emphasis on personal honor and keeping one's word.

yemeni.name: Abdo
yemeni.meaning: Carries the devoted humility of one who serves a purpose greater than themselves.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name, a shortened form of Abdullah. Very common in Yemen; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of servitude to God embedded in everyday Yemeni culture.

yemeni.name: Thuraya
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of the Pleiades star cluster, seven brilliant points scattered across the night sky.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Thuraya, meaning 'the Pleiades.' Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the ancient Arab tradition of celestial navigation and star-naming.

yemeni.name: Khaled
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the enduring quality of one who remains, unchanged and eternal across all time.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Khalid, meaning 'eternal' or 'immortal.' One of the most common male names in Yemen and across the Arab world.

yemeni.name: Zainab
yemeni.meaning: Reflects the beauty and fragrance of a blossoming plant adorned with its full flowers.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Zaynab. Very common in Yemen; the name of the Prophet Muhammad's daughter and granddaughter, both deeply revered in Islamic tradition.

yemeni.name: Faisal
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of the decisive one who separates truth from falsehood with a single clear stroke.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Faysal, meaning 'decisive' or 'arbitrator.' Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the Yemeni tribal tradition of respected judgment.

yemeni.name: Nawal
yemeni.meaning: Carries the gift of something freely given and the grace of receiving what one did not expect.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Nawal, meaning 'gift' or 'grace.' Used in Yemen and across North Africa; reflects the Arabic tradition of names expressing unexpected blessings.

yemeni.name: Raghib
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the desire and yearning of one who reaches toward what they love with full intention.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Raghib, meaning 'desirous' or 'willing.' Used in Yemen; reflects the Yemeni tradition of names expressing purposeful aspiration.

yemeni.name: Asma
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of elevated standing and the lofty dignity of one of distinguished character.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Asma, meaning 'lofty' or 'elevated names.' Common in Yemen; associated with Asma bint Abi Bakr, a revered companion of the Prophet.

yemeni.name: Samir
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of the evening companion whose presence turns the night into a time of warmth.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Samir, meaning 'evening entertainer' or 'companion.' Common in Yemen; reflects the Arab tradition of valued evening gatherings and storytelling.

yemeni.name: Marwa
yemeni.meaning: Carries the sacred significance of the white stone hill, a place of ancient pilgrimage.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name referring to Marwa, one of the hills in Mecca central to the Hajj. Very common in Yemen; reflects the deep Islamic faith woven into everyday Yemeni naming culture.

yemeni.name: Hatem
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the legendary generosity of one whose open-handedness has become a byword for giving.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from Hatem al-Tai, the legendary Yemeni figure of generosity. Very common in Yemen; reflects the profound Yemeni cultural pride in the virtue of generosity.

yemeni.name: Iman
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of steadfast faith, a belief so firm it holds one upright in every storm.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Iman, meaning 'faith' or 'belief.' Common in Yemen; reflects the centrality of Islamic faith in Yemeni personal and communal identity.

yemeni.name: Badr
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of the full moon at its most complete, flooding the night with silver light.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Badr, meaning 'full moon.' Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; associated with the Battle of Badr, a significant event in Islamic history.

yemeni.name: Saba
yemeni.meaning: Carries the gentle morning breeze, cool and refreshing, moving softly through the world.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Saba, meaning 'morning breeze' or also evoking the ancient Kingdom of Saba (Sheba). Used in Yemen; carries both natural and historical resonance.

yemeni.name: Mahdi
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the guided one, the person directed rightly toward what is true and just.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Mahdi, meaning 'the rightly guided.' Common in Yemen; carries Quranic significance and is used widely across Shia and Sunni Muslim communities.

yemeni.name: Widad
yemeni.meaning: Reflects the tender affection and love shared between people deeply fond of one another.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Widad, meaning 'affection' or 'love.' Used in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the Yemeni tradition of names expressing emotional warmth.

yemeni.name: Ammar
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of a long life, flourishing across many years of building and creating.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Ammar, meaning 'long-lived' or 'one who builds.' Common in Yemen; the name of Ammar ibn Yasir, a revered companion of the Prophet.

yemeni.name: Ibtisam
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the smile spreading across a face, gentle and irresistible in its warmth.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Ibtisam, meaning 'smiling' or 'a smile.' Used in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the value of a warm and welcoming demeanor.

yemeni.name: Yahya
yemeni.meaning: Carries the living vitality of one who thrives and brings life to everything around them.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Yahya, meaning 'he lives.' One of the most common male names in Yemen; the Quranic name for the prophet John the Baptist.

yemeni.name: Bushra
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of good news and the joyful tidings that lift the heart of all who hear them.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Bushra, meaning 'good news' or 'glad tidings.' Common in Yemen; reflects the Islamic tradition of names expressing positive and hopeful qualities.

yemeni.name: Ghalib
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of one who prevails and overcomes, emerging victorious from every challenge.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Ghalib, meaning 'victorious' or 'dominant.' Used in Yemen; reflects the Yemeni tribal tradition of names expressing strength and prevailing character.

yemeni.name: Maysaa
yemeni.meaning: Carries the graceful swaying walk of one who moves through the world with elegant confidence.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Maysa', meaning 'walking with a proud gait.' Used in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the classical Arabic poetic tradition of naming.

yemeni.name: Rashid
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the rightly guided character of one who knows the true path and walks it faithfully.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Rashid, meaning 'rightly guided.' Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the Islamic value of divine guidance.

yemeni.name: Hessa
yemeni.meaning: Speaks of the share and portion set aside for one who is deserving of their place.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Hissa, meaning 'share' or 'portion.' Used in Yemen and the Gulf; reflects the naming tradition of the Arabian Peninsula.

yemeni.name: Abdulrahman
yemeni.meaning: Carries the devoted service to the most compassionate, the merciful one above all others.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Abd al-Rahman, meaning 'servant of the Most Merciful.' One of the most common male names in Yemen; reflects the centrality of Islamic faith.

yemeni.name: Randa
yemeni.meaning: Paints the image of a fragrant desert tree whose sweet scent drifts on the breeze.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni female name derived from the Arabic Randa, referring to a fragrant desert tree. Common in Yemen and across the Arab world; reflects the poetic tradition of botanical naming.

yemeni.name: Nasser
yemeni.meaning: Evokes the helper and supporter who stands alongside another and ensures their victory.
yemeni.notes: A Yemeni male name derived from the Arabic Nasir, meaning 'helper' or 'supporter.' Very common in Yemen; reflects the Islamic tradition and the deep Yemeni cultural value of solidarity.

persian.name: Shirin
persian.meaning: Carries the sweetness of something delightful that dissolves on the tongue like honey.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'sweet' or 'pleasant.' Very common in Iran; associated with the legendary Sassanid queen Shirin, immortalized in the classical Persian epic poetry of Nizami.

persian.name: Dariush
persian.meaning: Evokes the one who holds goodness firmly and guards what is precious without loosening his grip.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'he who holds firm to good.' Common in Iran; the Persian form of Darius, carried by ancient Achaemenid kings and still widely used in modern Iran.

persian.name: Nasrin
persian.meaning: Paints the image of the wild rose climbing freely, its petals soft and its fragrance drifting wide.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'wild rose' or 'briar rose.' Very common in Iran; one of the most beloved Persian female names; associated with the rose imagery central to Persian poetry.

persian.name: Arash
persian.meaning: Carries the swift precision of the legendary archer whose arrow flew across the entire land.
persian.notes: A Persian male name associated with Arash the Archer, a hero of Persian mythology. Very common in Iran; one of the most recognizable traditional male names celebrating pre-Islamic Persian heritage.

persian.name: Mahsa
persian.meaning: Speaks of the moon's companion, one who walks alongside the silver light of night.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'like the moon' or 'moon-like.' Very common in Iran; one of the most popular female names in modern Iranian society.

persian.name: Kamran
persian.meaning: Reflects the successful and fortunate quality of one who achieves everything they set out to do.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'successful' or 'fortunate.' Common in Iran and Afghanistan; reflects the Persian naming tradition of names expressing prosperity and achievement.

persian.name: Bahar
persian.meaning: Evokes the spring season bursting with new blossoms and the fresh energy of renewal.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'spring.' Very common in Iran; reflects the Persian cultural celebration of Nowruz (Persian New Year) and the deep symbolic importance of spring.

persian.name: Reza
persian.meaning: Carries the divine contentment and the approval of one who is satisfied with what is given.
persian.notes: A Persian male name derived from the Arabic Rida, meaning 'contentment' or 'divine approval.' Very common in Iran; associated with Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Ridha, deeply revered in Shia Islam.

persian.name: Golnar
persian.meaning: Paints the image of the pomegranate blossom, red and full, jeweled with seeds of abundance.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'pomegranate blossom.' Used in Iran and Afghanistan; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of floral and botanical names celebrating natural beauty.

persian.name: Farhad
persian.meaning: Speaks of the joy and happiness that accompanies one who radiates cheerful vitality.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'happiness' or 'joy.' Very common in Iran; associated with the legendary stonemason Farhad in Persian classical poetry, who cut mountains for love.

persian.name: Setareh
persian.meaning: Evokes the single star shining quietly in the dark sky, steady and enduring through the night.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'star.' Common in Iran; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of celestial imagery and the cultural significance of stars in Persian literature and astronomy.

persian.name: Mehran
persian.meaning: Carries the solar warmth of a name radiant with light and the kindness of giving rays.
persian.notes: A Persian male name combining mehr (sun or love) with the suffix -an. Common in Iran; reflects the Zoroastrian heritage in which the sun and light hold central spiritual significance.

persian.name: Parisa
persian.meaning: Paints the image of a fairy-like creature of ethereal beauty, otherworldly and enchanting.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'like a fairy' or 'fairy-faced.' Very popular in Iran; reflects the Persian literary tradition of the pari (fairy), a being of great beauty in Persian mythology.

persian.name: Siavash
persian.meaning: Evokes the black horse, powerful and swift, an image of noble masculine force.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'he who owns black horses.' One of the most beloved names in Iran; the hero Siavash in the Shahnameh epic of Ferdowsi is a symbol of innocence and tragic nobility.

persian.name: Darya
persian.meaning: Carries the vast expanse of the sea, deep and restless, holding everything within it.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'sea' or 'ocean.' Common in Iran; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of vast natural imagery; also popular among Iranian diaspora communities worldwide.

persian.name: Bahram
persian.meaning: Speaks of the victorious one, the warrior who defeats his enemies and stands triumphant.
persian.notes: A Persian male name related to the planet Mars and meaning 'victorious.' Very common in Iran; associated with several Sassanid kings and carries deep roots in pre-Islamic Persian culture.

persian.name: Maryam
persian.meaning: Reflects the graceful purity and spiritual depth of a revered and deeply honored woman.
persian.notes: A Persian female name, the Persian form of Mary. Very common in Iran among both Muslim and Christian families; reflects the veneration of Maryam in both Islam and Christianity.

persian.name: Omid
persian.meaning: Evokes hope and the bright expectation of better things yet to come on the horizon.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'hope.' Very common in Iran; one of the most widely used Persian male names; reflects the Persian cultural value of optimism and forward-looking spirit.

persian.name: Yasaman
persian.meaning: Paints the image of jasmine flowers, delicate white blossoms spilling their sweetness into the air.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'jasmine.' Common in Iran; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of floral names and the cultural importance of jasmine in Persian garden aesthetics.

persian.name: Ardeshir
persian.meaning: Carries the righteous fire that burns cleanly and illuminates all that is upright.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'holy fire' or 'righteous fire.' Used in Iran; associated with the Sassanid king Ardeshir and carries deep Zoroastrian heritage in which fire is sacred.

persian.name: Fereshteh
persian.meaning: Speaks of the angel whose radiant purity moves between the divine and the earthly world.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'angel.' Common in Iran; reflects the Persian Islamic and Zoroastrian tradition of angelic beings as messengers of beauty and divine grace.

persian.name: Kaveh
persian.meaning: Evokes the heroic blacksmith who raised his leather apron as a banner against tyranny.
persian.notes: A Persian male name associated with Kaveh the Blacksmith, the legendary hero of the Shahnameh who led a revolt against the tyrant Zahhak. Very common in Iran as a symbol of justice.

persian.name: Paria
persian.meaning: Paints the image of a fairy creature of dazzling beauty, moving through the world with grace.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'fairy' or 'fairy-like.' Common in Iran; reflects the Persian mythological tradition of the pari as a being of extraordinary and otherworldly beauty.

persian.name: Shahram
persian.meaning: Carries the joy of a king, the happiness that belongs to one of royal bearing and good fortune.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'king's joy' or 'royal happiness.' Common in Iran; reflects the Persian cultural tradition of royal naming and the aesthetic of kingly virtue.

persian.name: Laleh
persian.meaning: Evokes the tulip, that cup-shaped flower standing upright and red in the spring field.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'tulip.' Very common in Iran; the tulip holds deep cultural and symbolic significance in Persian culture, appearing throughout classical poetry and art.

persian.name: Pouya
persian.meaning: Reflects the searching and investigative spirit of one who seeks knowledge with relentless curiosity.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'researcher' or 'one who seeks.' Common in modern Iran; reflects the Persian cultural value of intellectual pursuit and the love of learning.

persian.name: Azadeh
persian.meaning: Carries the free and unbound spirit of one who belongs to no master and lives without chains.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'free' or 'noble.' Common in Iran; also associated with a character in the Shahnameh; reflects the Persian cultural celebration of freedom and noble birth.

persian.name: Kourosh
persian.meaning: Speaks of the sun and its life-giving warmth, spreading outward to touch every living thing.
persian.notes: A Persian male name, the Persian form of Cyrus, meaning 'sun' or 'like the sun.' Very common in Iran as a name of national pride; associated with Cyrus the Great, founder of the Achaemenid Empire.

persian.name: Niloufar
persian.meaning: Evokes the lotus or water lily, floating serenely on still water, pure and untouched.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'lotus' or 'water lily.' Common in Iran and Central Asia; reflects the Persian and broader Central Asian poetic tradition of floral names.

persian.name: Sohrab
persian.meaning: Paints the image of radiant water, bright and pure, flowing with luminous clarity.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'bright water' or 'radiant.' Very common in Iran; associated with the tragic hero Sohrab in Ferdowsi's Shahnameh; one of the most emotionally resonant Persian names.

persian.name: Roxana
persian.meaning: Carries the brightness of dawn, the glowing light of early morning spreading across the sky.
persian.notes: A Persian female name derived from the ancient Persian Roshanak, meaning 'bright' or 'dawn.' Common in Iran; associated with the Bactrian princess who married Alexander the Great.

persian.name: Farzan
persian.meaning: Evokes the wisdom and learning of one whose understanding runs deep and wide.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'wise' or 'learned.' Common in Iran and Afghanistan; reflects the Persian cultural reverence for wisdom and scholarship rooted in the classical Persian literary tradition.

persian.name: Shireen
persian.meaning: Speaks of sweetness and the pleasurable delight of something gentle and deeply enjoyable.
persian.notes: A variant spelling of Shirin; a Persian female name meaning 'sweet.' Widely used in Iran and among Persian-speaking diaspora communities worldwide.

persian.name: Hormoz
persian.meaning: Carries the ancient and sacred name of the supreme wisdom, light prevailing over darkness.
persian.notes: A Persian male name derived from Ahura Mazda in its Pahlavi form. Used in Iran; reflects the Zoroastrian heritage in which the supreme deity of wisdom and light is central.

persian.name: Tara
persian.meaning: Paints the image of the star, solitary and brilliant, fixed in its place in the night sky.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'star.' Used in Iran and among diaspora communities; also found in other cultures with related meanings; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of celestial imagery.

persian.name: Navid
persian.meaning: Evokes the good news and glad tidings whose arrival lifts the spirits of all who receive it.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'good news' or 'herald.' Very common in Iran; reflects the Persian cultural tradition of names expressing positive hope and the bringing of joyful information.

persian.name: Golsa
persian.meaning: Reflects the rose garden and the beauty of being surrounded by blossoms in full fragrant flower.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'rose garden' or 'rose-like.' Used in Iran; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of the garden as a paradise of beauty.

persian.name: Bijan
persian.meaning: Speaks of the hero and the champion, one whose deeds place him above ordinary men.
persian.notes: A Persian male name associated with the hero Bijan in Ferdowsi's Shahnameh. Very common in Iran; one of the most recognizable Persian male names drawn from the classical epic tradition.

persian.name: Paniz
persian.meaning: Carries the sweetness of sugar, soft and dissolving, leaving only pleasure in its wake.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'sugar' or 'sweet.' Used in Iran; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of names evoking sweetness and sensory pleasure.

persian.name: Mehdi
persian.meaning: Evokes the guided one, directed rightly toward truth and just action.
persian.notes: A Persian male name derived from the Arabic Mahdi. Very common in Iran; carries deep significance in Shia Islam as the title of the awaited twelfth Imam, making it one of Iran's most used names.

persian.name: Elnaz
persian.meaning: Paints the image of one who moves with the graceful coquetry of someone who knows their own beauty.
persian.notes: A Persian female name combining el (tribe) and naz (grace/coquetry), meaning 'tribal grace' or 'graceful one.' Common in Iran and Azerbaijan; reflects the Persianate naming tradition.

persian.name: Arman
persian.meaning: Carries the ideal and the cherished dream of something longed for but not yet attained.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'wish,' 'ideal,' or 'dream.' Very common in Iran; reflects the Persian philosophical tradition of naming children for aspirations and unfulfilled but beautiful longings.

persian.name: Sahar
persian.meaning: Evokes the magical stillness of the hours just before sunrise, the world held in quiet suspension.
persian.notes: A Persian female name meaning 'dawn' or 'early morning.' Common in Iran and across the Persian-speaking world; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of naming women for beautiful natural phenomena.

persian.name: Shahin
persian.meaning: Reflects the sovereign falcon soaring above all others, swift and free in the open sky.
persian.notes: A Persian male name meaning 'falcon' or 'royal falcon.' Very common in Iran; the falcon is a symbol of royalty and power in Persian culture; appears throughout classical Persian poetry.

hebrew.name: Tamar
hebrew.meaning: Carries the image of the tall and graceful date palm, standing upright in the desert heat.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'date palm.' Very common in Israel; one of the most popular traditional female names across Israeli Jewish communities of all backgrounds.

hebrew.name: Eyal
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the ram's strength and the powerful vitality of one who is robustly alive.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'strength' or 'ram.' Common in Israel; a distinctly modern Israeli male name popular since the mid-twentieth century.

hebrew.name: Noa
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the gentle movement and restful calm of still water undisturbed by wind.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'movement' or 'rest.' The most popular female name in Israel for many years; reflects the modern Israeli naming tradition of reviving ancient biblical names.

hebrew.name: Ilan
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of a tree standing tall, its roots deep and its branches spreading wide.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'tree.' Common in Israel; reflects the modern Israeli naming tradition of nature names connected to the land of Israel.

hebrew.name: Shira
hebrew.meaning: Carries the beauty of song rising upward, a melody that moves the heart of all who hear.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'song' or 'poem.' Very common in Israel; one of the most popular female names in Israeli society across both Ashkenazi and Mizrahi communities.

hebrew.name: Doron
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of a gift freely given and received with the gratitude it deserves.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'gift,' used for both males and females in Israel. Common across Israeli society; reflects the Hebrew naming tradition of names expressing divine or human generosity.

hebrew.name: Michal
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the profound question of who can be compared to what is truly divine and great.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'who is like God.' Very common in Israel; a distinctively Israeli female name that reverses the gender association of Michael found elsewhere.

hebrew.name: Ran
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of joyful song ringing out and filling a space with its clear, happy sound.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'he sang' or 'joyful song.' Common in Israel; a characteristically short and modern Israeli male name reflecting the preference for concise Hebrew names.

hebrew.name: Liron
hebrew.meaning: Carries the personal joy and the song that belongs entirely to oneself.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'my song' or 'my joy,' used for both males and females in Israel. Popular in modern Israeli naming; reflects the Hebrew musical and poetic naming tradition.

hebrew.name: Gal
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the wave breaking on the shore, rolling in with energy and retreating in foam.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'wave,' used for both males and females in Israel. Common across Israeli society; reflects the modern Israeli tradition of nature names connected to the Mediterranean.

hebrew.name: Yael
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the mountain goat, sure-footed and confident on steep and difficult terrain.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'mountain goat' or 'ibex.' Very common in Israel; one of the most popular female names across all Israeli Jewish communities.

hebrew.name: Amit
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of a trusted friend and companion, one who stands close and does not leave.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'friend' or 'companion,' used for both males and females in Israel. Very common across Israeli society; one of the most widely used names in the country.

hebrew.name: Tali
hebrew.meaning: Carries the freshness of dew resting on the morning grass, delicate and cool.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'my dew' or 'dew.' Common in Israel; reflects the modern Israeli naming tradition of nature names with a distinctly poetic sensibility.

hebrew.name: Omer
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the measure of grain and the harvest abundance of a field brought fully to ripeness.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'sheaf of grain' or 'measure,' used for both males and females in Israel. Very common in modern Israel; reflects the renewed connection to agricultural symbolism in Israeli culture.

hebrew.name: Ronit
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of joyful song and the happiness of one whose voice brings music to those around them.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'my song' or 'joyful.' Common in Israel; the feminine form of Ron; reflects the Israeli tradition of creating distinctly feminine forms of Hebrew names.

hebrew.name: Nadav
hebrew.meaning: Reflects the generous impulse of one who gives freely and gladly without being asked.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'generous' or 'noble.' Common in Israel; reflects the Hebrew tradition of names celebrating generosity as a fundamental virtue.

hebrew.name: Dafna
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the laurel tree, evergreen and fragrant, associated with triumph and poetic glory.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name, the Hebrew form of Daphne, meaning 'laurel.' Common in Israel; reflects the Israeli naming tradition that embraces names with Greek origins alongside Hebrew ones.

hebrew.name: Lior
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of one's own personal light, the illumination that belongs to them alone.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'my light,' used for both males and females in Israel. Very popular in modern Israel; reflects the Israeli tradition of nature and light names.

hebrew.name: Nir
hebrew.meaning: Carries the tilled field and the freshly turned earth ready for seed and new growth.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'ploughed field' or 'to plow.' Common in Israel; reflects the Zionist agricultural ideology that influenced Israeli naming in the twentieth century.

hebrew.name: Hadar
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the splendor and glory of something magnificent that commands admiration.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'splendor' or 'glory,' used for both males and females in Israel. Common across Israeli society; reflects the Hebrew poetic tradition of names expressing magnificence.

hebrew.name: Avigail
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the father's joy and the delight a daughter brings to one who loves her.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'father's joy.' Common in Israel; the Hebrew form of Abigail; associated with the wise woman Abigail in the Hebrew Bible.

hebrew.name: Gilad
hebrew.meaning: Carries the ancient witness of stone hills that have seen everything and remembered it all.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name referring to the ancient region of Gilead, meaning 'heap of testimony' or 'rocky ground.' Common in Israel; reflects the Israeli tradition of geographic and biblical place names.

hebrew.name: Einat
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of the spring of water welling up from the earth, fresh and inexhaustible.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'spring of water' or 'spring.' Common in Israel; reflects the Israeli tradition of water and nature names connected to the landscape.

hebrew.name: Eran
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the awakened and alert quality of one whose senses are sharp and fully engaged.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'awake' or 'alert.' Common in Israel; a distinctly Israeli name reflecting the modern tradition of short, energetic Hebrew names.

hebrew.name: Sigal
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the violet flower, small and vivid, its purple petals bright against the green.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'violet flower.' Common in Israel; reflects the Israeli naming tradition of wildflower names connected to the landscape of the Land of Israel.

hebrew.name: Rotem
hebrew.meaning: Carries the hardy desert shrub that provides shade in barren places and survives where nothing else does.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'broom plant' or 'white weeping broom,' a desert plant of Israel, used for both males and females. Popular in Israel; reflects the connection to the Israeli landscape.

hebrew.name: Hila
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the halo of light surrounding a source of radiance, soft and encircling.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'halo' or 'praise.' Very common in Israel; one of the most popular female names in Israeli society; reflects the Hebrew tradition of light-imagery names.

hebrew.name: Yuval
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of the river flowing onward, carrying everything in its gentle current.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'stream' or 'flowing brook,' used for both males and females in Israel. Very popular in modern Israel; associated with music through the biblical figure credited with inventing instruments.

hebrew.name: Avital
hebrew.meaning: Carries the father's dew, a freshness and blessing that descends from above.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'father's dew.' Common in Israel; reflects the Israeli tradition of compound names drawing on natural and familial imagery.

hebrew.name: Kobi
hebrew.meaning: Reflects the one who follows closely and takes hold of what another has set before them.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name, an informal Israeli form of Yaakov (Jacob). Very common in Israel; reflects the Israeli cultural tradition of creating affectionate shortened forms of biblical names.

hebrew.name: Orit
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the light that shines outward and makes everything around it visible.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'light.' Common in Israel; the feminine form of Or; reflects the Israeli naming tradition of light names connected to the Hebrew concept of divine illumination.

hebrew.name: Amir
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the commanding presence of one who speaks with authority and is listened to.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'he who speaks' or 'treetop.' Very common in Israel among both Jewish and Arab communities; one of the most widely used male names in the country.

hebrew.name: Nufar
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of the yellow water lily floating serenely on a still and quiet pond.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'water lily' or 'yellow lotus.' Used in Israel; reflects the Israeli naming tradition of botanical names drawing on the native flora of the region.

hebrew.name: Shai
hebrew.meaning: Carries the quality of a gift given freely, something offered without expectation of return.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'gift,' used for both males and females in Israel. Common across Israeli society; a short and elegant name reflecting the Israeli preference for concise, meaningful names.

hebrew.name: Roni
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the personal joy and the song that rises from within and cannot be suppressed.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'my joy' or 'my song,' used for both males and females in Israel. Common in modern Israel; reflects the Israeli musical naming tradition.

hebrew.name: Ido
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the appointed time, the moment that has been set and arrives exactly when it should.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'appointed time' or 'to praise.' Common in Israel; a distinctly Israeli name reflecting the modern preference for short, resonant Hebrew names.

hebrew.name: Shaked
hebrew.meaning: Carries the image of the almond tree, the first to blossom, heralding the end of winter.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'almond tree,' used for both males and females in Israel. Popular in modern Israel; the almond tree is a symbol of awakening and the beginning of spring in Israeli culture.

hebrew.name: Yonit
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the dove, that symbol of peace and gentleness, moving softly through clear air.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'dove' or 'little dove.' Common in Israel; the feminine form of Yona; reflects the Hebrew tradition of bird names associated with peace and gentle spirit.

hebrew.name: Lavi
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of the lion, powerful and proud, carrying itself with sovereign dignity.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'lion.' Common in Israel; reflects the Hebrew biblical tradition in which the lion is a symbol of the tribe of Judah and of strength.

hebrew.name: Maayan
hebrew.meaning: Carries the spring of fresh water welling up from the earth, giving life to all around it.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew name meaning 'spring of water' or 'fountain,' used for both males and females in Israel. Very popular in modern Israel; reflects the Israeli connection to water sources in an arid landscape.

hebrew.name: Nimrod
hebrew.meaning: Speaks of the great hunter who pursued his quarry across vast territories with relentless skill.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'hunter' or 'rebel.' Used in Israel; associated with the mighty hunter in the Hebrew Bible; reflects the modern Israeli tradition of reclaiming strong biblical male names.

hebrew.name: Lihi
hebrew.meaning: Reflects the personal quality of having something that is entirely and uniquely one's own.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew female name meaning 'I have' or 'my own.' Common in modern Israel; a distinctly Israeli female name reflecting the contemporary tendency toward intimate, first-person Hebrew names.

hebrew.name: Avi
hebrew.meaning: Evokes the father and all the strength and tenderness that paternal love contains.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'my father' or used as a standalone name and prefix in compound names. Very common in Israel; one of the most recognizable Israeli given names across all communities.

hebrew.name: Tomer
hebrew.meaning: Paints the image of the date palm, tall and straight and bearing its sweet fruit with patience.
hebrew.notes: A Hebrew male name meaning 'date palm.' Common in Israel; reflects the Israeli tradition of tree names connected to the landscape of the Land of Israel.

turkish.name: Zeynep
turkish.meaning: Carries the beauty and fragrance of a flowering plant adorned with its blossoms.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name derived from the Arabic Zaynab. Very common in Turkey; one of the most popular female names in the country across all regions and generations.

turkish.name: Emre
turkish.meaning: Evokes the bond of brotherhood and the deep fraternal connection between those who share a path.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'brother' or 'friend.' Very common in Turkey; associated with the medieval Anatolian poet Yunus Emre; one of the most recognizable Turkish male names.

turkish.name: Ayşe
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the vibrant aliveness of one whose presence fills every room with energy.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name derived from the Arabic Aisha. Very common in Turkey; historically the most popular female name in the country; reflects the deep Islamic influence on Turkish naming.

turkish.name: Burak
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the radiant lightning bolt, swift and brilliant in its flash across the sky.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name derived from the Arabic Buraq, the name of the divine creature in Islamic tradition. Common in Turkey; one of the most used male names in modern Turkish society.

turkish.name: Defne
turkish.meaning: Evokes the laurel tree, evergreen and fragrant, crowned with the garland of triumph.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'laurel' or 'bay tree.' Very popular in modern Turkey; reflects the Turkish naming tradition of nature names; associated with the Greek mythological Daphne.

turkish.name: Mert
turkish.meaning: Carries the steadfast manliness and reliable courage of one who holds their ground.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'manly,' 'brave,' or 'honest.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish cultural tradition of names expressing masculine virtue and integrity.

turkish.name: Selin
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the stream flowing gently, its water moving with calm persistence over stones.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'flowing water' or 'stream.' Very common in Turkey; one of the most popular female names in modern Turkish society.

turkish.name: Berk
turkish.meaning: Evokes the solid firmness of something hard and enduring that cannot easily be broken.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'solid,' 'firm,' or 'strong.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names expressing strength and reliable character.

turkish.name: Ceren
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the young gazelle, swift and graceful, moving with effortless lightness.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'young gazelle.' Very common in Turkey; one of the most popular female names in the country; reflects the Turkish poetic tradition of gazelle imagery.

turkish.name: Onur
turkish.meaning: Carries the honor and dignity of one who conducts themselves with integrity and pride.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'honor' or 'dignity.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish cultural value of personal and family honor as central to identity.

turkish.name: Gizem
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the mystery and the hidden quality of one who keeps something of themselves veiled.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'mystery' or 'enigma.' Very common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish naming tradition of abstract quality names expressing intriguing feminine character.

turkish.name: Kaan
turkish.meaning: Evokes the sovereign authority of the ruler, the one whose command is absolute.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name derived from Khan, meaning 'ruler' or 'sovereign.' Very common in Turkey; reflects the Turkic nomadic heritage and the tradition of names associated with leadership.

turkish.name: Pınar
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the spring welling up from the earth, clear and fresh and inexhaustible.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'spring of water' or 'fountain.' Very common in Turkey; one of the most popular female names in Turkish society; reflects the tradition of water and nature names.

turkish.name: Çağan
turkish.meaning: Carries the lively energy of a festive time full of sound, color, and celebration.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'festive' or 'lively.' Used in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names expressing vibrant and celebratory qualities.

turkish.name: Naz
turkish.meaning: Evokes the coquettish grace of one who is spoiled with affection and moves with gentle pride.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'coquetry,' 'grace,' or 'being pampered.' Common in Turkey; often used as a standalone name or as the first element of compound female names.

turkish.name: Tolga
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the helmet and the warrior's protection, the shield raised against harm.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'warrior's helmet.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkic martial heritage and the tradition of names associated with battle readiness and protection.

turkish.name: Rüya
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the dream, that soft world entered in sleep full of visions and meaning.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'dream.' Common in Turkey; one of the more poetic modern Turkish female names; reflects the Turkish tradition of names evoking the inner imaginative life.

turkish.name: Arda
turkish.meaning: Evokes flowing water carving through ancient, layered terrain.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name associated with the Arda River in Thrace. Common in Turkey and the Balkans; reflects the Turkish historical connection to Thrace and the broader Anatolian landscape.

turkish.name: Yıldız
turkish.meaning: Evokes the star shining steadily overhead, fixed in its place and guiding those below.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'star.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of celestial names and the cultural significance of stars in Turkic nomadic heritage.

turkish.name: Serkan
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the chief and leader whose head guides all those who follow beneath it.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'chief' or 'leader.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkic tradition of leadership names expressing authority and command.

turkish.name: Bahar
turkish.meaning: Evokes the spring season bursting with blossoms and the fresh energy of renewed life.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'spring.' Very common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish and broader Persianate tradition of spring names celebrating renewal and natural beauty.

turkish.name: Uğur
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of good fortune and the lucky energy that accompanies one through life.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'good luck' or 'auspicious.' Very common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names expressing positive fortune and the blessing of a favored destiny.

turkish.name: Senem
turkish.meaning: Carries the image of the beloved idol, one who is so beautiful they become an object of devotion.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name derived from the Persian Sanam, meaning 'idol' or 'beloved.' Used in Turkey; reflects the Persianate influence on Turkish poetic and naming tradition.

turkish.name: Hakan
turkish.meaning: Evokes the emperor and the supreme ruler whose authority encompasses all those beneath.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name derived from the Turkic Khagan, meaning 'emperor' or 'supreme ruler.' Very common in Turkey; reflects the deep Turkic nomadic heritage in which the khagan held ultimate authority.

turkish.name: Deniz
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the sea, vast and deep, stretching to a horizon that the eye cannot reach.
turkish.notes: A Turkish name meaning 'sea,' used for both males and females in Turkey. Very common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish cultural connection to the sea surrounding the Anatolian peninsula.

turkish.name: Tayfun
turkish.meaning: Carries the powerful fury of the typhoon, a force of nature that sweeps everything before it.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name derived from the word for typhoon or cyclone. Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names evoking powerful natural forces.

turkish.name: Ece
turkish.meaning: Evokes the queen of the sea, the sovereign lady whose domain is the deep water.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'queen' or 'queen of the sea.' Very popular in modern Turkey; one of the most fashionable female names in contemporary Turkish society.

turkish.name: Murat
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the desired wish finally attained, the longing of the heart fulfilled.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name derived from the Arabic Murad, meaning 'wish' or 'desired.' Very common in Turkey; one of the most widely used Turkish male names across all regions.

turkish.name: İrem
turkish.meaning: Carries the legendary garden of paradise, a place of impossible beauty and eternal fragrance.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name evoking Iram, the mythical garden mentioned in the Quran. Very common in Turkey; one of the most popular female names in modern Turkish society.

turkish.name: Çetin
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the hard and difficult quality of something that resists all pressure and endures.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'hard,' 'difficult,' or 'strong.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names expressing resilience and the ability to withstand hardship.

turkish.name: Gül
turkish.meaning: Evokes the rose in full bloom, its petals open and its fragrance drifting across the garden.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'rose.' Very common in Turkey; the rose holds deep cultural significance in Turkish and broader Persianate tradition; often the first element of compound female names.

turkish.name: Yusuf
turkish.meaning: Carries the beauty and grace of one specially added to increase joy and bring it to fullness.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name derived from the Arabic Yusuf (Joseph). Very common in Turkey; reflects the Quranic story of Yusuf, beloved in Turkish Islamic tradition for its themes of patience and beauty.

turkish.name: Aylin
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the moon and its halo, that soft ring of light encircling the night.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'moon halo' or 'belonging to the moon.' Very popular in Turkey; one of the most fashionable female names in modern Turkish society; reflects lunar naming traditions.

turkish.name: Kerem
turkish.meaning: Evokes the generous vineyard, abundant with fruit and giving freely to all who come.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'generosity' or 'vineyard.' Common in Turkey; associated with the legendary Azerbaijani ashug (troubadour) Kerem; reflects the Persianate tradition of generous naming.

turkish.name: Simge
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the symbol or sign, the mark that contains a meaning beyond itself.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'symbol' or 'emblem.' Common in modern Turkey; reflects the Turkish naming tradition of abstract concept names expressing depth of character.

turkish.name: Alp
turkish.meaning: Carries the heroic bravery of the warrior who faces danger without flinching.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'hero' or 'brave man.' Common in Turkey; reflects the deep Turkic martial heritage in which the alp, the hero-warrior, was the ideal of masculinity.

turkish.name: Seray
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of the palace by the water's edge, combining beauty, wealth, and the sea.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name meaning 'palace' or 'palace by the sea.' Popular in modern Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names evoking opulent imagery of water and royal architecture.

turkish.name: Bora
turkish.meaning: Evokes the powerful north wind that sweeps down suddenly and transforms everything in its path.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'blizzard' or 'violent north wind.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkic tradition of weather and natural force names expressing wild, uncontrolled energy.

turkish.name: Melis
turkish.meaning: Carries the sweetness of honey, that golden gift produced by patient, tireless labor.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name related to the word for honey bee or honey. Very popular in modern Turkey; one of the most fashionable contemporary female names in Turkish society.

turkish.name: Doruk
turkish.meaning: Speaks of the summit, the very peak of the mountain where nothing stands higher.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'summit' or 'peak.' Common in Turkey; reflects the Turkish tradition of names expressing aspiration and the reaching of the highest possible point.

turkish.name: Yaren
turkish.meaning: Reflects the quality of the companion and close friend who walks alongside through every journey.
turkish.notes: A Turkish name meaning 'companion' or 'friend,' used for both males and females in Turkey. Popular in modern Turkey; reflects the Anatolian tradition of friendship and companionship as cherished virtues.

turkish.name: Sinan
turkish.meaning: Evokes the spearhead, sharp and purposeful, aimed with precision at its target.
turkish.notes: A Turkish male name meaning 'spearhead.' Common in Turkey; associated with the legendary Ottoman architect Mimar Sinan; the name itself carries associations with precision, craft, and directness.

turkish.name: Nazlı
turkish.meaning: Paints the image of one who is gracefully coy, pampered by those who adore them.
turkish.notes: A Turkish female name derived from Naz, meaning 'coquettish' or 'gracefully spoiled.' Very common in Turkey; one of the most recognizable traditional female names in Turkish culture.

kurdish.name: Berivan
kurdish.meaning: Carries the image of the milkmaid tending her flock in the mountains at break of day.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'milkmaid' or 'one who milks.' Very common among Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria; one of the most distinctively Kurdish female names.

kurdish.name: Soran
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the musical cadence of a lover's call, the melodic voice reaching out across the valley.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name associated with a traditional Kurdish musical mode and the Soran dialect spoken in Iraqi Kurdistan. Common in Iraqi Kurdistan; reflects the deep connection of Kurdish identity to music.

kurdish.name: Rojda
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of the break of dawn, the first light spreading across the horizon and renewing the day.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'dawn' or 'sunrise.' Very common among Kurdish communities in Turkey, Syria, and Iraq; reflects the Kurdish poetic tradition of sun and dawn imagery.

kurdish.name: Serdar
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the military chief whose leadership guides the force in the field.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'commander' or 'military leader.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, and Iran; reflects the Kurdish martial heritage and tradition of leadership names.

kurdish.name: Helin
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the gazelle, swift and graceful, moving through the mountain landscape with ease.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'gazelle.' Very common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; one of the most popular Kurdish female names; reflects the Kurdish poetic tradition of animal imagery.

kurdish.name: Welat
kurdish.meaning: Carries the homeland and the beloved land where one's roots are sunk deep and firm.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'homeland' or 'country.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the Kurdish cultural emphasis on love of homeland as a central identity value.

kurdish.name: Delal
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of the beloved and the precious one cherished beyond all others.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'beloved' or 'precious one.' Very common across Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria; reflects the Kurdish poetic tradition of love-naming.

kurdish.name: Zagros
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the great mountain range, ancient and massive, defining the landscape with its ridges.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name associated with the Zagros Mountains of western Iran and northern Iraq. Used among Kurdish communities; reflects the deep Kurdish cultural and historical connection to the mountain landscape.

kurdish.name: Sipan
kurdish.meaning: Carries the commanding height and solid permanence of a great mountain peak.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name associated with Mount Süphan in eastern Turkey. Common among Kurdish communities; reflects the Kurdish tradition of mountain names connecting identity to the landscape.

kurdish.name: Nazdar
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the graceful and coy beloved, a vision of tender refinement.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'graceful' or 'beloved.' Common among Kurdish communities in Iraq and Iran; reflects the Persianate influence on Kurdish naming traditions.

kurdish.name: Baran
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the rain falling steadily, the water that the land needs and welcomes with relief.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish name meaning 'rain,' used for both males and females in Kurdish communities across Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria. Reflects the Kurdish agricultural connection to rainfall as a life-giving blessing.

kurdish.name: Arjin
kurdish.meaning: Carries the quality of freedom and the open, unbound spirit that belongs to no one but itself.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'free woman' or related to freedom. Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the contemporary Kurdish naming tradition celebrating female freedom.

kurdish.name: Hogir
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of the mountain and its towering presence, standing above everything with patient dignity.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'mountain' or 'strong as a mountain.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey; reflects the Kurdish cultural identification with the mountains as homeland.

kurdish.name: Berfin
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the first snowflake falling softly and silently, covering the ground in white silence.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'snowflake.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the Kurdish poetic tradition of winter imagery and the mountain landscape.

kurdish.name: Diyar
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the homeland and the region where one was born and belongs.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'homeland' or 'region.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Iraq; reflects the central importance of territorial identity in Kurdish culture.

kurdish.name: Zeynep
kurdish.meaning: Carries the beauty and fragrance of a blossoming plant filled with flowers.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name derived from the Arabic Zaynab. Used across Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, and Syria; reflects the Islamic influence on Kurdish naming alongside indigenous Kurdish names.

kurdish.name: Zinar
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the gold, precious and gleaming, a thing of enduring value and beauty.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'gold.' Common among Kurdish communities in Iraq and Iran; reflects the Kurdish tradition of precious metal names expressing worth and brilliance.

kurdish.name: Viyan
kurdish.meaning: Carries the longing and desire of one who reaches toward what they love with full heart.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'desire' or 'longing.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the Kurdish poetic tradition of names expressing deep emotional yearning.

kurdish.name: Hewas
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of hope and the forward-looking expectation of something better yet to come.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'hope.' Used among Kurdish communities in Iraq and Syria; reflects the Kurdish tradition of virtue names expressing resilience and optimism.

kurdish.name: Gulistan
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the rose garden in full bloom, a paradise of fragrance and color.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'rose garden.' Common among Kurdish communities in Iran and Iraq; reflects the Persianate influence on Kurdish culture; also the title of a classical Persian literary work.

kurdish.name: Azad
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the free and unbound spirit, one who belongs to no master and moves without chains.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'free.' Very common across Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria; reflects the central Kurdish cultural value of freedom and independence.

kurdish.name: Roza
kurdish.meaning: Carries the rose and its layers of petals, soft and fragrant in the morning light.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'rose.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Iraq; reflects the Kurdish poetic tradition of floral names drawing on Persianate imagery.

kurdish.name: Kemal
kurdish.meaning: Reflects the perfection and complete excellence of one who has reached their fullest development.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name derived from the Arabic Kamal, meaning 'perfection.' Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Iraq; reflects the Islamic influence on Kurdish naming traditions.

kurdish.name: Jiyan
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of life itself, the vital breath and living energy that animates all existence.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'life.' Very common across Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria; one of the most beloved Kurdish female names.

kurdish.name: Serhad
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the frontier, the border territory where one world ends and another begins.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'frontier' or 'border region.' Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey; reflects the Kurdish experience of living across international borders and the naming of that liminal identity.

kurdish.name: Ruken
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the foundation pillar, firm and upright, holding everything above it in place.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'pillar' or 'strong foundation.' Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the Kurdish tradition of names expressing structural strength and reliability.

kurdish.name: Barzan
kurdish.meaning: Carries the highland identity and the elevated territory where ancient roots grow deep.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name associated with the Barzan region of Iraqi Kurdistan. Common in Iraqi Kurdistan; reflects the Kurdish tradition of place names that carry clan and territorial identity.

kurdish.name: Nergiz
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the narcissus flower, pale and fragrant, the first to appear after the long winter.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'narcissus flower.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Iraq; reflects the Persianate influence on Kurdish naming and the tradition of spring flower names.

kurdish.name: Siyamend
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of the black-browed one whose dark eyes and brows mark a face of striking beauty.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name associated with the legendary Kurdish romantic hero Siyamend o Xece. Very common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; one of the most cherished names in Kurdish oral tradition.

kurdish.name: Xezal
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the gazelle, graceful and alert, moving through open land with swift beauty.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'gazelle.' Very common across Kurdish communities; the Kurmanji spelling with X reflects the unique orthography of the northern Kurdish dialect spoken in Turkey and Syria.

kurdish.name: Rizgar
kurdish.meaning: Carries the freedom and liberation of one who has been released from all that confined them.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'freedom' or 'liberation.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, and Syria; reflects the central Kurdish value of freedom in naming culture.

kurdish.name: Perwer
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the one who is nurtured and raised with care, shaped and educated by loving hands.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'educated' or 'nurtured.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey; associated with the singer Şivan Perwer, whose music is central to Kurdish cultural identity.

kurdish.name: Havin
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of the summer season, warm and full, when the days are long and the earth is generous.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'summer.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the Kurdish tradition of seasonal names connecting human identity to the natural cycle.

kurdish.name: Şoreş
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of the revolution, the great turning that changes everything in its wake.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'revolution.' Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the Kurdish political consciousness and the tradition of ideologically expressive naming.

kurdish.name: Çiya
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the mountain standing immovable and ancient, defining the horizon for all who live below.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish name meaning 'mountain,' used for both males and females in Kurdish communities in Turkey and Iraq. Reflects the Kurdish cultural identification with the mountains as the spiritual homeland.

kurdish.name: Bêrîtan
kurdish.meaning: Carries the image of a milkmaid on the high pasture at dawn, her work beginning before the world wakes.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'milkmaid.' Common among Kurdish communities in Turkey; reflects the agricultural and pastoral heritage of Kurdish mountain communities.

kurdish.name: Dilşad
kurdish.meaning: Carries the happy heart and the contentment of one whose inner world is at peace.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'happy heart' or 'joyful.' Common across Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, and Iran; reflects the Persianate influence on Kurdish naming in compound heart-names.

kurdish.name: Serhat
kurdish.meaning: Speaks of the borderland and the frontier territory that stretches between two different worlds.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'frontier' or 'border.' Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey; a variant of Serhad; reflects the Kurdish experience of territorial identity across multiple states.

kurdish.name: Narin
kurdish.meaning: Reflects the delicate fineness of something slender and tender, graceful in its fragility.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'delicate' or 'slender.' Very common across Kurdish communities in Turkey, Iraq, and Iran; one of the most popular Kurdish female names in modern usage.

kurdish.name: Kocher
kurdish.meaning: Evokes the nomad who moves with the seasons, following the green pastures across the landscape.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'nomad' or 'one who moves.' Used among Kurdish communities in Iraq and Iran; reflects the pastoral nomadic heritage of Kurdish mountain communities.

kurdish.name: Bêrxwedan
kurdish.meaning: Paints the image of resistance and the stubborn refusal to yield to what would crush the spirit.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish female name meaning 'resistance.' Used among Kurdish communities in Turkey and Syria; reflects the contemporary Kurdish naming tradition of giving children politically expressive names.

kurdish.name: Karwan
kurdish.meaning: Carries the image of the caravan moving slowly across the landscape, connected and purposeful.
kurdish.notes: A Kurdish male name meaning 'caravan.' Common among Kurdish communities in Iraq and Iran; reflects the Kurdish historical role in the trade routes of the ancient Silk Road.

armenian.name: Ani
armenian.meaning: Evokes the ancient city of grace and beauty, a place of magnificent civilization carved in stone.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name associated with the medieval Armenian capital Ani. Very common in Armenia and among the Armenian diaspora worldwide; one of the most recognizable Armenian female names.

armenian.name: Tigran
armenian.meaning: Carries the power and authority of one who commands like a great ruler over their domain.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name derived from the ancient Armenian king Tigranes. Very common in Armenia; associated with Tigranes the Great, the most powerful ruler in Armenian history.

armenian.name: Anahit
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the sacred goddess of fertility and water, the divine feminine of the ancient world.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name derived from the pre-Christian Armenian goddess Anahit. Very common in Armenia; reflects the revival of pre-Christian Armenian names following independence.

armenian.name: Armen
armenian.meaning: Paints the image of the homeland itself, the nation and its ancient identity embodied in a person.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name meaning 'Armenian' or 'son of Armenia.' Very common in Armenia and the diaspora; one of the most distinctively Armenian male names, directly evoking national identity.

armenian.name: Lusine
armenian.meaning: Evokes the moon and its soft light spreading gently over the sleeping world at night.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'moon.' Very common in Armenia; reflects the Armenian poetic tradition of celestial names and the cultural significance of the moon in Armenian folklore.

armenian.name: Vardan
armenian.meaning: Carries the heroic rose and the warrior who fought and died for faith and homeland.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name meaning 'of roses' or related to the Armenian word for rose. Very common in Armenia; associated with the hero Vardan Mamikonian who led the Battle of Avarayr in 451 CE.

armenian.name: Nune
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the holy woman who brought light into a kingdom still sitting in darkness.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name associated with Saint Nino who converted Georgia to Christianity. Common in Armenia; reflects the deep importance of early Christian history in Armenian identity.

armenian.name: Hakob
armenian.meaning: Reflects the one who follows closely and takes hold of what another has set before them.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name, the Armenian form of Jacob. Very common in Armenia; the patron saint of Armenia is also named Hakob in Armenian tradition.

armenian.name: Astghik
armenian.meaning: Evokes the little star, small and bright, twinkling with its own quiet radiance.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'little star' and associated with the Armenian goddess of love and beauty Astghik. Used in Armenia; reflects the revival of pre-Christian Armenian goddess names.

armenian.name: Gegham
armenian.meaning: Paints the image of beautiful water and the lakeside beauty of an ancient and storied place.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name associated with Lake Gegharkunik (Lake Sevan) and the surrounding region. Used in Armenia; reflects the Armenian tradition of place names connected to the homeland landscape.

armenian.name: Mariam
armenian.meaning: Carries the graceful purity and spiritual depth of a revered and holy woman.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name, the Armenian form of Mary. Very common in Armenia among Armenian Apostolic Christian families; the Virgin Mary holds profound reverence in Armenian Christianity.

armenian.name: Davit
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the beloved one chosen by the divine for an extraordinary and lasting purpose.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name, the Armenian form of David. Very common in Armenia; associated with Saint David and the beloved biblical king; one of the most used male names in the country.

armenian.name: Gohar
armenian.meaning: Evokes the precious gem, brilliant and valuable, formed under great pressure into something rare.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'gem' or 'jewel.' Very common in Armenia and the Armenian diaspora; one of the most beloved and recognizable Armenian female names.

armenian.name: Khachatur
armenian.meaning: Carries the sacred cross, the symbol of faith that marks one as belonging to the ancient church.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name meaning 'cross-giver' or 'one who gives the cross.' Common in Armenia; deeply embedded in Armenian Apostolic Christian identity; reflects the centrality of the cross in Armenian faith.

armenian.name: Siranush
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the charming and graceful beloved, one whose presence is sweet and irresistible.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'charming' or 'lovely beloved.' Common in Armenia; reflects the Armenian poetic tradition of love-names; associated with a famous Armenian patriotic song.

armenian.name: Arshak
armenian.meaning: Paints the image of the royal bear, strong and commanding, the king of the wild places.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name, the Armenian form of Arsaces, meaning 'bear' or 'royal.' Used in Armenia; associated with the Arsacid dynasty that ruled Greater Armenia for centuries.

armenian.name: Sevan
armenian.meaning: Evokes a vast expanse of deep blue water, serene and jewel-like.
armenian.notes: An Armenian name associated with Lake Sevan, the largest lake in Armenia. Used for both males and females; reflects the deep Armenian cultural attachment to the landscape of the homeland.

armenian.name: Ararat
armenian.meaning: Carries the sacred mountain, the symbol of the nation, visible on the horizon and forever present.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name associated with Mount Ararat, the national symbol of Armenia. Used in Armenia as a given name expressing profound national pride; the mountain appears on the Armenian coat of arms.

armenian.name: Tamar
armenian.meaning: Reflects the date palm standing tall and straight, bearing its fruit with quiet dignity.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name shared with the Georgian and Hebrew traditions. Used in Armenia; reflects the overlapping Christian naming traditions of the South Caucasus region.

armenian.name: Mher
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the sun and the ancient deity of light who drives away darkness with his blazing chariot.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name derived from Mithra, the ancient sun deity. Used in Armenia; reflects the pre-Christian Armenian religious heritage that persists in names; associated with the hero Mher in the Armenian epic Daredevils of Sassoun.

armenian.name: Knarik
armenian.meaning: Evokes the harp, that ancient stringed instrument whose music rises sweetly and fills the air.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'harp.' Common in Armenia; reflects the deep importance of music in Armenian cultural identity; the duduk and traditional instruments are central to Armenian heritage.

armenian.name: Vazgen
armenian.meaning: Carries the precious and noble quality of something greatly valued and held in high esteem.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name meaning 'precious' or 'dear.' Common in Armenia; associated with Catholicos Vazgen I, the supreme patriarch of the Armenian Apostolic Church for decades.

armenian.name: Hayarpi
armenian.meaning: Paints the image of a gentle dove in flight, carrying peace and grace.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'Armenian dove.' Used in Armenia; reflects the Armenian naming tradition of compound names combining national identity with natural imagery.

armenian.name: Spartak
armenian.meaning: Evokes the legendary slave who led a great uprising against those who held power.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name, the Armenian form of Spartacus. Used in Armenia; reflects the Soviet-era influence on Armenian naming when classical and revolutionary names became popular.

armenian.name: Ruzanna
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the rose, fragrant and beautiful, standing in full bloom.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'rose' or related to rose imagery. Used in Armenia and the diaspora; reflects the Armenian poetic and cultural tradition of rose imagery throughout art and literature.

armenian.name: Ashot
armenian.meaning: Reflects the brilliant blaze of fire, vivid and consuming in its intensity.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name meaning 'fire' or 'blazing fire.' Very common in Armenia; associated with several Armenian kings named Ashot; one of the oldest and most historically rooted Armenian male names.

armenian.name: Liana
armenian.meaning: Carries the climbing vine that winds gracefully upward, seeking the light with patient beauty.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name used in Armenia and the diaspora. Reflects the European influence on modern Armenian naming alongside traditional Armenian names.

armenian.name: Gevorg
armenian.meaning: Evokes the farmer who tends the earth and draws from it the sustenance of life.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name, the Armenian form of George. Very common in Armenia; Saint George is venerated in the Armenian Apostolic Church; the name is deeply embedded in Armenian Christian identity.

armenian.name: Zabel
armenian.meaning: Paints the image of one dedicated to the divine, set apart for a sacred and elevated purpose.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name, the Armenian form of Isabel/Elizabeth. Used in Armenia; associated with Queen Zabel of Armenian Cilicia, who ruled in the 13th century as one of the few female monarchs of Armenia.

armenian.name: Narek
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the place of fire, the sacred hill where flames burn and prayers rise skyward.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name associated with Narekavank monastery and the great poet-monk Grigor Narekatsi. Very popular in Armenia; one of the most beloved names in modern Armenian society.

armenian.name: Arevik
armenian.meaning: Carries the small sun, a little radiant disc spreading its warmth over everything near it.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'little sun.' Common in Armenia; reflects the Armenian tradition of diminutive sun-names and the cultural symbolism of the sun in Armenian art and khachkar designs.

armenian.name: Hayk
armenian.meaning: Evokes a legendary archer-warrior and the founding ancestor of a great lineage.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name associated with Hayk, the legendary forefather of the Armenian nation. Very common in Armenia; carries the deepest possible connection to Armenian national and ethnic identity.

armenian.name: Tsovinar
armenian.meaning: Reflects the stormy sea, turbulent and wild, a force of nature that cannot be tamed.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'stormy sea' or 'she of the stormy sea.' Used in Armenia; reflects the Armenian poetic tradition of nature names evoking powerful and dramatic natural forces.

armenian.name: Samvel
armenian.meaning: Paints the image of the prophet who was heard by the divine and called to serve from childhood.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name, the Armenian form of Samuel. Common in Armenia; reflects the Armenian Apostolic Christian tradition of biblical naming; associated with the prophet Samuel in Armenian church culture.

armenian.name: Nvard
