armenian.meaning: Evokes a rose in full bloom—delicate yet resilient, a flower steeped in beauty and symbolic weight.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name meaning 'rose.' Used in Armenia; one of the older traditional Armenian female names; reflects the central place of rose imagery in Armenian poetry and cultural expression.

armenian.name: Aram
armenian.meaning: Speaks of the exalted height of one elevated above the ordinary, reaching toward greatness.
armenian.notes: An Armenian male name associated with the legendary ancestor of the Armenians and meaning 'exalted.' Very common in Armenia and the diaspora; one of the most recognizable and beloved Armenian names.

armenian.name: Anjel
armenian.meaning: Carries the radiant purity of a celestial being moving between the divine and the earthly.
armenian.notes: An Armenian female name, the Armenian form of Angel. Used in Armenia; reflects the Armenian Apostolic Christian tradition and the modern influence of European names on Armenian naming practice.

georgian.name: Nino
georgian.meaning: Evokes the holy woman who brought light into a kingdom sitting in darkness and transformed it.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name associated with Saint Nino, who converted Georgia to Christianity in the 4th century. The most popular female name in Georgia; a name of profound national and spiritual significance.

georgian.name: Giorgi
georgian.meaning: Carries the virtue of the farmer and the patron saint, the dragon-slayer who protects the weak.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of George. The most common male name in Georgia; Saint George is the patron saint of Georgia and holds the deepest reverence in the Georgian Orthodox Church.

georgian.name: Tamara
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the date palm standing in full height, graceful and bearing its fruit with patience.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, the Georgian form of Tamar. Very common in Georgia; associated with Queen Tamar, the greatest ruler of medieval Georgia, who presided over the Golden Age of Georgian culture.

georgian.name: Sandro
georgian.meaning: Paints the image of the defender and protector of all those who come under their shield.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of Alexander. Very common in Georgia; reflects the Georgian naming tradition of adapting European and Greek names into the Georgian phonetic system.

georgian.name: Mariam
georgian.meaning: Evokes the graceful purity and spiritual depth of a revered and most holy woman.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, the Georgian form of Mary. Very common in Georgia among Georgian Orthodox Christian families; the Virgin Mary holds deep veneration in the Georgian Orthodox Church.

georgian.name: Irakli
georgian.meaning: Carries the legendary strength and heroic endurance of one who overcame every impossible task.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of Herakles (Hercules). Very common in Georgia; reflects the classical Greek influence on Georgian culture through the Byzantine tradition.

georgian.name: Elene
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the bright torch whose radiance lights the way for all who follow.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, the Georgian form of Helen. Very common in Georgia; associated with Saint Nino's sponsor Queen Nana and with Saint Helena; deeply embedded in Georgian Christian tradition.

georgian.name: Levan
georgian.meaning: Evokes the lion and the royal strength it embodies, majestic and commanding in its bearing.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name derived from the Hebrew Levi or the Persian Arslan (lion). Very common in Georgia; one of the most recognizable Georgian male names across all regions.

georgian.name: Tamar
georgian.meaning: Paints the image of the date palm, tall and upright, bearing its sweet fruit with quiet dignity.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name shared with the Hebrew tradition, meaning 'date palm.' Very common in Georgia; closely associated with the revered Queen Tamar, whose name carries the weight of the Georgian Golden Age.

georgian.name: Mikheil
georgian.meaning: Carries the rhetorical question of who could be compared to what is truly great and divine.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of Michael. Very common in Georgia; the archangel Michael holds special veneration in the Georgian Orthodox Church.

georgian.name: Nana
georgian.meaning: Evokes the tenderness of a mother's love, all warmth and nurturing care distilled into a name.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'mother' or carrying the tender sense of maternal affection. Very common in Georgia; also the name of the fourth-century queen who adopted Christianity before Saint Nino's arrival.

georgian.name: Nika
georgian.meaning: Speaks of victory and the triumph of one who prevails over all opposition.
georgian.notes: A Georgian name meaning 'victory,' used for both males and females in Georgia. Very common in Georgia; derived from the Greek Nike; reflects the Greek and Byzantine cultural influence on Georgian naming.

georgian.name: Ketevan
georgian.meaning: Carries the gem-like quality of something precious, brilliant, and rare beyond ordinary measure.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'precious gem' or 'jewel.' Very common in Georgia; associated with Queen Ketevan the Martyr, who was tortured and killed for refusing to convert from Christianity.

georgian.name: Gogi
georgian.meaning: Paints the image of the farmer and protector, the one who tends and defends with equal devotion.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, an informal form of Giorgi. Very common in Georgia as a given name and nickname; reflects the Georgian tradition of affectionate shortened forms of the most common names.

georgian.name: Lika
georgian.meaning: Evokes a small and radiant light, bright and warm in its intimate glow.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name used as both a diminutive and a standalone given name. Common in Georgia; reflects the Georgian naming tradition of creating elegant shortened forms as independent names.

georgian.name: Vakhtang
georgian.meaning: Carries the wolf-bodied strength and the hawk's keenness of sight combined in a single force.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name meaning 'wolf-bodied' or 'with the body of a wolf.' Very common in Georgia; associated with the fifth-century king Vakhtang Gorgasali, the founder of Tbilisi and a national hero.

georgian.name: Ana
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the grace and favor freely given to one who is blessed and held in high regard.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, the Georgian form of Anna. Very common in Georgia; one of the most widely used female names in the country; reflects the Georgian Orthodox Christian tradition.

georgian.name: Archil
georgian.meaning: Reflects the noble and princely bearing of one born to walk with the dignity of royalty.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name of ancient Georgian origin meaning 'noble' or 'princely.' Used in Georgia; associated with several medieval Georgian kings; one of the oldest distinctly Georgian male names.

georgian.name: Nini
georgian.meaning: Evokes the tenderness of a sacred name repeated twice, a name of devoted and gentle piety.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, a diminutive and variant of Nino. Common in Georgia; carries the same spiritual resonance as Nino but with an added quality of intimate affection.

georgian.name: Tornike
georgian.meaning: Carries the thunderbolt and the rolling power of the storm crashing through the sky.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name meaning 'thunderbolt' or 'thunder.' Common in Georgia; one of the distinctly Georgian names with no direct parallel in other cultures; reflects the Georgian naming tradition of dramatic natural imagery.

georgian.name: Salome
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the wholeness and peace that rest over a life lived in full and satisfied contentment.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, the Georgian form of Salome. Very common in Georgia; associated with Saint Salome, one of the myrrhbearers in the Georgian Orthodox tradition.

georgian.name: Zaza
georgian.meaning: Paints the image of something rare and precious, set apart for its extraordinary quality.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, a distinctive diminutive form used widely in Georgia. Very common; often used alongside the formal name Zakaria or independently as a full given name.

georgian.name: Maka
georgian.meaning: Evokes the beloved and the one held close, a name carrying warmth and intimate affection.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name, a diminutive form of Makvala or used independently. Common in Georgia; reflects the Georgian tradition of warm and short given names.

georgian.name: Konstantine
georgian.meaning: Carries the steadfast and constant quality of one who remains firm and unchanging.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of Constantine. Common in Georgia; associated with Emperor Constantine who legalized Christianity and with the Georgian tradition of Byzantine naming.

georgian.name: Khatia
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the cross and the sacred symbol of faith that defines one's identity and devotion.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'cross' or related to the Georgian word for the cross. Common in Georgia; reflects the centrality of the cross in Georgian Orthodox Christian identity.

georgian.name: Davit
georgian.meaning: Evokes the beloved one chosen by the divine, set apart for extraordinary and lasting purpose.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of David. Very common in Georgia; associated with King David the Builder, who is considered the greatest ruler in Georgian history.

georgian.name: Tinatin
georgian.meaning: Paints the image of the sun's warmth, gentle and golden, spreading its light over the world.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'sun' or 'solar warmth.' Very common in Georgia; associated with the heroine Tinatin in the Georgian national epic The Knight in the Panther's Skin by Shota Rustaveli.

georgian.name: Zurab
georgian.meaning: Carries the powerful resonance of radiant water, bright and pure in its flowing clarity.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name derived from the Persian Sohrab, meaning 'radiant water.' Very common in Georgia; reflects the Persian cultural influence on medieval Georgian court culture.

georgian.name: Lali
georgian.meaning: Evokes the ruby, deep red and glowing, precious and rare in its vivid beauty.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'ruby.' Very common in Georgia; one of the most recognizable Georgian female names; reflects the Georgian tradition of gem-names for women.

georgian.name: Beka
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the promise made and kept, the covenant between those who bind themselves together.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, a Georgian form of Beka or a diminutive of other names. Common in Georgia; used widely as both a standalone name and an affectionate form.

georgian.name: Mzia
georgian.meaning: Paints the image of the sun itself, blazing and generous, the source of all warmth and growth.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'sun.' Very common in Georgia; one of the most beloved Georgian female names; reflects the ancient Georgian sun symbolism found throughout Georgian art and architecture.

georgian.name: Ramaz
georgian.meaning: Evokes the proud and noble quality of one who carries themselves with dignified bearing.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name of Persian origin meaning 'proud' or 'noble.' Common in Georgia; reflects the Persian and Iranian cultural influence on medieval Georgian naming tradition.

georgian.name: Manana
georgian.meaning: Carries the gentle falling of the manna, sustenance given freely from above to those below.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'manna' or inspired by the biblical manna from heaven. Common in Georgia; one of the distinctly Georgian female names with deep biblical resonance in the Orthodox tradition.

georgian.name: Kakha
georgian.meaning: Evokes a sense of proud identity and distinctive cultural belonging.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name associated with the Kakheti region of eastern Georgia. Very common in Georgia; reflects the tradition of regional names expressing territorial pride; Kakheti is the heartland of Georgian wine culture.

georgian.name: Ia
georgian.meaning: Speaks of the violet flower, delicate and purple, scenting the air of early spring.
georgian.notes: A Georgian female name meaning 'violet flower.' Common in Georgia; one of the shortest and most elegant Georgian female names; reflects the tradition of flower names in Georgian culture.

georgian.name: Ioseb
georgian.meaning: Carries the beauty and grace of one who was specially added to increase the joy of those around.
georgian.notes: A Georgian male name, the Georgian form of Joseph. Common in Georgia; reflects the Georgian Orthodox Christian tradition of biblical naming.

georgian.name: Nino Matiashvili
georgian.meaning: Evokes the holy light-bringer whose family name speaks of those who belong to the lineage of Matia.
georgian.notes: A compound Georgian name using the female name Nino alongside a common Georgian surname ending in -shvili, meaning 'child of.' Illustrates the Georgian naming convention of combining a given name with a patronymic family name.

azerbaijani.name: Aytən
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the full moon and the clarity of its complete silver light flooding the night.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from Ay (moon) and tən (body), meaning 'moon-bodied.' Very common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Azerbaijani tradition of compound names drawing on lunar imagery.

azerbaijani.name: Elçin
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the messenger and envoy who travels between worlds carrying important news.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name meaning 'envoy' or 'messenger.' Very common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Turkic tradition of names expressing important social roles.

azerbaijani.name: Günel
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the sunlight itself, the radiant energy that defines the day and warms the earth.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from Güneş (sun). Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most popular female names in the country; reflects the Azerbaijani tradition of solar naming.

azerbaijani.name: Tural
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the young and vibrant man in the freshness of his living prime.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name meaning 'young man' or 'vigorous.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most widely used male names in modern Azerbaijani society.

azerbaijani.name: Sevinc
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the joy that comes suddenly and fills the heart with brightness and celebration.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name meaning 'joy' or 'happiness.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Azerbaijani tradition of emotion and virtue names.

azerbaijani.name: Nicat
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the sense of deliverance and the relief of salvation from what threatened harm.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Najat, meaning 'salvation' or 'deliverance.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition integrated into Azerbaijani culture.

azerbaijani.name: Aysel
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the moonlight flowing like a stream, silver and moving across the landscape.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from Ay (moon) and sel (flood/stream), meaning 'moonlight stream.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most popular female names in the country.

azerbaijani.name: Kamran
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the successful and fortunate one who achieves all they set out to do.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Persian Kamran, meaning 'successful' or 'prosperous.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Persianate influence on Azerbaijani naming culture.

azerbaijani.name: Könül
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the heart and all its capacity for love, longing, and deep inner feeling.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name meaning 'heart' or 'spirit.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most distinctively Azerbaijani female names; reflects the tradition of emotional and philosophical naming.

azerbaijani.name: Rauf
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the compassionate and kindly quality of one whose heart is full of gentle mercy.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Ra'uf, meaning 'compassionate' or 'kind.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Islamic tradition of virtue names expressing divine attributes.

azerbaijani.name: Nərmin
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the delicate fineness of something tender and graceful, soft in its movement.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Persian Narmin, meaning 'delicate' or 'slender.' Very common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Persianate influence on Azerbaijani female naming traditions.

azerbaijani.name: Əli
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the elevated and noble quality of one who stands high above the ordinary.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name, the local form of Ali. Very common in Azerbaijan; deeply revered in Shia Islam as the name of the first Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib; reflects the Shia dimension of Azerbaijani religious identity.

azerbaijani.name: Lalə
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the tulip, that cup-shaped flower standing upright and red in the spring field.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name meaning 'tulip.' Very common in Azerbaijan; the tulip holds deep cultural significance in Azerbaijani and broader Turkic tradition; one of the most beloved female names.

azerbaijani.name: Murad
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the deep desire and wish of a heart that reaches toward what it loves.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Murad, meaning 'wish' or 'desired.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most widely used male names in the country.

azerbaijani.name: Türkan
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the strength and enduring pride of an ancient lineage.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name meaning 'of the Turks' or 'Turkic lady.' Used in Azerbaijan and Iran; reflects the Azerbaijani pride in Turkic identity; common in northern Iran where a large Azerbaijani population lives.

azerbaijani.name: Vüsal
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the reunion and the union of those who were separated and longed to be together.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Wisal, meaning 'union' or 'meeting of lovers.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Azerbaijani poetic tradition influenced by classical Persian and Arabic love poetry.

azerbaijani.name: Gülnar
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the pomegranate blossom, red and full, jeweled with the promise of abundance.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Persian Golnar, meaning 'pomegranate blossom.' Very common in Azerbaijan and among Azerbaijanis in Iran; reflects the Persianate influence on Azerbaijani culture.

azerbaijani.name: Elnur
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the light of the people, a radiance that belongs not just to one but illuminates all.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name meaning 'people's light' or combining el (people/tribe) with nur (light). Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most recognizable modern Azerbaijani male names.

azerbaijani.name: Xuraman
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the charm and graceful movement of one who walks through the world with irresistible ease.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name meaning 'charming' or 'graceful.' Common in Azerbaijan; the Azerbaijani spelling with X reflects the unique orthographic conventions of the Azerbaijani Latin alphabet.

azerbaijani.name: Orxan
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the royal camp and the military might of a ruler whose authority extends far.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Turkic Orhan, associated with the early Ottoman ruler. Very common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Azerbaijani connection to Turkic historical heritage.

azerbaijani.name: Samirə
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the evening companion whose conversation fills the night with warmth.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Arabic Samira, meaning 'evening companion.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most widely used female names in the country.

azerbaijani.name: Farid
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the unique and unmatched quality of one who is singular and without equal.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Farid, meaning 'unique.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition integrated into Azerbaijani cultural practice.

azerbaijani.name: Nuray
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the moon's radiant light and the brightness it sheds over everything it touches.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from Nur (light) and Ay (moon), meaning 'moonlight.' Very common in Azerbaijan and across Turkic-speaking countries; one of the most popular female names.

azerbaijani.name: Samir
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the evening companion whose presence makes the night hours pass with pleasure.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Samir, meaning 'evening entertainer.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Arabic and Islamic influence on Azerbaijani naming traditions.

azerbaijani.name: Aynur
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the moonlight, that soft silver illumination falling gently over the sleeping world.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from Ay (moon) and Nur (light), meaning 'moonlight.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Azerbaijani tradition of lunar names.

azerbaijani.name: Cavid
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of eternal existence, something that endures beyond all ordinary limits of time.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Persian Javid, meaning 'eternal' or 'immortal.' Very common in Azerbaijan; associated with the national poet Hüseyn Cavid, a towering figure in Azerbaijani literature.

azerbaijani.name: Zəhra
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the brilliant radiance of one who shines with a luminous and dazzling brightness.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Arabic Zahra, meaning 'brilliant' or 'radiant.' Very common in Azerbaijan; deeply associated in Shia Islam with Fatima al-Zahra, daughter of the Prophet.

azerbaijani.name: Azər
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the crackling warmth and sacred power of flame.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name meaning 'fire.' Very common in Azerbaijan; the name is connected to the etymology of Azerbaijan itself, thought to mean 'land of fire'; reflects the ancient fire-worship traditions of the region.

azerbaijani.name: Ülviyyə
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the elevated and exalted quality of one who stands above and moves through the world with dignity.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Arabic Ulwiyya, meaning 'elevated' or 'sublime.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Arabic influence on Azerbaijani female naming traditions.

azerbaijani.name: Həsən
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of something beautiful and good, fine in every dimension of character.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name, the local form of Hasan. Very common in Azerbaijan; deeply revered in Shia Islam as the name of the second Imam Hasan ibn Ali; one of the most used male names.

azerbaijani.name: İlahə
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the divine quality of something sacred, a name touched by the light of the godly.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Arabic Ilaha, meaning 'goddess' or 'divine one.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Arabic influence on Azerbaijani naming in contexts of elevated spiritual meaning.

azerbaijani.name: Rasul
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the messenger sent with a divine mission, the bearer of truth to those who await it.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Rasul, meaning 'messenger' or 'prophet.' Common in Azerbaijan; refers to the Prophet Muhammad as Rasulullah; reflects the Islamic naming tradition.

azerbaijani.name: Fidan
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the young sapling, slender and fresh, reaching upward toward the light with new growth.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name meaning 'young tree' or 'sapling.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most popular female names in the country; reflects the Azerbaijani tradition of nature names.

azerbaijani.name: Toğrul
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the falcon, that noble hunting bird, swift and sovereign in its flight.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Turkic Tugrul, meaning 'falcon.' Used in Azerbaijan; associated with the Seljuk founder Tughril Bey; reflects the deep Turkic nomadic heritage of Azerbaijani culture.

azerbaijani.name: Mələk
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the radiant purity of an angel, a heavenly being moving between worlds.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Arabic Malak, meaning 'angel.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most popular female names in the country; reflects the Islamic tradition of angelic naming.

azerbaijani.name: Vüqar
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the dignity and self-possessed composure of one who carries themselves with quiet pride.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Waqar, meaning 'dignity' or 'composure.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Azerbaijani cultural value of dignified bearing and composed character.

azerbaijani.name: Arzu
azerbaijani.meaning: Speaks of the wish and yearning desire of one who reaches toward what they dream of.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Persian Arzu, meaning 'wish' or 'desire.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most widely used female names; reflects the Persianate influence on Azerbaijani culture.

azerbaijani.name: Kənan
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the ancient land of ancient promise, the fertile territory of beginnings.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Kan'an, referring to the biblical Canaan. Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Quranic and Islamic influence on Azerbaijani naming traditions.

azerbaijani.name: Nərgiz
azerbaijani.meaning: Reflects the narcissus flower, pale and fragrant, the first to appear after the long cold winter.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Persian Nargis, meaning 'narcissus flower.' Very common in Azerbaijan; one of the most beloved and recognizable Azerbaijani female names.

azerbaijani.name: Bəhruz
azerbaijani.meaning: Carries the fortune of a good and lucky day, the auspiciousness of a life that begins well.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Persian Bahruz, meaning 'lucky day' or 'fortunate.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Persianate influence on Azerbaijani male naming traditions.

azerbaijani.name: Əsmər
azerbaijani.meaning: Evokes the olive-skinned beauty and the warm tawny complexion of the sun-touched beloved.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani female name derived from the Arabic Asmar, meaning 'olive-skinned' or 'tanned.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the Arabic poetic tradition of praising the darker complexion as beautiful.

azerbaijani.name: Namiq
azerbaijani.meaning: Paints the image of the letter-writer whose elegant words carry messages across distances.
azerbaijani.notes: An Azerbaijani male name derived from the Arabic Namiq, meaning 'writer' or 'one who writes.' Common in Azerbaijan; reflects the cultural value placed on literacy and the art of writing in Azerbaijani tradition.

kazakh.name: Aizat
kazakh.meaning: Carries the moon's pure essence and the dignity of one touched by celestial grace.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name derived from Ay (moon) and zat (essence/being). Very common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tradition of compound lunar names celebrating feminine beauty and dignity.

kazakh.name: Nurlan
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the light of the people, a radiance that belongs to the whole community.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining nur (light) and lan (people/tribe). Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most widely used male names in the country; reflects the Kazakh tradition of light-names.

kazakh.name: Aigerim
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the moon's beauty and the enchanting loveliness of its silver light.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'beautiful moon' or 'moon's charm.' Very common in Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular female names in modern Central Asia.

kazakh.name: Dauren
kazakh.meaning: Carries the sense of an era, a time of significance that shapes everything within it.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name meaning 'era' or 'epoch.' Very common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh naming tradition of names expressing temporal and historical significance.

kazakh.name: Saltanat
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of the sultanate and sovereign authority, the grandeur of royal power.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name derived from the Arabic Saltana, meaning 'sovereignty' or 'sultanate.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Persianate and Islamic influence on Kazakh naming traditions.

kazakh.name: Arman
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the ideal and cherished dream of something longed for but not yet attained.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name derived from the Persian Arman, meaning 'wish' or 'dream.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Persian influence on Kazakh culture.

kazakh.name: Assel
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of honey and the golden sweetness of something pure and delightfully pleasant.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name derived from the Arabic Asal, meaning 'honey.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Islamic cultural influence on Kazakh naming.

kazakh.name: Bekzat
kazakh.meaning: Carries the noble essence of a lord, the dignity of one born to lead and be respected.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining bek (lord/noble) and zat (essence). Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tribal tradition of names expressing noble status and leadership qualities.

kazakh.name: Gulsim
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of the rose's silver beauty, a flower radiant and delicate in moonlight.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name combining gul (flower/rose) and sim (silver). Used in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tradition of compound names drawing on floral and metallic imagery.

kazakh.name: Temirlan
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the iron hero, unyielding and strong as metal forged in the hottest fire.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name meaning 'iron hero' or combining temir (iron) with a heroic suffix. Common in Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Turkic tradition of naming boys for strength and endurance.

kazakh.name: Zarina
kazakh.meaning: Carries the golden quality of something precious and gleaming with warm radiance.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name derived from the Persian Zarin, meaning 'golden.' Common in Kazakhstan and across Central Asia; reflects the Persianate influence on Kazakh female naming traditions.

kazakh.name: Galym
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the scholar and the one whose learning runs deep and broad.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name derived from the Arabic Alim, meaning 'scholar' or 'knowledgeable.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Islamic tradition of valuing religious and secular scholarship.

kazakh.name: Meruert
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of the pearl, luminous and formed in quiet darkness, precious beyond measure.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'pearl.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most beloved Kazakh female names; reflects the Kazakh tradition of gem-names celebrating feminine beauty.

kazakh.name: Serik
kazakh.meaning: Carries the quality of the companion and the partner who walks alongside through every challenge.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name meaning 'companion' or 'fellow traveler.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh nomadic heritage in which the trusted companion on the steppe was deeply valued.

kazakh.name: Ainur
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the moonlight, that silvery illumination that falls gently and makes the night visible.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name combining ay (moon) and nur (light). Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Central Asian tradition of moon and light compound names.

kazakh.name: Kairat
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the vigilant and alert quality of one who stays awake and watchful through the night.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name meaning 'vigilant' or 'alert.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most recognizable traditional Kazakh male names; reflects the nomadic tradition of watchfulness.

kazakh.name: Zhuldyz
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of the star shining steadily overhead, a fixed point in the vast night sky.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'star.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most beloved Kazakh female names; reflects the importance of stars in Kazakh nomadic navigation and cosmology.

kazakh.name: Erlan
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the spirited freedom and strength of one who moves boldly through open landscapes.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining er (man/hero) and lan (wide/people). Very common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh cultural ideal of the strong, free man of the steppe.

kazakh.name: Saule
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the sun and its golden warmth pouring down generously on everything below.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'sun ray' or 'sunlight.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the ancient Kazakh reverence for the sun in steppe cosmology.

kazakh.name: Marat
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the wish and the desired goal that one sets their heart on and pursues.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name derived from the Arabic Murad, meaning 'wish' or 'desired.' Very common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Islamic influence on Kazakh naming alongside Soviet-era adoption.

kazakh.name: Balzhan
kazakh.meaning: Reflects the sweet soul and the honey-like quality of one whose spirit is gentle and pleasant.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name combining bal (honey) and zhan (soul/spirit). Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tradition of compound names combining sweetness with spiritual qualities.

kazakh.name: Adil
kazakh.meaning: Carries the just and equitable character of one who deals fairly with all.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name derived from the Arabic Adil, meaning 'just' or 'fair.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Islamic value of justice as a fundamental virtue.

kazakh.name: Karlygash
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the swallow in flight, swift and graceful, returning with the spring.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'swallow.' Very common in Kazakhstan; the swallow is associated with the return of warmth and spring; one of the most distinctively Kazakh female names.

kazakh.name: Bauyrzhan
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of the brotherly soul, the spirit of one bound to others by deepest kinship.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining bauyr (liver, seen as the seat of affection, meaning 'close relative') and zhan (soul). Very common in Kazakhstan; reflects the deep Kazakh emphasis on family loyalty.

kazakh.name: Moldir
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the transparent purity of something clear and crystalline, without any shadow.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'transparent' or 'crystal clear.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tradition of names expressing purity and clarity of character.

kazakh.name: Erzhan
kazakh.meaning: Carries the heroic soul and the brave spirit of one who faces challenges without hesitation.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining er (hero/man) and zhan (soul). Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh cultural ideal of heroic masculinity and the spiritual dimension of bravery.

kazakh.name: Zhibek
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the silk, soft and lustrous, flowing with a smoothness that delights the touch.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'silk.' Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most beloved traditional Kazakh female names; associated with the legendary figure Kyz Zhibek in Kazakh epic poetry.

kazakh.name: Alnur
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of light at the forehead, a radiance that shines from the very front.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining al (front/first) and nur (light). Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tradition of compound light-names expressing leadership and prominence.

kazakh.name: Venera
kazakh.meaning: Reflects the beauty of the evening star, bright and solitary in the twilight sky.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name derived from the Latin Venus/Venera through Russian usage. Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Soviet-era influence on Kazakh naming when classical names became fashionable.

kazakh.name: Almas
kazakh.meaning: Carries the hardness and brilliance of the diamond, the hardest and most dazzling of gems.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh name meaning 'diamond,' used for both males and females in Kazakhstan. Reflects the Kazakh tradition of gem-names and the Persian influence through the word Almas.

kazakh.name: Serzhan
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the soul of the chief, the spiritual essence of leadership and command.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining ser (chief/head) and zhan (soul). Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh tribal tradition of names expressing leadership qualities and authority.

kazakh.name: Aigul
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the moon flower, a blossom that opens under the silver light of the night sky.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name combining ay (moon) and gul (flower). Very common in Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular female names in Central Asia.

kazakh.name: Daniyar
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of wisdom combined with intimate closeness, a knowledgeable companion.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining dani (wise) and yar (friend/companion). Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most widely used male names in modern Kazakhstan.

kazakh.name: Nazgul
kazakh.meaning: Carries the coquettish flower and the graceful refinement of a bloom that draws all eyes.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name combining naz (grace/coquetry) and gul (flower). Common in Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Persianate influence on Central Asian female naming.

kazakh.name: Askar
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the soldier and the military man, one of disciplined strength and organized courage.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name derived from the Arabic Askar, meaning 'soldier' or 'army.' Very common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Islamic influence and the Kazakh tradition of warrior-names.

kazakh.name: Madina
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the luminous city, the place of civilization and gathered community.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name referring to the holy city of Medina. Common in Kazakhstan among Muslim families; reflects the Islamic pilgrimage tradition deeply integrated into Kazakh naming culture.

kazakh.name: Alibek
kazakh.meaning: Carries the nobility of the exalted lord, combining high standing with dignified authority.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining the Arabic Ali (exalted) with the Turkic bek (lord/noble). Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the blending of Arabic Islamic names with Turkic honorifics.

kazakh.name: Kamila
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of perfection and the complete excellence of one who is fully accomplished.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name derived from the Arabic Kamil, meaning 'perfect' or 'complete.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of virtue names expressing ideal character.

kazakh.name: Baurzhan
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the deep fraternal love that binds those who share blood and share the same hearth.
kazakh.notes: A variant spelling of Bauyrzhan, a very common Kazakh male name in Kazakhstan. Reflects the Kazakh cultural emphasis on brotherhood and kinship as the foundation of communal identity.

kazakh.name: Dilnoza
kazakh.meaning: Reflects the rose of the heart, a beauty that blooms within and warms all who come near.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh and Uzbek female name combining dil (heart) and noz/noza (rose). Common in Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate influence on Central Asian compound naming.

kazakh.name: Yerzhan
kazakh.meaning: Speaks of the manly soul and the brave spirit of one who embodies the ideal of heroism.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name combining er (man/hero) and zhan (soul). Very common in Kazakhstan; a variant of Erzhan; one of the most recognizable Kazakh male names.

kazakh.name: Tolkyn
kazakh.meaning: Carries the wave and its rolling energy, surging forward with persistent momentum.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name meaning 'wave.' Common in Kazakhstan; reflects the Kazakh poetic tradition of nature names drawing on the imagery of water and natural movement.

kazakh.name: Nurzhan
kazakh.meaning: Paints the image of the luminous soul, a spirit that radiates light wherever it moves.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh name combining nur (light) and zhan (soul), used for both males and females. Very common in Kazakhstan; one of the most popular compound names in the country.

kazakh.name: Gulzhan
kazakh.meaning: Evokes the floral soul, a spirit as fragrant and beautiful as a garden in full bloom.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh female name combining gul (flower) and zhan (soul). Common in Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Central Asian tradition of compound names joining natural beauty with the spiritual dimension.

kazakh.name: Ruslan
kazakh.meaning: Reflects the lion and the fearless courage of one who faces the world with sovereign strength.
kazakh.notes: A Kazakh male name derived from the Turkic Arslan, meaning 'lion.' Very common in Kazakhstan and across the former Soviet Union; reflects the Turkic lion-name tradition; popularized by Pushkin's epic poem.

uzbek.name: Dilnoza
uzbek.meaning: Carries the rose of the heart, a beauty that blooms within and fills all around with warmth.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name combining dil (heart) and noza (tender/rose). Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular female names in the country; reflects the Persianate influence on Uzbek naming.

uzbek.name: Jasur
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the bold courage of one who faces danger with a heart that does not know fear.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Arabic Jasur, meaning 'brave' or 'bold.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most widely used male names; reflects the Islamic naming tradition.

uzbek.name: Mohira
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the skilled and expert person whose mastery of their craft is complete.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Mahira, meaning 'skilled' or 'expert.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek tradition of virtue names expressing excellence and mastery.

uzbek.name: Sherzod
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of the lion-born, one who comes from a lineage of strength and courage.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining sher (lion) and zod (born of/son of). Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate tradition of lion-names expressing strength and noble origin.

uzbek.name: Feruza
uzbek.meaning: Carries the essence of a luminous blue-green stone, precious and radiant.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Persian Firouza, meaning 'turquoise.' Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the historical significance of turquoise along the Silk Road through Central Asia.

uzbek.name: Behruz
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the lucky day and the auspicious beginning of a fortunate life well-started.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Persian Bahruz, meaning 'lucky day' or 'fortunate.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate influence on Uzbek male naming traditions.

uzbek.name: Zulfiya
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the curl and the winding lock of hair, a detail of beauty that enchants all who see it.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Zulfa, meaning 'a lock of hair.' Very common in Uzbekistan; associated with the revered Uzbek poetess Zulfiya; one of the most beloved female names.

uzbek.name: Otabek
uzbek.meaning: Carries the authority of the fatherly lord, one who combines paternal wisdom with noble bearing.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining ota (father) and bek (lord/noble). Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Uzbek tradition of compound names honoring paternal authority.

uzbek.name: Barno
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of the young girl in the fullness of her youth, tender and full of promise.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name meaning 'young girl' or 'maiden.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most distinctively Uzbek female names; reflects the tradition of names celebrating youth and freshness.

uzbek.name: Sanjar
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the warrior who pierces through every obstacle with his lance and emerges victorious.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name meaning 'he who pierces' or associated with the Seljuk sultan Sanjar. Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Turkic martial heritage and the admiration for historical warrior-rulers.

uzbek.name: Gulbahor
uzbek.meaning: Carries the spring flower, that blossom which opens first and announces the new season.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name combining gul (flower) and bahor (spring). Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most beloved female names; reflects the Uzbek poetic tradition of spring and flower imagery.

uzbek.name: Ulugbek
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the great lord, one of magnificent stature and wide-reaching authority.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining ulug (great) and bek (lord). Common in Uzbekistan; associated with the 15th-century Timurid ruler and astronomer Ulugbek; reflects Uzbek pride in the Timurid Golden Age.

uzbek.name: Maftuna
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of one who is enchanted and captivated, completely lost in devotion.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Maftun, meaning 'enchanted' or 'infatuated.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek poetic tradition of names expressing the state of being captivated by beauty.

uzbek.name: Nodir
uzbek.meaning: Carries the rarity and preciousness of something that appears seldom and is cherished when found.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Arabic Nadir, meaning 'rare' or 'scarce.' Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names expressing exceptional quality.

uzbek.name: Hulkar
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the Pleiades star cluster, those seven brilliant points scattered across the night sky.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name meaning 'Pleiades' or related to the star cluster. Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the ancient Central Asian tradition of astronomical naming connected to Sogdian and Persian star lore.

uzbek.name: Sardor
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the commander and the leader who stands at the front and guides the rest.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name meaning 'commander' or 'leader.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular male names in modern Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate tradition of leadership names.

uzbek.name: Nargiza
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of the narcissus flower, pale and fragrant, the first to bloom after winter.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Persian Nargis, meaning 'narcissus.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the deep Persianate influence on Uzbek culture.

uzbek.name: Bobur
uzbek.meaning: Carries the power and magnificence of the tiger, that striped hunter of the forest.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name meaning 'tiger' or derived from the word babur. Used in Uzbekistan; associated with Babur, the founder of the Mughal Empire who was born in Fergana, Uzbekistan; used as a name of cultural pride.

uzbek.name: Zilola
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the shadow and the gentle shade cast by something tall and protective overhead.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Zill, meaning 'shade' or 'shadow.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek naming tradition of names evoking protection and cool relief from the heat.

uzbek.name: Mansur
uzbek.meaning: Reflects the triumphant quality of one whom the divine has aided to final and complete victory.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Arabic Mansur, meaning 'victorious.' Common in Uzbekistan; associated with the Sufi mystic Mansur al-Hallaj; reflects the influence of Sufism in Central Asian Islamic culture.

uzbek.name: Shahnoza
uzbek.meaning: Carries the kingly grace and the refined elegance of one bearing royal coquetry.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name combining shah (king/royal) and noz (grace/coquetry). Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Persianate tradition of royal compound names.

uzbek.name: Alisher
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the exalted lion, one of noble status and lion-like courage combined.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining the Arabic Ali (exalted) with the Persian sher (lion). Very common in Uzbekistan; associated with the great 15th-century Uzbek poet Alisher Navoi, the father of Uzbek literature.

uzbek.name: Muazzam
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of the glorified and exalted one, raised up and honored above the ordinary.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Muazzam, meaning 'glorified' or 'exalted.' Used in Uzbekistan; reflects the Arabic and Islamic influence on Uzbek female naming traditions.

uzbek.name: Dilshod
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the happy heart, a spirit made joyful and whose inner world is at peace.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining dil (heart) and shod (happy). Very common in Uzbekistan and Tajikistan; reflects the Persianate tradition of compound heart-names expressing emotional states.

uzbek.name: Lobar
uzbek.meaning: Carries the flicker of the flame, that dancing light that moves and cannot be held still.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name meaning 'flame' or related to fire imagery. Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek tradition of names evoking light and warmth through the imagery of fire.

uzbek.name: Abdulloh
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the devoted service and humble surrender of one dedicated entirely to the divine.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name, the local form of Abdullah. Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most used male names in the country; reflects the centrality of Islamic faith in Uzbek naming culture.

uzbek.name: Javlon
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the swift movement and agile energy of one who races through the world with vigor.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Arabic Jawlan, meaning 'wandering' or 'roaming freely.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the tradition of names expressing freedom of movement.

uzbek.name: Nafisa
uzbek.meaning: Carries the precious and refined quality of something rare and valuable beyond ordinary measure.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Nafisa, meaning 'precious' or 'refined.' Common in Uzbekistan; associated with Nafisa bint al-Hasan, a revered Islamic scholar; widely used across Central Asia.

uzbek.name: Komil
uzbek.meaning: Reflects the completeness and perfection of one who has achieved the fullest development.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Arabic Kamil, meaning 'perfect' or 'complete.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names expressing ideal virtue and accomplished character.

uzbek.name: Malika
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the queen and the sovereign lady whose bearing commands respect and admiration.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Malika, meaning 'queen.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Islamic and Persianate tradition of regal feminine names.

uzbek.name: Mirzo
uzbek.meaning: Carries the authority of the scribe-prince, one of both learning and noble standing.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from Mirza, meaning 'prince' or 'nobleman-scribe.' Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Timurid and Mughal courtly tradition where Mirza was a title of educated nobility.

uzbek.name: Nilufar
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of the lotus or water lily, floating serenely on still water, pure and untouched.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Persian Niloufar, meaning 'lotus.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most beloved female names in the country; reflects the Persianate poetic naming tradition.

uzbek.name: Firdavs
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the highest paradise, a garden of eternal beauty where peace and joy are without end.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Persian Firdaws, meaning 'paradise.' Common in Uzbekistan; also the name of the great Persian epic poet Ferdowsi; reflects the deep reverence for classical Persian literature.

uzbek.name: Sarvinoz
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the cypress-like grace of one who moves tall and slender with elegant refinement.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name combining sarv (cypress tree) and noz (grace). Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek poetic tradition of the cypress as a symbol of elegant stature.

uzbek.name: Baxtiyor
uzbek.meaning: Carries the fortune of one who is blessed by good luck and has the favor of destiny.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name derived from the Persian Bakhtiyar, meaning 'fortunate' or 'lucky.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most widely used male names; reflects the Persianate influence.

uzbek.name: Muhabbat
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of love in its fullest sense, the affection that binds people deeply together.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Muhabbat, meaning 'love' or 'affection.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek and Tajik Sufi poetic tradition in which love is the highest spiritual state.

uzbek.name: Otajon
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the fatherly soul, the dear father whose spirit is revered and carried forward.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining ota (father) and jon (soul/dear). Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek tradition of names honoring paternal relationships and familial respect.

uzbek.name: Gavhar
uzbek.meaning: Reflects the precious gem, brilliant and formed under pressure, rare and deeply valuable.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Persian Gauhar, meaning 'jewel' or 'precious gem.' Very common in Uzbekistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Persianate tradition of gem-names.

uzbek.name: Ulmas
uzbek.meaning: Carries the imperishable quality of something that does not wither or fade with time.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name meaning 'immortal' or 'everlasting.' Used in Uzbekistan; reflects the Uzbek tradition of names expressing enduring qualities beyond ordinary life.

uzbek.name: Xurshida
uzbek.meaning: Speaks of the sun and its radiant light warming everything it touches with generous brightness.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Persian Khurshid, meaning 'sun.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate solar naming tradition; the Uzbek spelling with Xh- reflects the Uzbek Latin alphabet.

uzbek.name: Ibrohim
uzbek.meaning: Carries the legacy of the great patriarch whose faith anchored generations that followed.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name, the local form of Ibrahim. Very common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Quranic tradition of prophetic naming that is central to Islamic naming practices in Uzbek culture.

uzbek.name: Manzura
uzbek.meaning: Evokes the one who is looked upon with divine approval and regarded with gracious favor.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek female name derived from the Arabic Manzur, meaning 'approved' or 'regarded with favor.' Common in Uzbekistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of names expressing divine approbation.

uzbek.name: Shamsiddin
uzbek.meaning: Paints the image of the sun of the faith, the radiant illumination of religious devotion.
uzbek.notes: An Uzbek male name combining the Arabic shams (sun) and din (religion/faith). Used in Uzbekistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of compound names honoring both celestial imagery and religious identity.

kyrgyz.name: Aizat
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the moon's pure essence and the dignity of one touched by celestial grace.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining ay (moon) and zat (essence). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the shared Central Asian Turkic tradition of lunar compound names between Kyrgyz and Kazakh communities.

kyrgyz.name: Manas
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the great hero and the spirit of a people embodied in one legendary champion.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name associated with Manas, the hero of the world's longest epic, the Manas Epic. Very common in Kyrgyzstan; carries the deepest possible connection to Kyrgyz national identity.

kyrgyz.name: Aigerim
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the moon's enchanting beauty and the captivating loveliness of its silver light.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'beautiful moon' or 'moon's charm.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular female names in the country; shared with Kazakh naming tradition.

kyrgyz.name: Bakyt
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of happiness and the good fortune that makes a life feel complete.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'happiness' or 'good fortune.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most widely used male names; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of names expressing joyful well-being.

kyrgyz.name: Nurzat
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the luminous essence, a being whose very nature radiates light and brightness.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining nur (light) and zat (essence/being). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of compound light-names celebrating inner radiance.

kyrgyz.name: Tilek
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the wish and the heartfelt desire of one who reaches toward what they truly want.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'wish' or 'desire.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of names expressing aspiration and longing.

kyrgyz.name: Gulnara
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the pomegranate flower, that vivid red blossom announcing the fruit's hidden abundance.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining gul (flower) and nar (pomegranate). Very common in Kyrgyzstan and across Central Asia; reflects the Persianate influence on Kyrgyz naming traditions.

kyrgyz.name: Azamat
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the grandeur and magnificence of something vast and awe-inspiring in its scale.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from the Arabic Azamat, meaning 'grandeur' or 'magnificence.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most recognizable Kyrgyz male names; reflects Islamic cultural influence.

kyrgyz.name: Kanykei
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the beloved blood, one connected to the heart's deepest affection.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name associated with Kanykei, the wise and devoted wife of Manas in the Kyrgyz epic. Common in Kyrgyzstan; carries the weight of literary and cultural tradition.

kyrgyz.name: Erkin
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the free and unbound spirit, one who moves through the world without chains.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'free' or 'independent.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz cultural emphasis on freedom deeply embedded in nomadic steppe identity.

kyrgyz.name: Aijan
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the moon's soul, a spirit as radiant and serene as moonlight on still water.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining ay (moon) and jan (soul). Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of compound soul-and-moon names.

kyrgyz.name: Talant
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the talent and the gifted quality of one who has been blessed with natural ability.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from the Russian/Greek Talant, meaning 'talent.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Soviet-era influence on Kyrgyz naming when secular ability-names became popular.

kyrgyz.name: Zuura
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the planet Venus, the evening star shining brightly at the horizon.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name derived from the Arabic Zuhra, meaning 'Venus' or 'brilliance.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Islamic astronomical naming tradition.

kyrgyz.name: Nurdin
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the light of the faith, the radiance of religious devotion illuminating the way.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name combining nur (light) and din (faith/religion). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Islamic influence on Kyrgyz naming and the tradition of compound religious names.

kyrgyz.name: Tolkun
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the wave and its rolling forward energy, persistent and impossible to stop.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'wave.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz poetic tradition of nature names drawing on water imagery; shared with the Kazakh name Tolkyn.

kyrgyz.name: Kanat
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the wing spreading wide for flight, lifting the bird upward into the open sky.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'wing.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most recognizable Kyrgyz male names; reflects the nomadic tradition of bird imagery as a symbol of freedom.

kyrgyz.name: Ainura
kyrgyz.meaning: Reflects the moonlight and its gentle radiance, falling softly over the sleeping world.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining ay (moon) and nur (light). Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular female names; a variant form of Ainur shared across Central Asian cultures.

kyrgyz.name: Mirlan
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the lord's people, a name expressing the bond between leader and tribe.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name combining mir (lord/prince) and lan (people). Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most widely used male names in modern Kyrgyz society.

kyrgyz.name: Jyldyz
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the star shining steadily in the night sky, a fixed point that guides those below.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'star.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most beloved female names; reflects the Kyrgyz nomadic tradition of stellar navigation and the cosmological significance of stars.

kyrgyz.name: Almaz
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the hardness and dazzling brilliance of the diamond, most precious of all stones.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz name meaning 'diamond,' used for both males and females in Kyrgyzstan. Reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of gem-names; the word comes from Persian through the Silk Road.

kyrgyz.name: Eliza
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the divine promise, the vow made and kept across generations.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name used in modern Kyrgyzstan. Reflects the influence of European names adopted in the Soviet era and continuing in post-Soviet Kyrgyz naming practice.

kyrgyz.name: Ulan
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the identity of the young man and the vigor of youth entering the world.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'young man' or 'son.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most distinctively Kyrgyz male names; reflects the nomadic tradition of names celebrating youthful masculine energy.

kyrgyz.name: Baktygul
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the flower of happiness, a bloom that opens only for those who are fortunate.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining bakyt (happiness) and gul (flower). Used in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of compound names joining emotional states with natural imagery.

kyrgyz.name: Alymkul
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the servant of the learned one, a person dedicated to knowledge and wisdom.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name combining alym (learned/scholar) and kul (servant). Used in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Islamic tradition of humility before knowledge and the Central Asian reverence for learning.

kyrgyz.name: Cholpon
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the morning star, that bright herald of dawn appearing before all others.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'morning star' or the planet Venus at dawn. Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most distinctive Kyrgyz female names; associated with the beloved Kyrgyz novel Cholpon.

kyrgyz.name: Bolot
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the hardness and enduring strength of steel, that refined metal of extraordinary toughness.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'steel.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of strong material names expressing hardness and resilience; the word came via Russian from the Slavic.

kyrgyz.name: Nazgul
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the graceful coquettish flower, delicate and drawing all eyes with its beauty.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining naz (grace/coquetry) and gul (flower). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Persianate influence on Central Asian compound naming traditions.

kyrgyz.name: Maksat
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the goal and the purpose that one pursues with focused and determined intention.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from the Arabic Maqsad, meaning 'goal' or 'purpose.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Islamic and Persianate influence.

kyrgyz.name: Zulaika
kyrgyz.meaning: Reflects the beauty of the beloved who captivates with a loveliness that cannot be resisted.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name derived from the Arabic Zulaikha, the wife of Potiphar in the Quranic story of Joseph. Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Islamic literary tradition deeply woven into Kyrgyz culture.

kyrgyz.name: Dastan
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the epic story told around the fire, the tale of heroes passed from mouth to mouth.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'epic story' or 'legend.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the central importance of oral epic tradition in Kyrgyz culture; the Manas is itself a dastan.

kyrgyz.name: Anar
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the pomegranate, that jeweled fruit whose seeds suggest abundance beyond counting.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'pomegranate.' Common in Kyrgyzstan and across Central Asia; the pomegranate is a symbol of fertility and abundance in Turkic and Persian cultures.

kyrgyz.name: Kubat
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the strong and powerful one whose force is solid and immovable.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name meaning 'strength' or 'power.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of names expressing masculine strength and the physical power admired in nomadic society.

kyrgyz.name: Bermet
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the pearl and its luminous beauty, formed in patient darkness and precious beyond measure.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'pearl.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan; one of the most beloved Kyrgyz female names; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of gem-names celebrating rare and enduring beauty.

kyrgyz.name: Mukhtar
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the quality of being chosen and selected above all others for a special purpose.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from the Arabic Mukhtar, meaning 'the chosen one.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names expressing divine selection and favor.

kyrgyz.name: Ainagul
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the moon flower, a blossom opening under the silver light of the night sky.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining ayna (mirror/moon) and gul (flower). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Central Asian tradition of compound names joining lunar imagery with floral beauty.

kyrgyz.name: Nurbol
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of one who has become light, a person transformed into radiance itself.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name combining nur (light) and bol (be/become). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz Islamic tradition of light-names and the aspiration to embody divine radiance.

kyrgyz.name: Begim
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the princess and the noble lady of high standing who carries herself with dignity.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name derived from the Turkic Begim, meaning 'princess' or 'noblewoman.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tribal tradition of names expressing aristocratic status.

kyrgyz.name: Omurbek
kyrgyz.meaning: Reflects the long-lived lord, one whose life spans many years and whose authority endures.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name combining omur (life/lifespan) and bek (lord/noble). Very common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of compound names combining the wish for long life with noble status.

kyrgyz.name: Sezim
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the intuition and the sensitive feeling of one who perceives what others cannot see.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name meaning 'intuition' or 'sensitivity.' Common in modern Kyrgyzstan; reflects the contemporary Kyrgyz naming tradition of names expressing inner perceptual qualities.

kyrgyz.name: Adil
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the just and equitable character of one who deals fairly with all around them.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from the Arabic Adil, meaning 'just' or 'fair.' Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Islamic tradition of justice-names embedded in Kyrgyz Muslim naming culture.

kyrgyz.name: Gulsanam
kyrgyz.meaning: Paints the image of the flower idol, a blossom so beautiful it becomes an object of devotion.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining gul (flower) and sanam (idol/beloved). Used in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Persianate Sufi tradition of the beloved as a divine flower deserving worship.

kyrgyz.name: Ruslan
kyrgyz.meaning: Evokes the lion and the fearless sovereign courage of one who faces the world without fear.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from the Turkic Arslan, meaning 'lion.' Very common in Kyrgyzstan and across the former Soviet Union; one of the most widely recognized Turkic lion-names.

kyrgyz.name: Kumushay
kyrgyz.meaning: Carries the silver moon, a celestial body of soft radiance and enduring gentle beauty.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz female name combining kumush (silver) and ay (moon). Common in Kyrgyzstan; reflects the Kyrgyz tradition of compound names drawing on precious metals and celestial imagery.

kyrgyz.name: Chyngyz
kyrgyz.meaning: Speaks of the vast ocean and the immeasurable power of water extending beyond all sight.
kyrgyz.notes: A Kyrgyz male name derived from Chinggis, meaning 'oceanic' or 'vast.' Used in Kyrgyzstan; the name was revived in the Soviet era largely through the influence of the revered Kyrgyz writer Chingiz Aitmatov.

tajik.name: Farzona
tajik.meaning: Carries the wisdom and learning of one whose understanding exceeds the ordinary measure.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Persian Farzana, meaning 'wise' or 'learned.' Very common in Tajikistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Persianate literary and scholarly naming tradition.

tajik.name: Firuz
tajik.meaning: Evokes the luminous blue-green of a precious stone, brilliant and radiant.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Persian Firuz, meaning 'turquoise' or 'victorious.' Very common in Tajikistan; reflects the ancient Persian naming tradition and the historical significance of turquoise.

tajik.name: Dilnoza
tajik.meaning: Speaks of the rose of the heart, a beauty that blooms within and warms all who come near.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name combining dil (heart) and noza (rose/tender). Very common in Tajikistan and Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate tradition of compound heart-names celebrating inner beauty.

tajik.name: Davron
tajik.meaning: Paints the image of the era and the time, the epoch that shapes everything within it.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Arabic Dawran, meaning 'era' or 'time.' Very common in Tajikistan; reflects the Arabic and Islamic influence on Tajik naming alongside the Persian tradition.

tajik.name: Sabohat
tajik.meaning: Carries the freshness of morning and the early beauty that comes before the heat of day.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Arabic Sabaha, meaning 'morning freshness' or 'beauty of the morning.' Common in Tajikistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names celebrating morning light.

tajik.name: Suhail
tajik.meaning: Evokes the bright star Canopus, visible in the southern sky and used by sailors to navigate.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Arabic Suhayl, the name of the star Canopus. Common in Tajikistan; reflects the Islamic astronomical tradition and the importance of stars in Persian navigation poetry.

tajik.name: Nargis
tajik.meaning: Speaks of the narcissus flower, pale and fragrant, its petals delicate against the spring green.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Persian Nargis, meaning 'narcissus.' Very common in Tajikistan; one of the most beloved female names; reflects the Persianate tradition of spring flower naming.

tajik.name: Parviz
tajik.meaning: Carries the victorious quality of one who prevails and finds success in all their endeavors.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Persian Parviz, meaning 'victorious.' Very common in Tajikistan; associated with the Sassanid king Khusrau Parviz; reflects the deep Persianate naming heritage.

tajik.name: Zulfiya
tajik.meaning: Paints the image of the curl and the winding lock of hair, a beauty that enchants all who see it.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Arabic Zulfa, meaning 'a lock of hair.' Very common in Tajikistan and Uzbekistan; reflects the Islamic poetic tradition in which the beloved's hair is a central image.

tajik.name: Bahodir
tajik.meaning: Evokes the bold and fearless hero whose courage carries them through every danger.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Persian Bahadur, meaning 'brave' or 'hero.' Very common in Tajikistan and Uzbekistan; reflects the Persianate and Mongol tradition of bravery-names.

tajik.name: Sarvinoz
tajik.meaning: Carries the cypress-like grace of one who moves tall and slender with elegant refinement.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name combining sarv (cypress) and noz (grace/coquetry). Very common in Tajikistan; reflects the Tajik-Persian poetic tradition in which the cypress is the ultimate symbol of graceful stature.

tajik.name: Mirzo
tajik.meaning: Speaks of the prince-scribe, one who combines noble standing with the accomplishments of learning.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from Mirza, meaning 'prince' or 'nobleman who can write.' Very common in Tajikistan; reflects the Timurid and Persian courtly tradition of educated nobility.

tajik.name: Maftuna
tajik.meaning: Paints the image of one who is enchanted and completely captivated by what they love.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Arabic Maftun, meaning 'enchanted' or 'infatuated.' Common in Tajikistan; reflects the Sufi poetic tradition in which being enchanted by the beloved is a spiritual state.

tajik.name: Rustam
tajik.meaning: Evokes the legendary hero of invincible strength, the greatest warrior who ever lived.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name associated with Rustam, the invincible hero of Ferdowsi's Shahnameh. Very common in Tajikistan and across the Persian cultural sphere; one of the most celebrated names in Persian literature.

tajik.name: Muazzama
tajik.meaning: Carries the glorified and exalted quality of one raised above the ordinary in dignity.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Arabic Muazzam, meaning 'glorified' or 'exalted.' Used in Tajikistan; reflects the Arabic influence on Tajik naming through the Islamic tradition.

tajik.name: Ibrohim
tajik.meaning: Reflects the legacy of the great patriarch whose faith was the foundation of generations.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name, the local form of Ibrahim. Very common in Tajikistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of naming children after the prophets mentioned in the Quran.

tajik.name: Mahliyo
tajik.meaning: Speaks of the moon-faced beauty, one whose face shines with the gentle radiance of the moon.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name combining mah (moon) and liyo (face/cheek). Very common in Tajikistan; one of the most beloved female names; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of the moon-faced beloved.

tajik.name: Komron
tajik.meaning: Paints the image of the successful one who achieves all they set out to accomplish.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Persian Kamran, meaning 'successful' or 'prosperous.' Very common in Tajikistan; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Persianate tradition.

tajik.name: Gulbahor
tajik.meaning: Evokes the spring flower, that blossom which opens first and heralds the arrival of warmth.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name combining gul (flower) and bahor (spring). Very common in Tajikistan and Uzbekistan; reflects the Persian spring-naming tradition celebrating Nowruz and seasonal renewal.

tajik.name: Sirojiddin
tajik.meaning: Carries the lamp of the faith, its light burning steadily to guide all who seek the right path.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name combining the Arabic siraj (lamp) and din (faith/religion). Common in Tajikistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of compound names combining religious devotion with luminous imagery.

tajik.name: Ozoda
tajik.meaning: Speaks of freedom and the unbound spirit of one who moves through the world without constraint.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Persian Azada, meaning 'free' or 'noble.' Very common in Tajikistan; one of the most popular female names in the country; reflects the Persianate value of freedom.

tajik.name: Firdavs
tajik.meaning: Carries the highest paradise, a garden of eternal beauty where peace and joy never end.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Persian Firdaws, meaning 'paradise.' Common in Tajikistan; also associated with the poet Ferdowsi; reflects the deep reverence for Persian classical literature in Tajik culture.

tajik.name: Nasiba
tajik.meaning: Paints the image of one's destined lot, the fortune that has been allocated to them.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Arabic Nasib, meaning 'destiny' or 'fate.' Common in Tajikistan; reflects the Islamic concept of divine predestination integrated into naming practice.

tajik.name: Navruz
tajik.meaning: Evokes the new day and the fresh beginning celebrated at the turning of the year.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name meaning 'new day,' referring to the Persian New Year festival Nowruz. Used in Tajikistan; reflects the centrality of Nowruz to Tajik cultural and national identity.

tajik.name: Rohila
tajik.meaning: Carries the gentle and easy quality of one who moves through the world without creating disturbance.
tajik.notes: A Tajik female name derived from the Arabic Rahila, meaning 'gentle' or 'easy.' Used in Tajikistan; reflects the Arabic influence on Tajik female naming through Islamic tradition.

tajik.name: Khurshed
tajik.meaning: Speaks of the sun and the radiant light that defines the day and sustains all life below.
tajik.notes: A Tajik male name derived from the Persian Khurshid, meaning 'sun.' Common in Tajikistan; reflects the ancient Persian reverence for the sun preserved in Zoroastrian and Islamic naming traditions.

tajik.name: Muhabbat
tajik.meaning: Paints the image of love in its purest form, the deep affection that binds souls together.
