odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Ashita, meaning 'dark' or 'one who has not eaten' (in a spiritual context). Used in Odisha; reflects the Odia Sanskrit naming tradition.

odia.name: Swagatika
odia.meaning: Speaks of the welcome and the warm greeting extended to one whose arrival is celebrated.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Svagata, meaning 'welcome.' Common in Odisha; one of the distinctively Odia female names reflecting the tradition of names expressing warmth and hospitality.

odia.name: Gouranga
odia.meaning: Paints the image of the golden-complexioned divine being, radiant with the beauty of pure gold.
odia.notes: An Odia male name meaning 'golden complexioned,' a name for the Vaishnava saint Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Common in Odisha; reflects the significant influence of the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition in Odisha.

odia.name: Priyanka
odia.meaning: Evokes the endearing and beloved quality of one who is cherished by all who know her.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Priyanka, meaning 'beloved.' Common in Odisha and across India; reflects the Sanskrit tradition of names expressing deep affection.

odia.name: Biswanath
odia.meaning: Carries the lord of the universe, the sovereign deity whose authority encompasses all of existence.
odia.notes: An Odia male name meaning 'lord of the universe,' a name for Shiva or Vishnu. Very common in Odisha; reflects the Odia tradition of names expressing the all-encompassing sovereignty of the divine.

odia.name: Sunita
odia.meaning: Speaks of the well-guided one of upright conduct, a person rightly directed along the proper path.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Sunita, meaning 'well-guided' or 'of good conduct.' Common in Odisha; reflects the Odia tradition of names expressing right conduct and proper guidance.

odia.name: Tapan
odia.meaning: Paints the image of the summer sun blazing down with intense heat, the great warming force.
odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Tapana, meaning 'the sun' or 'that which heats.' Very common in Odisha; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Odia solar naming tradition.

odia.name: Sweta
odia.meaning: Evokes the pure white light and the immaculate quality of something free from all shadow.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Shveta, meaning 'white' or 'pure.' Very common in Odisha; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Odia tradition of purity names.

odia.name: Satyanarayan
odia.meaning: Evokes the eternal preserver of cosmic order, the ultimate truth woven through all existence.
odia.notes: An Odia male name combining satya (truth) with Narayana (Vishnu). Common in Odisha; the Satyanarayan puja is one of the most popular Vaishnava rituals in Odisha; the name expresses deep devotion.

odia.name: Kabita
odia.meaning: Speaks of the poem, the beautiful arrangement of language that expresses what cannot be spoken plainly.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Kavita, meaning 'poem.' Common in Odisha; reflects the rich Odia literary tradition in which poetry has occupied a central place for centuries.

odia.name: Sudhansu
odia.meaning: Reflects the moon and its gentle nectar-light, the cool beam that soothes the night.
odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Sudhamshu, meaning 'moon' or 'moon-rayed.' Common in Odisha; reflects the Odia tradition of lunar names celebrating the gentle light of the moon.

odia.name: Ipsita
odia.meaning: Paints the image of the deeply desired one, the person or thing one has longed for with great intensity.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Ipsita, meaning 'desired' or 'wished for.' Very common in Odisha; one of the most popular female names; one of the most distinctively Odia female names.

odia.name: Ambarish
odia.meaning: Evokes the lord of the sky, the sovereign deity who rules over all that is vast and celestial.
odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Ambarisha, meaning 'lord of the sky.' Used in Odisha; associated with a devotee-king in the Bhagavata Purana; reflects the Odia Vaishnava tradition.

odia.name: Rashmita
odia.meaning: Carries the ray of light and the one who shines with its radiance, a beam of living brightness.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Rashmi, meaning 'ray of light.' Very common in Odisha; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Odia tradition of light-names.

odia.name: Manoj
odia.meaning: Speaks of the one born of the mind, the mind-born deity of love who springs from thought itself.
odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Manoja, meaning 'born of the mind,' a name for Kamadeva the god of love. Very common in Odisha and across India; one of the most widely used male names.

odia.name: Lalita
odia.meaning: Paints the image of the playful and graceful woman who moves through the world with ease and charm.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Lalita, meaning 'playful' or 'graceful.' Common in Odisha; associated with the goddess Lalita Tripura Sundari; reflects the Odia Shakta naming tradition.

odia.name: Prabhakar
odia.meaning: Evokes the sun and the maker of light, the great creator whose radiance illuminates all things.
odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Prabhakara, meaning 'one who makes light' or 'the sun.' Common in Odisha; reflects the Odia solar naming tradition and the ancient Vedic reverence for the sun.

odia.name: Nirupama
odia.meaning: Carries the quality of being without equal, incomparable in beauty and excellence beyond all measure.
odia.notes: An Odia female name derived from the Sanskrit Nirupama, meaning 'without equal' or 'incomparable.' Common in Odisha; reflects the Odia tradition of names expressing singular and unmatched excellence.

odia.name: Ratnakar
odia.meaning: Speaks of the ocean as the maker of gems, the vast deep that produces all that is precious.
odia.notes: An Odia male name derived from the Sanskrit Ratnakar, meaning 'maker of gems' or 'ocean.' Common in Odisha; associated with the sage Valmiki (originally Ratnakar) who composed the Ramayana.

nepali.name: Sita
nepali.meaning: Carries the sacred furrow in the earth, the one drawn out of the ground by a divine plough.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Sita, meaning 'furrow.' Very common in Nepal; associated with Sita, the wife of Rama and the daughter of the earth in the Ramayana, deeply revered in Nepal.

nepali.name: Ramesh
nepali.meaning: Evokes a righteous ruler whose wisdom and benevolence govern with noble strength.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Ramesha, meaning 'lord Rama.' Very common in Nepal; reflects the deep Vaishnava tradition and the reverence for Rama that is central to Nepali Hindu culture.

nepali.name: Sunita
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the well-guided one whose conduct is upright and whose path is properly directed.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Sunita, meaning 'well-guided' or 'of good conduct.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Nepali naming tradition.

nepali.name: Bikash
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the unfolding and blossoming of something opening to its fullest expression.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Vikas, meaning 'development' or 'blossoming.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most popular male names; reflects the modern Nepali aspiration for growth.

nepali.name: Anita
nepali.meaning: Carries the grace and amiable quality of one who moves through the world with pleasant ease.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Anita, meaning 'grace.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most popular female names in the country across all regions and communities.

nepali.name: Dipak
nepali.meaning: Evokes the blazing lamp whose flame drives back the darkness with its warm and steady light.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Dipaka, meaning 'lamp' or 'kindling.' Very common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali Hindu tradition of light-names connected to knowledge and divine guidance.

nepali.name: Sabina
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the morning and its freshness, the early hours when the world is clean and still.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name used in Nepal. Reflects the modern Nepali naming tradition of adopting names with an international sound; common among both Hindu and Muslim Nepali families.

nepali.name: Anil
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the wind, that invisible and free-moving force that flows through all spaces.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Anila, meaning 'wind.' Very common in Nepal; reflects the Vedic tradition of naming children for the five elements; one of the most used male names in Nepal.

nepali.name: Manisha
nepali.meaning: Carries the wisdom of the mind, the intellectual queen whose intelligence is her greatest power.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Manisha, meaning 'wisdom of the mind' or 'queen of the mind.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most popular female names across the country.

nepali.name: Suresh
nepali.meaning: Evokes the lord of the gods, the divine sovereign who presides over the entire celestial realm.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Suresha, meaning 'lord of the gods.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most widely used male names; reflects the Vedic naming tradition.

nepali.name: Puja
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the act of worship, the devotional offering made with complete reverence and open heart.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Puja, meaning 'worship.' Very common in Nepal; reflects the centrality of daily puja in Nepali Hindu household life.

nepali.name: Rajan
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the king and the sovereign dignity of one who bears a regal bearing naturally.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Raja, meaning 'king.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali tradition of regal names and the historical importance of the monarchy in Nepali culture.

nepali.name: Kabita
nepali.meaning: Carries the poem and the beauty of language shaped into verse that moves the heart.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Kavita, meaning 'poem.' Common in Nepal; reflects the rich Nepali literary tradition and the cultural importance of poetry in Nepali culture.

nepali.name: Nabin
nepali.meaning: Evokes the new and fresh quality of something recently arrived, full of the energy of beginning.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Nava, meaning 'new.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Nepali tradition of names expressing freshness and new beginning.

nepali.name: Durga
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the invincible goddess who cannot be reached or overcome by any force in existence.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name associated with the goddess Durga, the warrior mother goddess. Common in Nepal; the goddess Durga is one of the most widely worshipped deities in Nepal, especially during Dasain.

nepali.name: Pramod
nepali.meaning: Carries the joy and delight of one who radiates happiness and spreads gladness around them.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Pramoda, meaning 'joy' or 'delight.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most recognizable Nepali male names; reflects the Sanskrit tradition of joy-names.

nepali.name: Samjhana
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of memory and the act of remembering, holding what is dear alive in the mind.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Nepali samjhana, meaning 'memory' or 'remembrance.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most distinctively Nepali female names; reflects the Nepali linguistic naming tradition.

nepali.name: Binod
nepali.meaning: Evokes the happy and playful one whose nature is full of joyful amusement and lighthearted delight.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Vinoda, meaning 'happy' or 'playful.' Common in Nepal; one of the most widely recognized Nepali male names; reflects the Sanskrit tradition.

nepali.name: Nirmala
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the pure and spotless quality of one unstained by anything that might diminish them.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Nirmala, meaning 'pure' or 'spotless.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali Hindu tradition of names expressing spiritual purity and cleanliness of character.

nepali.name: Suman
nepali.meaning: Carries the beauty of the flower and the good mind, a person of both outer and inner loveliness.
nepali.notes: A Nepali name derived from the Sanskrit Sumana, meaning 'flower' or 'good mind,' used for both males and females. Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali tradition of names expressing natural beauty and intellect.

nepali.name: Pratima
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the sacred idol and the divine image through which the unseen is made visible.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Pratima, meaning 'idol' or 'image.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali temple tradition in which sacred images are the focal point of daily devotion.

nepali.name: Shyam
nepali.meaning: Evokes the beautiful dark complexion of the divine, the blue-black color associated with the cosmic lord.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Shyama, meaning 'dark' or 'dark blue,' associated with Krishna and Shiva. Very common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali Vaishnava tradition of Krishna devotion.

nepali.name: Sarita
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the flowing river, that stream of water moving steadily and giving life to all around.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Sarita, meaning 'river' or 'flowing stream.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali cultural significance of rivers in agriculture and religious tradition.

nepali.name: Ganesh
nepali.meaning: Evokes the wise, gentle guardian of new beginnings and gatherings, remover of obstacles both seen and unseen.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name for the elephant-headed deity Ganesha. Common in Nepal; Ganesha is worshipped before all new endeavors in Nepali culture; Nepal has numerous important Ganesh temples.

nepali.name: Mina
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the fish, that creature moving freely in the deep, a sign of good fortune.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Mina, meaning 'fish.' Common in Nepal and across South Asia; the fish is an auspicious symbol in Hindu culture; reflects the Nepali tradition of nature names.

nepali.name: Ramji
nepali.meaning: Evokes a beloved figure of divine heroism and devotion, wrapped in affectionate warmth.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name combining Rama with the honorific -ji, meaning 'revered Rama.' Common in Nepal; reflects the deep Nepali devotion to Rama; the -ji suffix expresses the Nepali tradition of respectful affection.

nepali.name: Binita
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the humble and modest one whose quiet decorum makes them worthy of admiration.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Vinita, meaning 'humble' or 'modest.' Very common in Nepal; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Nepali value of humility as a personal virtue.

nepali.name: Tekraj
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the protective king, the sovereign who gives support and holds up what depends on them.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name combining tek (support/refuge) and raj (king). Used in Nepal; reflects the Nepali tradition of compound names expressing the ideal of a protective and supportive leadership.

nepali.name: Kamala
nepali.meaning: Carries the lotus and the goddess who sits upon it, pure beauty rising from the water.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Kamala, meaning 'lotus,' a name for Lakshmi. Very common in Nepal; reflects the deep reverence for Lakshmi in Nepal and the centrality of the lotus in Hindu symbolism.

nepali.name: Bishnu
nepali.meaning: Evokes a vast, sustaining presence that holds all things in harmonious balance.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name, the Nepali form of Vishnu meaning 'the all-pervading one.' Very common in Nepal; Vishnu is one of the principal deities of Nepal; reflects the deep Vaishnava tradition of the country.

nepali.name: Anjali
nepali.meaning: Speaks of the folded hands, the gesture of offering made when one presents something with reverence.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Anjali, meaning 'offering' or 'folded hands.' Common in Nepal and across India; reflects the Hindu tradition of anjali mudra as a gesture of devotional offering.

nepali.name: Dil Bahadur
nepali.meaning: Reflects the brave heart and the courageous soul who faces life's challenges with bold and fearless spirit.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male compound name combining dil (heart) with bahadur (brave). Common in Nepal and among Gurung, Tamang, and other hill communities; reflects the Nepali warrior tradition and the martial heritage of Nepal.

nepali.name: Kriti
nepali.meaning: Paints the image of the artistic creation and the work of art made through skilled and devoted effort.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Kriti, meaning 'creation' or 'work of art.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali artistic tradition including the renowned thangka painting, woodcarving, and dance heritage.

nepali.name: Sujan
nepali.meaning: Carries the quality of the good and wise person, someone of fine character whose goodness is complete.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male name derived from the Sanskrit Sujana, meaning 'good person' or 'wise.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali tradition of names expressing moral excellence and goodness of character.

nepali.name: Pratibha
nepali.meaning: Evokes the brilliance of talent and the luminous inner intelligence that sets one apart.
nepali.notes: A Nepali female name derived from the Sanskrit Pratibha, meaning 'talent' or 'brilliance.' Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali tradition of names expressing intellectual gifts and outstanding natural ability.

nepali.name: Bishnu Prasad
nepali.meaning: Speaks of divine grace and the blessed gift of an all-pervading protective presence.
nepali.notes: A Nepali male compound name combining Bishnu (Vishnu) with Prasad (divine gift/grace). Common in Nepal; reflects the Nepali tradition of compound names honoring the deity and expressing gratitude for a child.

sinhala.name: Dilini
sinhala.meaning: Carries the heartfelt quality of one whose spirit is warm and full of genuine feeling.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'of the heart.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular Sinhala female names; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist naming tradition.

sinhala.name: Nuwan
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the wisdom and intellectual clarity of one whose understanding runs deep and wide.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'wisdom' or 'knowledge.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Buddhist value of prajna (wisdom) in Sinhala culture.

sinhala.name: Chamari
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the yak-tail fan, an ancient symbol of royalty and auspicious ceremony.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Chamara, meaning 'a fly-whisk made of yak tail.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most recognized Sinhala female names.

sinhala.name: Kasun
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of golden light streaming down, warm and radiant in its generosity.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'golden' or 'of gold.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Sinhala tradition of golden imagery in naming.

sinhala.name: Nethmi
sinhala.meaning: Carries the beauty of the eyes, those luminous windows that reveal the soul within.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'of beautiful eyes.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular modern female names; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names celebrating physical beauty.

sinhala.name: Tharaka
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the star shining steadily in the night sky, constant and guiding in its quiet brilliance.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala name meaning 'star,' used for both males and females in Sri Lanka. Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of celestial names.

sinhala.name: Sachini
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of truth and the genuine authenticity of one who lives without pretense.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Sachina, meaning 'truth.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular female names in modern Sri Lanka.

sinhala.name: Chathura
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the clever and skillful mind that moves through problems with dexterity.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Chatura, meaning 'clever' or 'skillful.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala appreciation for intelligence as a virtue.

sinhala.name: Hasini
sinhala.meaning: Carries the smiling and joyful quality of one whose happiness shines outward for all to see.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Hasini, meaning 'smiling' or 'laughing.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular female names; reflects the tradition of names expressing joy.

sinhala.name: Lahiru
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the gentle breeze that moves pleasantly and refreshes everything in its path.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'pleasant breeze.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Sinhala tradition of nature names drawing on natural phenomena.

sinhala.name: Piyumi
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of love and the tender affection that flows between people who are deeply bound together.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'love' or 'affection.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular female names in modern Sri Lanka.

sinhala.name: Supun
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the dream and the beautiful visions that arise in the world of sleep.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'dream.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names drawing on the imagery of beautiful and meaningful dreams.

sinhala.name: Dulani
sinhala.meaning: Carries the swinging and flowing quality of something that moves with graceful, rhythmic ease.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'swinging' or 'flowing.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names drawing on imagery of graceful movement.

sinhala.name: Ravindu
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the sun and the moon combined, a celestial duality of radiance shining together.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name combining ravi (sun) and indu (moon). Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of compound celestial names drawing on Sanskrit imagery.

sinhala.name: Thilini
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the sesame seed, small but full of rich sustenance and hidden nourishment.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Tilini, related to the sesame plant. Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala cultural connection to agricultural imagery in naming.

sinhala.name: Dinesh
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the lord of the day, the sovereign ruler whose light defines all waking hours.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Dinesha, meaning 'lord of the day' or 'sun.' Very common in Sri Lanka and across South Asia; reflects the Sinhala solar naming tradition.

sinhala.name: Vishaka
sinhala.meaning: Carries the auspicious star cluster and the festival born under that luminous arrangement.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Vishakha, the name of an important Buddhist festival in Sri Lanka. Very common in Sri Lanka; the Vesak festival celebrates the Buddha's birth, enlightenment, and death.

sinhala.name: Asela
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the calm and untroubled quality of one who moves through the world with peaceful ease.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'peaceful' or 'calm.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Buddhist influence on Sinhala naming and the cultural value of tranquility.

sinhala.name: Sanduni
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the moonlight and the gentle silver radiance that falls softly on the sleeping world.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'moonlight.' Very common in Sri Lanka; one of the most popular female names in modern Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of lunar names.

sinhala.name: Malinda
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the garland of flowers worn as a crown, fragrant and beautifully arranged.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Malinda, meaning 'garland of flowers.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist tradition of flower offerings as an act of devotion.

sinhala.name: Hiruni
sinhala.meaning: Carries the golden quality of sunlight reflecting off precious metal, warm and luminous.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'golden.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of golden imagery in naming, associated with beauty and auspiciousness.

sinhala.name: Dhanushka
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the bow and the skilled archer who draws it with precision toward the distant mark.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Dhanushka, meaning 'small bow' or 'archer.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala martial heritage and the Sanskrit naming tradition.

sinhala.name: Pavithra
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the pure and sacred quality of something cleansed and consecrated for the divine.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Pavitra, meaning 'pure' or 'sacred.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist and Hindu naming traditions of purity.

sinhala.name: Nadun
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the gift and the generous bestowing of something precious to one who receives.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'given' or 'gift.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names expressing gratitude for the gift of a child.

sinhala.name: Isuri
sinhala.meaning: Carries the sovereign power and the divine authority of one who holds supreme command.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Ishwari, meaning 'goddess' or 'sovereign.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names drawing on divine feminine imagery.

sinhala.name: Thisara
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the swan, that graceful white bird moving serenely across the still water.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala name meaning 'swan,' used for both males and females in Sri Lanka. Common in Sri Lanka; the swan is a symbol of grace and purity in the Sanskrit and Sinhala literary traditions.

sinhala.name: Sewwandi
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the fragrant flower and the sweet scent that drifts from blossoms in the warm air.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name meaning 'fragrant flower.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist tradition of flower names and the use of flowers as offerings at temples.

sinhala.name: Amila
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the incomparable one who stands apart from all others in excellence.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala name derived from the Sanskrit Amila, meaning 'incomparable,' used for both males and females. Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names expressing unmatched quality.

sinhala.name: Buddhima
sinhala.meaning: Reflects the quality of wisdom and the deeply enlightened intelligence of one touched by understanding.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Buddhima, meaning 'wise' or 'intelligent.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Buddhist influence on Sinhala culture and the centrality of wisdom in Buddhist ethics.

sinhala.name: Chanaka
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the clever strategist and the sharp mind that sees through complexity to find the solution.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Chanakya. Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Indian classical tradition in Sinhala naming.

sinhala.name: Renuka
sinhala.meaning: Carries the one born of earth and dust, rooted in the ground that sustains all life.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Renuka, meaning 'born of dust' or 'earth.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the shared Sanskrit naming tradition across South Asian cultures.

sinhala.name: Ruwantha
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the golden light and the warm precious radiance that touches everything it falls upon.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name meaning 'golden light.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of compound names drawing on golden and luminous imagery.

sinhala.name: Nimesha
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the blink of an eye, that swift and fleeting moment barely perceptible.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Nimesha, meaning 'blink of an eye.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names drawing on subtle natural imagery.

sinhala.name: Sadeep
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the blazing lamp that burns with great brightness and illuminates a wide area around it.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Sadeep, meaning 'blazing lamp.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist tradition of the lamp as a symbol of wisdom and the Buddha's teaching.

sinhala.name: Yoshani
sinhala.meaning: Carries the young woman in the fullness of her youth, vibrant and full of fresh potential.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Yuvashani, meaning 'young woman.' Common in Sri Lanka; one of the distinctively Sinhala female names used in modern Sri Lanka.

sinhala.name: Malsha
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the gentle flower girl and the one who carries the fragrance of blossoms with her.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name related to the Sinhala word for flowers. Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala cultural tradition of flower imagery in naming; flowers are central to Buddhist temple offerings.

sinhala.name: Kavinda
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the poet king, one who combines the creative power of verse with sovereign authority.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Kavindra, meaning 'king of poets' or 'great poet.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala literary tradition and the high cultural status of poetry.

sinhala.name: Uthpala
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the blue lotus, that rare and precious flower blooming in the depths of still water.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Utpala, meaning 'blue lotus.' Common in Sri Lanka; the lotus is a central symbol in Buddhism representing purity and spiritual awakening.

sinhala.name: Chaminda
sinhala.meaning: Carries the moon's lord and the sovereign who governs the gentle light of the night sky.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Chandrinda, meaning 'lord of the moon.' Common in Sri Lanka; one of the most recognizable Sinhala male names.

sinhala.name: Nishadi
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the night sky's quality and the mysterious beauty of the dark hours filled with stars.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name related to the Sanskrit Nisha, meaning 'night.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names drawing on the imagery of the beautiful night.

sinhala.name: Hasitha
sinhala.meaning: Reflects the smiling and joyful quality of one whose happiness is constant and freely expressed.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala name derived from the Sanskrit Hasita, meaning 'smiling,' used for both males and females. Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala tradition of names celebrating happiness.

sinhala.name: Udara
sinhala.meaning: Paints the image of the noble and generous one whose magnanimity extends to all around them.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Udara, meaning 'noble' or 'magnanimous.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist tradition of generosity (dana) as a primary virtue.

sinhala.name: Damayanthi
sinhala.meaning: Evokes the one who subdues all others by virtue of extraordinary and captivating beauty.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name derived from the Sanskrit Damayanti, meaning 'one who subdues.' Common in Sri Lanka; associated with the legendary beauty Damayanti from the Mahabharata.

sinhala.name: Pradeep
sinhala.meaning: Speaks of the lamp shining forward and the light that illuminates the path ahead.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala male name derived from the Sanskrit Pradipa, meaning 'shining lamp.' Common in Sri Lanka; reflects the Sinhala Buddhist tradition of the lamp as a symbol of the Buddha's wisdom.

sinhala.name: Sewmini
sinhala.meaning: Carries the floral sweetness and the fragrant garden quality of someone surrounded by blossoms.
sinhala.notes: A Sinhala female name related to the Sinhala sew (flower). Common in Sri Lanka; one of the distinctively Sinhala female names; reflects the tradition of flower-names in Buddhist naming culture.

urdu-pakistan.name: Zainab
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the beauty and fragrance of a flowering plant adorned with its blossoms.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Zaynab. Very common in Pakistan; the name of the Prophet Muhammad's daughter and granddaughter, both deeply revered in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Hamza
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the lion-like strength and fierce protective courage of one who guards what they love.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Hamza. Common in Pakistan; associated with Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib, uncle of the Prophet Muhammad, venerated for his bravery in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Areeba
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the witty and intelligent mind that is quick and perceptive in understanding.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Ariba, meaning 'witty' or 'intelligent.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistani society.

urdu-pakistan.name: Bilal
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the moisture and freshness of water in the throat, quenching a deep thirst.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Bilal. Very common in Pakistan; associated with Bilal ibn Rabah, the first muezzin of Islam, deeply revered in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Mahnoor
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the moonlight and its gentle radiance falling over everything with soft silver brightness.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name combining mah (moon) and noor (light), meaning 'moonlight.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Urdu poetic tradition of lunar imagery.

urdu-pakistan.name: Omer
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes a life of long flourishing and the abundant prosperity of one richly blessed.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name, an Urdu form of Umar. Common in Pakistan; the name of the second Caliph of Islam, deeply respected in Sunni Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Ayesha
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the vibrant aliveness of one whose presence fills every space with energy and life.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name, an Urdu form of Aisha. One of the most common female names in Pakistan; the name of the Prophet Muhammad's wife, widely revered in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Shahzaib
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the prince adorned with beauty, the royal one whose appearance commands admiration.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name combining shah (king) and zaib (adornment/beauty). Very common in Pakistan; reflects the Persianate tradition of royal compound names in Urdu naming culture.

urdu-pakistan.name: Hira
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the diamond's hardness and dazzling brilliance, the most precious and enduring of all stones.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Hira, meaning 'diamond.' Also associated with the Cave of Hira where the Prophet received the first revelation. Very common in Pakistan.

urdu-pakistan.name: Usman
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the quality of a loyal companion whose steadfast friendship defines their character.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name, the Urdu form of Uthman. Very common in Pakistan; the name of the third Caliph of Islam, highly respected in Sunni Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Nimra
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the tigress and her powerful, graceful strength moving with sovereign ease.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Nimra, meaning 'tigress.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistani society.

urdu-pakistan.name: Talha
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Reflects the resilient tree of the arid land, surviving and bearing fruit where others cannot.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Talha, meaning 'a type of tree.' Common in Pakistan; associated with Talha ibn Ubaydullah, one of the ten companions promised paradise in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Momina
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the faithful believer whose conviction is complete and unwavering.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Mu'mina, meaning 'believer.' Very common in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic value of iman (faith) as the foundation of a Muslim woman's identity.

urdu-pakistan.name: Zubair
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the sharpness of steel, the strength and precision of one who is finely and firmly made.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Zubayr. Common in Pakistan; associated with Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, a companion of the Prophet Muhammad revered in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Laiba
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the celestial maiden of paradise, a figure of ethereal beauty dwelling in the highest realm.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Laiba, meaning 'angel of paradise.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistan.

urdu-pakistan.name: Farhan
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the happiness and the joyful delight of one whose life is filled with gladness.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Farhan, meaning 'happy' or 'joyful.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names expressing positive emotional states.

urdu-pakistan.name: Rida
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of divine contentment and the approval that rests over a life lived rightly.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Rida, meaning 'contentment' or 'divine approval.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic concept of rida (divine pleasure) as the ultimate spiritual goal.

urdu-pakistan.name: Asad
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the sovereign courage of the lion, that fearless beast moving through the world with power.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Asad, meaning 'lion.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of lion-names expressing strength and noble courage.

urdu-pakistan.name: Iqra
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the sacred command to read and to learn, the first imperative of divine revelation.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Iqra, meaning 'read.' Very common in Pakistan; the word iqra was the first word of the Quranic revelation, making it deeply significant in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Waleed
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the fresh arrival of a newborn, the first breath of a life just beginning.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Walid, meaning 'newborn.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Arabic tradition of names celebrating the moment of birth.

urdu-pakistan.name: Maham
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the moon at its fullest, round and luminous in the clear night sky.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Mah, meaning 'moon.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Urdu and Persian poetic tradition of lunar naming.

urdu-pakistan.name: Junaid
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the young warrior and the youthful courage of one who faces the world bravely.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Junayd, meaning 'young warrior.' Common in Pakistan; associated with the Sufi mystic Junayd al-Baghdadi, deeply revered in the Sufi tradition of Pakistan.

urdu-pakistan.name: Hoorain
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the beautiful dark-eyed maiden of paradise, a vision of divine and celestial loveliness.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Hur, meaning 'houris' or 'dark-eyed maidens of paradise.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic concept of paradise in naming culture.

urdu-pakistan.name: Saad
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of good fortune and the happiness of one whose life is blessed with prosperity.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Sa'd, meaning 'happiness' or 'good fortune.' Common in Pakistan; associated with several companions of the Prophet Muhammad revered in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Nisha
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Reflects the night and its quiet, mysterious beauty filled with stars and unspoken depth.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Sanskrit/Persian Nisha, meaning 'night.' Used in Pakistan; reflects the multilingual Urdu naming tradition drawing on both Persian and Sanskrit vocabulary.

urdu-pakistan.name: Waqar
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the dignified and composed person who carries themselves with quiet pride.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Waqar, meaning 'dignity' or 'composure.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Pakistani cultural value of dignified bearing and respectful composure.

urdu-pakistan.name: Minahil
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the spring of fresh water welling up from the earth, clean and endlessly giving.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Minhil, meaning 'spring of water.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistan.

urdu-pakistan.name: Rehan
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the sweet-smelling basil plant, fragrant and fresh, used in both cooking and ceremony.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Reyhan, meaning 'sweet-smelling basil.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Urdu poetic tradition of names evoking fragrant plants.

urdu-pakistan.name: Ramsha
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the beautiful garden and the one who moves through it with the grace of its flowers.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Persian Ramsha, meaning 'bunch of flowers' or 'beautiful.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistan.

urdu-pakistan.name: Faizan
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the overflowing grace and divine beneficence that pours from a generous sacred source.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Fayzan, meaning 'overflow of grace' or 'beneficence.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Sufi concept of divine grace flowing from the spiritual master.

urdu-pakistan.name: Khadija
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the noble-born woman of distinguished character and the highest standing.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Khadija. Very common in Pakistan; the name of the Prophet Muhammad's first wife, one of the four greatest women in Islamic tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Sheraz
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the noble lion's courage, the fearless dignity of one who commands respect.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Persian Shiraz, combining sher (lion) with az. Common in Pakistan; reflects the Persianate literary and naming tradition embedded in Urdu culture.

urdu-pakistan.name: Hadia
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the guide who leads others along the right path with gentle and trustworthy direction.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Hadia, meaning 'guide' or 'gift.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistan.

urdu-pakistan.name: Muneeb
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Reflects the one who turns back in repentance toward the divine, returning with a sincere heart.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Muneeb, meaning 'one who turns to God.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic value of tawba (repentance) as a spiritual virtue.

urdu-pakistan.name: Zara
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the bright and luminous flower blooming in the morning light.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Zahra, meaning 'flower' or 'luminous.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most fashionable female names in modern Pakistani society.

urdu-pakistan.name: Kamran
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the successful and fortunate one who achieves everything they set their heart upon.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Persian Kamran, meaning 'successful' or 'prosperous.' Very common in Pakistan; reflects the Persianate influence on Urdu naming tradition.

urdu-pakistan.name: Sana
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the brilliant radiance of light shining upward, luminous and striking in its beauty.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Sana', meaning 'radiance' or 'brilliance.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names across the country.

urdu-pakistan.name: Hasnain
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the two who are beautiful and good, a name celebrating blessed duality.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic dual of Hasan, meaning 'the two who are good.' Common in Pakistan; associated with the grandsons of the Prophet Muhammad, Hasan and Husayn.

urdu-pakistan.name: Nadia
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the gentle caller whose voice reaches out and invites others near.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name used in Pakistan. Reflects the cosmopolitan Urdu naming tradition; common in urban Pakistan among both traditional and modern families.

urdu-pakistan.name: Umar
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes a life of long prosperity and flourishing abundance across many fruitful years.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name, the Urdu form of Umar. One of the most common male names in Pakistan; the second Caliph of Islam; deeply respected across the Sunni Muslim world.

urdu-pakistan.name: Tehreem
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Carries the quality of being held sacred and treated with the reverence due to what is holy.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic Tahrim, meaning 'sacred' or 'revered.' Common in Pakistan; one of the distinctively Pakistani female names used in modern Pakistani society.

urdu-pakistan.name: Zaid
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Evokes the growth and abundance of something that increases and multiplies beyond expectation.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Arabic Zayd, meaning 'growth' or 'increase.' Common in Pakistan; associated with Zayd ibn Haritha, a beloved companion of the Prophet Muhammad.

urdu-pakistan.name: Sehar
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Speaks of the magical stillness of the hours just before dawn when the world holds its breath.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Arabic/Persian Sehar, meaning 'dawn' or 'early morning.' Common in Pakistan; reflects the Urdu poetic tradition of naming women for the beautiful quality of dawn.

urdu-pakistan.name: Arslan
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Reflects the lion's sovereign strength and the fearless courage of one who commands respect.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani male name derived from the Turkic Arslan, meaning 'lion.' Common in Pakistan, particularly in Punjab and among families of Turkic heritage; reflects the Mughal and Turkic influence on Pakistani naming.

urdu-pakistan.name: Mehwish
urdu-pakistan.meaning: Paints the image of the moon at its full and luminous peak, flooding the night with silver beauty.
urdu-pakistan.notes: A Pakistani female name derived from the Persian Mah, meaning 'moonlike' or 'resembling the moon.' Very common in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistani society.

pashto.name: Zarghona
pashto.meaning: Carries the rich green of growing things, the vibrant color of fertile land and living nature.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'green' or 'lush.' Very common in Afghanistan and among Pashtun communities in Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in Pashto-speaking communities.

pashto.name: Nisar
pashto.meaning: Evokes the act of sacrificing and giving oneself completely for what one holds most dear.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Nisar, meaning 'sacrifice' or 'to give oneself.' Common among Pashtuns in Afghanistan and Pakistan; reflects the Islamic value of sacrifice.

pashto.name: Shukria
pashto.meaning: Speaks of gratitude and the thankfulness poured out toward the source of every blessing.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Arabic Shukr, meaning 'gratitude.' Very common in Afghanistan; one of the most widely used female names among Pashtun communities.

pashto.name: Daud
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the beloved one chosen by the divine for a special and lasting purpose.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name, the Pashto form of Dawud (David). Very common in Afghanistan and among Pashtun communities; reflects the Islamic tradition of prophetic naming.

pashto.name: Benazir
pashto.meaning: Carries the incomparable quality of one who stands apart from all others without equal.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Bina Nazir, meaning 'incomparable.' Common in Afghanistan and Pakistan among Pashtun families; reflects the Persian influence on Pashto naming.

pashto.name: Sohail
pashto.meaning: Evokes the bright star Canopus, visible and brilliant, used by navigators to find their way.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Suhayl, the name of the star Canopus. Common in Afghanistan and among Pashtuns in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic astronomical naming tradition.

pashto.name: Freshta
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the angel, that radiant and pure messenger moving between the divine and earthly worlds.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'angel.' Very common in Afghanistan; one of the most popular female names in Pashto-speaking communities; a distinctly Pashto name.

pashto.name: Haroon
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the mountain and the exalted high place where vision is clear and wide.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name, the Pashto form of Aaron. Very common in Afghanistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of prophetic naming that is central to Pashtun naming culture.

pashto.name: Mursal
pashto.meaning: Carries the messenger who has been sent on a divine mission to deliver truth to those who await it.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Arabic Mursal, meaning 'messenger' or 'sent one.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of prophetic imagery in Pashto female naming.

pashto.name: Yousuf
pashto.meaning: Evokes the grace and beauty of one specially added to increase the joy of those around them.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name, the Pashto form of Yusuf (Joseph). Very common in Afghanistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of naming children after prophets mentioned in the Quran.

pashto.name: Malali
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the woman of the battlefield, the one whose courage turns the tide of great struggles.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'grief' or associated with the legendary Pashtun heroine Malalai of Maiwand. Common in Afghanistan; carries deep cultural significance as a symbol of Pashtun female courage.

pashto.name: Gul
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the flower in full bloom, its petals open and its fragrance filling the air.
pashto.notes: A Pashto name meaning 'flower' or 'rose,' used for both males and females in Afghanistan and Pakistan. Very common; one of the most distinctively Pashto names; the flower holds deep poetic significance.

pashto.name: Nargis
pashto.meaning: Reflects the narcissus flower, pale and fragrant, the first to appear after the cold winter.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Nargis, meaning 'narcissus flower.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Persian literary influence on Pashto culture and naming traditions.

pashto.name: Hayatullah
pashto.meaning: Evokes the life given by God, the existence that flows from the divine source of all being.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'life of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun Islamic naming tradition of compound names expressing divine gifts.

pashto.name: Roya
pashto.meaning: Carries the dream and the beautiful visions seen in sleep, shimmering with hidden meaning.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Roya, meaning 'dream' or 'vision.' Very common in Afghanistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Persian influence on Pashto culture.

pashto.name: Khalid
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the eternal one who endures forever, unchanged and permanent across all time.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Khalid, meaning 'eternal.' Very common in Afghanistan and among Pashtuns in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition.

pashto.name: Spogmai
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the moonlight, that soft silver illumination spreading over the quiet landscape.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'moonlight.' Common in Afghanistan; one of the distinctively Pashto female names reflecting the tradition of lunar imagery native to Pashto poetry and culture.

pashto.name: Naweed
pashto.meaning: Evokes the good news and glad tidings whose arrival lifts the spirits of those who receive it.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Persian Navid, meaning 'good news' or 'herald.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Persian influence on Pashto naming and the value of hopeful tidings.

pashto.name: Laila
pashto.meaning: Carries the mystery and deep beauty of the night, dark and still and full of unspoken longing.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Arabic Layla, meaning 'night.' Very common in Afghanistan; reflects the classical Arabic and Persian love poetry tradition deeply embedded in Pashtun culture.

pashto.name: Bashir
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the bringer of good news, the one whose coming signals joyful tidings for all.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Bashir, meaning 'bringer of good news.' Very common in Afghanistan and among Pashtuns in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition.

pashto.name: Palwasha
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the moonlight scattered across water, shimmering and beautiful.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'scattered moonlight' or 'shimmer of moonlight.' Common in Afghanistan; one of the distinctively Pashto female names capturing the poetic nature of Pashto language.

pashto.name: Rahmatullah
pashto.meaning: Evokes the divine mercy, the compassion of the creator poured out over all of creation.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'mercy of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun Islamic naming tradition of compound names expressing divine attributes.

pashto.name: Wazhma
pashto.meaning: Carries the glowing ember and the warmth of a fire that continues to shine after the flames subside.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'glowing ember' or 'shine.' Common in Afghanistan; one of the distinctively Pashto female names that reflects the indigenous naming tradition of the Pashto language.

pashto.name: Tariq
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the morning star appearing just before dawn, brilliant and solitary in the dark sky.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Tariq, meaning 'morning star.' Common in Afghanistan and among Pashtuns in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic astronomical naming tradition.

pashto.name: Zarlasht
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of pure gold, refined and gleaming, the most precious metal at its finest.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'pure gold.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashto tradition of golden imagery in naming and the value of gold in Pashtun culture.

pashto.name: Ihsanullah
pashto.meaning: Carries the divine excellence and the perfect goodness that comes from God's own perfection.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'excellence of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun tradition of compound names honoring divine attributes; ihsan is one of the highest Islamic spiritual concepts.

pashto.name: Nadia
pashto.meaning: Evokes the tender calling of a voice that reaches out and draws others toward something beautiful.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name used in Afghanistan. Reflects the cosmopolitan naming tradition in Afghan urban centers; common among educated Pashtun families in Kabul and other cities.

pashto.name: Sharifullah
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the nobility of God, the elevated dignity that flows from the divine nature itself.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'nobility of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun Islamic tradition of compound names expressing divine attributes.

pashto.name: Gulalai
pashto.meaning: Reflects the rosy flower and the warm pink beauty of a blossom in its finest moment.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'rose-colored' or 'rosy.' Common in Afghanistan and among Pashtun communities; one of the distinctively Pashto female names.

pashto.name: Obaidullah
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the devoted servant of God, the humble one who gives themselves to the divine.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'small servant of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun Islamic tradition of names expressing humble servitude before the divine.

pashto.name: Samira
pashto.meaning: Carries the warmth of evening company, the conversation that flows freely under the stars.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Arabic Samira, meaning 'evening companion.' Common in Afghanistan; used widely in urban Afghan Pashtun communities.

pashto.name: Zafar
pashto.meaning: Evokes the victory and triumph of one who prevails against all the forces opposed to them.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Zafar, meaning 'victory' or 'triumph.' Common in Afghanistan and among Pashtuns in Pakistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of victory-names.

pashto.name: Breshna
pashto.meaning: Speaks of lightning and the brilliant flash that illuminates the entire sky in an instant.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name meaning 'lightning' or 'electricity.' Common in Afghanistan; one of the most distinctively Pashto female names; reflects the indigenous naming tradition of the Pashto language.

pashto.name: Noorullah
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the divine light, the radiance that flows directly from God's own being.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'light of God.' Very common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun Islamic naming tradition of compound names combining nur (light) with Allah.

pashto.name: Gulsanga
pashto.meaning: Carries the flower of stone, the rare blossom that grows in rocky places with persistent beauty.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name combining gul (flower) and sanga (stone), meaning 'stone flower.' Used in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashto tradition of compound flower-names with unexpected natural imagery.

pashto.name: Waliullah
pashto.meaning: Evokes the friend and guardian of God, one who has achieved the highest spiritual closeness to the divine.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'friend of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Sufi Islamic tradition in Pashtun culture where the concept of the wali (saint) holds special significance.

pashto.name: Shirin
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the sweetness and delightful pleasantness of something that dissolves like honey.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Shirin, meaning 'sweet.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Persian literary influence through the classical story of Shirin, deeply embedded in Pashto culture.

pashto.name: Ejaz
pashto.meaning: Reflects the miraculous quality and the divine wonder that stands beyond all ordinary explanation.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Ijaz, meaning 'miracle' or 'inimitability.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Islamic concept of the miracle of the Quran as linguistically inimitable.

pashto.name: Shaista
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the polished and refined person who is gentle, pleasant, and well-mannered.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Shaista, meaning 'refined' or 'pleasant.' Very common in Afghanistan and Pakistan; reflects the Persian cultural influence on Pashto naming traditions.

pashto.name: Nasratullah
pashto.meaning: Carries the divine help and the support of God that comes to those who are in need.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name meaning 'help of God.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Pashtun Islamic tradition of names expressing divine assistance and support.

pashto.name: Parisa
pashto.meaning: Evokes the fairy-like beauty of one who seems to belong to another, more ethereal world.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Parisa, meaning 'fairy-faced' or 'like a fairy.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the Persian literary tradition of pari (fairy) names.

pashto.name: Ghafor
pashto.meaning: Speaks of the forgiving one who pardons abundantly and whose mercy knows no limit.
pashto.notes: A Pashto male name derived from the Arabic Ghafur, meaning 'the all-forgiving.' Common in Afghanistan; also used as a short form of Abdulghafoor; reflects the Islamic divine attribute of forgiveness.

pashto.name: Mehria
pashto.meaning: Paints the image of the sunlight, that warm and life-giving radiance that defines the day.
pashto.notes: A Pashto female name derived from the Persian Mehr, meaning 'sun' or 'love.' Common in Afghanistan; reflects the ancient Persian Zoroastrian tradition of solar naming preserved in Pashto culture.

sindhi.name: Roshni
sindhi.meaning: Carries the brightness of light illuminating the darkness, bringing clarity where shadows gathered.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Persian Roshni, meaning 'light' or 'brightness.' Very common in Sindh, Pakistan; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Persian influence on Sindhi culture.

sindhi.name: Zulfiqar
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the legendary double-bladed sword, the weapon of extraordinary power and historic renown.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Dhulfiqar, the name of Imam Ali's famous sword. Common in Sindh; reflects the deep Shia Islamic influence in Sindhi culture and the veneration of Imam Ali.

sindhi.name: Nadia
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the gentle invitation and the caller whose voice draws others toward something good.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name used widely in Sindh, Pakistan. Reflects the cosmopolitan Sindhi naming tradition that combines Arabic, Persian, and modern influences in urban Sindh.

sindhi.name: Asif
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of the sorrowful one who laments with deep and earnest feeling.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Asif, meaning 'sorrowful' or associated with the vizier Asif in the story of Solomon. Common in Sindh and across Pakistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition.

sindhi.name: Hina
sindhi.meaning: Carries the fragrance of henna, that reddish plant whose scent fills the air at celebrations.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name meaning 'henna.' Very common in Sindh; henna is deeply central to Sindhi wedding and festival culture; reflects the Sindhi tradition of names connected to cultural practices.

sindhi.name: Shahid
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the witness who bears testimony and the martyr who gave the ultimate testimony with their life.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Shahid, meaning 'witness' or 'martyr.' Common in Sindh and across Pakistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of martyrdom as the highest form of witness.

sindhi.name: Mehwish
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the moon-like radiance, the beauty that glows with the silver light of the night.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Persian Mah, meaning 'moonlike.' Very common in Sindh and Pakistan; one of the most popular female names in modern Pakistani society.

sindhi.name: Naseem
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of the gentle breeze that moves through on warm days, cooling and refreshing.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi name derived from the Arabic Nasim, meaning 'gentle breeze,' used for both males and females. Common in Sindh; reflects the Sindhi poetic tradition of natural imagery in naming.

sindhi.name: Ayesha
sindhi.meaning: Reflects the vibrant aliveness of one whose presence fills every space with lively energy.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Aisha. Very common in Sindh; the name of the Prophet Muhammad's wife, widely revered across all Muslim communities in Sindh.

sindhi.name: Khalid
sindhi.meaning: Carries the eternal quality of one who remains unchanged and permanent across all time.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Khalid, meaning 'eternal.' Very common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic naming tradition prevalent throughout Sindh.

sindhi.name: Rubina
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the ruby, deep red and glowing, precious and rare in its vivid and enduring beauty.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Persian Rubin, meaning 'ruby.' Very common in Sindh; reflects the Persian influence on Sindhi culture; one of the most popular female names in the region.

sindhi.name: Imran
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the flourishing civilization and the cultivated prosperity of an abundant and organized society.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Imran, meaning 'civilization' or 'flourishing.' Common in Sindh; the name of the father of Maryam (Mary) in Islamic tradition.

sindhi.name: Sobia
sindhi.meaning: Carries the quality of praise and the celebrated reputation of one spoken of with admiration.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Subiyya, meaning 'praiseworthy.' Common in Sindh; one of the popular female names in the region; reflects the Islamic tradition of praise-names.

sindhi.name: Adil
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of the just and equitable one who deals fairly with all without discrimination.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Adil, meaning 'just' or 'fair.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic value of justice as one of the fundamental virtues.

sindhi.name: Noorjehan
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the light of the world, a radiance so vast and powerful it illuminates the entire universe.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name combining noor (light) and jahan (world), meaning 'light of the world.' Used in Sindh; reflects the Persianate Mughal naming tradition deeply embedded in Sindhi culture.

sindhi.name: Pervez
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the victorious and the one who has triumphed in all their endeavors.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Persian Parviz, meaning 'victorious.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Persian literary influence; associated with the Sassanid king Khusrau Parviz.

sindhi.name: Shazia
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of the rare and unusual one who stands apart from the ordinary in every way.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Shaziya, meaning 'rare' or 'unusual.' Very common in Sindh and Pakistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names expressing exceptional quality.

sindhi.name: Muneer
sindhi.meaning: Carries the luminous quality of one who shines brightly outward and makes everything around them bright.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Munir, meaning 'luminous' or 'radiant.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic tradition of light-names expressing divine illumination.

sindhi.name: Saima
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the one who fasts, the devoted person who disciplines themselves with spiritual abstinence.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Sa'ima, meaning 'fasting woman.' Common in Sindh and Pakistan; reflects the Islamic tradition of naming for spiritual practices like fasting.

sindhi.name: Riaz
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the garden and the place of cultivation where beautiful things grow under careful tending.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Riyad, meaning 'gardens.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic poetic tradition of garden imagery and the Sufi metaphor of the garden as paradise.

sindhi.name: Sundas
sindhi.meaning: Carries the silken fabric of paradise, that smooth and precious cloth woven of divine threads.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Sundus, meaning 'fine silk' or 'brocade of paradise.' Very common in Sindh; reflects the Quranic reference to the garments of paradise.

sindhi.name: Jameel
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of beauty in its fullest sense, a quality that is beautiful throughout and without flaw.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Jamil, meaning 'beautiful.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic naming tradition of names expressing beauty as both physical and spiritual virtue.

sindhi.name: Rabia
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the spring season in all its freshness, when new growth appears and the world renews.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Rabi, meaning 'spring.' Very common in Sindh; associated with the great Sufi mystic Rabia al-Adawiyya; reflects the significant Sufi tradition in Sindh.

sindhi.name: Sarwar
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the chief and master, the one who stands at the head and guides all those beneath.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Persian Sarvar, meaning 'chief' or 'master.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Persianate influence on Sindhi naming tradition.

sindhi.name: Mariam
sindhi.meaning: Carries the graceful purity and spiritual depth of a woman held in the highest reverence.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Maryam. Common in Sindh; the Quranic figure of Maryam (Mary) is deeply revered in Islam; one of the four greatest women in Islamic tradition.

sindhi.name: Nawaz
sindhi.meaning: Reflects the one who shows kindness and bestows favor, the generous patron who lifts others up.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Persian Nawaz, meaning 'kind' or 'one who favors.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Persian influence on Sindhi culture and the tradition of Sufi generous patronage.

sindhi.name: Komal
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of the soft and tender quality of something delicate that yields with gentle grace.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Sanskrit Komala, meaning 'soft' or 'tender.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Sanskrit influence on Sindhi culture through the ancient Hindu heritage of Sindh.

sindhi.name: Ghulam
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the devoted servant and the humble one who gives themselves fully to their master.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Ghulam, meaning 'servant' or 'youth.' Common in Sindh; often the first element of compound names like Ghulam Ali; reflects the Islamic naming tradition.

sindhi.name: Muskan
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the smile that spreads across a face, gentle and irresistible in its warming brightness.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Urdu Muskaan, meaning 'smile.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Urdu influence on Sindhi naming in urban areas.

sindhi.name: Niaz
sindhi.meaning: Carries the devotion and the supplication, the earnest petition offered with complete humility.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Persian Niaz, meaning 'devotion' or 'supplication.' Very common in Sindh; reflects the deep Sufi tradition of Sindh where niaz (offering) holds special spiritual significance.

sindhi.name: Sana
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of brilliant radiance shining upward, luminous and striking in its clarity.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Sana', meaning 'radiance' or 'brilliance.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Arabic influence on Sindhi naming through Islamic tradition.

sindhi.name: Basheer
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the bringer of good news whose coming signals joyful tidings for all who receive them.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Bashir, meaning 'bringer of good news.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic naming tradition prevalent throughout Sindh.

sindhi.name: Zubaida
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the cream and the finest part of milk, the most pure and concentrated essence.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Zubaida, meaning 'cream of milk' or 'the best.' Common in Sindh; associated with Zubayda, the wife of the Abbasid caliph Harun al-Rashid.

sindhi.name: Aziz
sindhi.meaning: Carries the mighty and precious quality of one who is both powerful and deeply valued.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Aziz, meaning 'mighty' or 'precious.' Common in Sindh and across Pakistan; reflects the Islamic naming tradition and one of the ninety-nine names of God.

sindhi.name: Nazia
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of the proud and graceful one who carries themselves with quiet dignity.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Nazia, meaning 'proud' or 'graceful.' Very common in Sindh; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Sindhi tradition of names expressing dignified character.

sindhi.name: Altaf
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the kindness and the gentle thoughtfulness of one whose manner is refined and considerate.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Altaf, meaning 'kindness' or 'gentleness.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Sindhi cultural tradition of valuing refined and considerate behavior.

sindhi.name: Shama
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the candle flame whose light burns steadily and draws everything toward its warmth.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Persian Shama, meaning 'candle.' Common in Sindh; the candle is a central metaphor in Sindhi Sufi poetry, particularly in the tradition associated with Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai.

sindhi.name: Rashid
sindhi.meaning: Reflects the rightly guided one who knows the true path and walks it with faithful commitment.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Rashid, meaning 'rightly guided.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic naming tradition prevalent throughout Sindhi Muslim communities.

sindhi.name: Mehak
sindhi.meaning: Carries the fragrance that drifts on the air, the sweet scent that announces beauty's presence.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Persian Mehak, meaning 'fragrance' or 'sweet smell.' Very common in Sindh; reflects the Persian influence on Sindhi culture through the Sufi poetic tradition.

sindhi.name: Sajid
sindhi.meaning: Paints the image of one prostrating in worship, the devout body bowed in complete submission.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Sajid, meaning 'one who prostrates in worship.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic practice of sujud (prostration) as the deepest act of prayer.

sindhi.name: Laila
sindhi.meaning: Evokes the night in all its mysterious beauty, deep and dark and full of unspoken longing.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Arabic Layla, meaning 'night.' Very common in Sindh; immortalized in the classical story of Laila and Majnun which is deeply embedded in Sindhi literary culture.

sindhi.name: Zafar
sindhi.meaning: Speaks of the victory and the triumph of one who prevails against all that opposes them.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Zafar, meaning 'victory.' Common in Sindh; reflects the Islamic tradition of victory-names expressing triumph through faith.

sindhi.name: Anila
sindhi.meaning: Carries the wind and the free-moving quality of the breeze that flows through every open space.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi female name derived from the Sanskrit Anila, meaning 'wind.' Used in Sindh; reflects the ancient Hindu heritage of Sindh preserved in some naming traditions alongside the dominant Islamic culture.

sindhi.name: Hafeez
sindhi.meaning: Reflects the guardian and preserver, the one who protects and keeps safe what has been entrusted.
sindhi.notes: A Sindhi male name derived from the Arabic Hafiz, meaning 'guardian' or 'preserver.' Common in Sindh; one of the ninety-nine names of God in Islam; reflects the deep Islamic identity of Sindhi culture.

javanese.name: Dewi
javanese.meaning: Carries the divine feminine quality of a goddess, one whose nature is celestial and radiant.
javanese.notes: A Javanese female name derived from the Sanskrit Devi, meaning 'goddess.' Very common in Java, Indonesia; one of the most widely used female names in Javanese culture; reflects the Hindu-Buddhist heritage.

javanese.name: Bagus
javanese.meaning: Evokes the handsome and fine quality of something good in every dimension of its being.
javanese.notes: A Javanese male name meaning 'handsome' or 'good.' Very common in Java; used both as a title for Javanese nobility and as a given name; reflects the Javanese cultural tradition of names expressing physical virtue.

javanese.name: Sari
javanese.meaning: Speaks of the essence and the finest distillation of something, its most concentrated and pure form.
javanese.notes: A Javanese female name derived from the Sanskrit Sara, meaning 'essence.' Very common in Java, Indonesia; one of the most widely used female names; reflects the Sanskrit influence on Javanese culture.

javanese.name: Budi
javanese.meaning: Paints the image of the awakened intellect, the mind that has blossomed into true understanding.
javanese.notes: A Javanese male name derived from the Sanskrit Buddha, meaning 'intellect' or 'understanding.' Very common in Java; reflects the Javanese cultural ideal of refined intelligence rooted in Buddhist-Hindu tradition.

javanese.name: Indah
javanese.meaning: Carries the beautiful quality of something that is lovely and pleasing in every aspect.
javanese.notes: A Javanese female name meaning 'beautiful.' Very common in Java and across Indonesia; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Javanese tradition of names expressing aesthetic beauty.

javanese.name: Suryo
javanese.meaning: Evokes the sun and its radiant energy, the great celestial force that sustains all living things.
javanese.notes: A Javanese male name derived from the Sanskrit Surya, meaning 'sun.' Very common in Java; reflects the Javanese Hindu-Buddhist tradition and the cultural significance of the sun in Javanese cosmology.

javanese.name: Ayu
javanese.meaning: Speaks of the beautiful and graceful feminine quality that radiates naturally from one's being.
