thai.meaning: Paints the image of the daughter of wisdom, one born into learning and noble understanding.
thai.notes: A Thai female name derived from the Sanskrit Thida, meaning 'daughter of the wise.' Common in Thailand; reflects the Pali and Sanskrit influence on Thai female naming through Buddhism.

thai.name: Peerawat
thai.meaning: Evokes the brave and decisive one whose courage leads to significant and lasting achievement.
thai.notes: A Thai male name combining peera (brave) and wat (significant). Common in Thailand; reflects the Thai cultural tradition of names combining courage with meaningful achievement.

thai.name: Kanchana
thai.meaning: Speaks of the golden one whose warm and precious nature radiates like refined metal.
thai.notes: A Thai female name derived from the Sanskrit Kanchana, meaning 'golden.' Common in Thailand; reflects the Buddhist significance of gold as the color of merit and excellence.

thai.name: Thanakrit
thai.meaning: Carries the wealth of achievement and the prosperity that comes from worthy accomplishment.
thai.notes: A Thai male name combining thana (wealth) and krit (achievement). Common in Thailand; reflects the Thai Buddhist tradition of names combining material and spiritual achievement.

thai.name: Suchada
thai.meaning: Paints the image of the good daughter of excellence, one born into virtue and fine character.
thai.notes: A Thai female name derived from the Sanskrit Suchada, meaning 'good daughter.' Common in Thailand; reflects the Pali and Sanskrit influence on Thai naming through Theravada Buddhist tradition.

thai.name: Parinya
thai.meaning: Evokes the deep knowledge and the comprehensive wisdom that comes from thorough learning.
thai.notes: A Thai male name derived from the Sanskrit Parinna, meaning 'complete knowledge.' Common in Thailand; reflects the Buddhist concept of parinya (comprehensive knowledge) in Thai naming.

thai.name: Wanlaya
thai.meaning: Speaks of the beautiful forest day and the lovely quality of light through the trees.
thai.notes: A Thai female name combining wan (day) and laya (forest/beautiful). Common in Thailand; reflects the Thai tradition of compound names drawing on natural imagery.

thai.name: Pakphum
thai.meaning: Carries the protective earth that shelters and sustains all things that grow upon it.
thai.notes: A Thai male name combining pak (protect) and phum (earth/ground). Common in Thailand; reflects the Thai cultural tradition of earth-protection compound names.

thai.name: Pornthip
thai.meaning: Paints the image of the heavenly blessing and the divine favor that falls from celestial realms.
thai.notes: A Thai female name combining porn (blessing) and tip (heaven). Very common in Thailand; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Thai Buddhist tradition of heavenly blessing names.

vietnamese.name: Cam
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the sweet orange fruit and the warm color of its generous and nourishing abundance.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female name meaning 'sweet orange' or 'orange color.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of names evoking the natural sweetness of fruits and warm colors.

vietnamese.name: Tien
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the celestial fairy whose beauty and grace belong to a higher and ethereal world.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female name meaning 'celestial' or 'fairy.' Very common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural tradition of fairy and celestial imagery in female naming.

vietnamese.name: Cuong
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the powerful and mighty quality of one whose force is solid and enduring.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'strong' or 'powerful.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural tradition of strength names for men.

vietnamese.name: Ngoc Anh
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the peaceful jade gem, the precious stone of purity resting in quiet and serene clarity.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Ngoc (jade) and Anh (peaceful). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names combining gem imagery with peaceful qualities.

vietnamese.name: Phong
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the wind moving freely through the world, refreshing and impossible to hold.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'wind' or 'style.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of nature names connecting with the free movement of the wind.

vietnamese.name: Hue
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the lily flower and its graceful blooming, delicate and pure in its seasonal beauty.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female name meaning 'lily' or 'fragrant.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of floral names for women celebrating natural beauty.

vietnamese.name: Hung Thinh
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the flourishing prosperity of one whose brave and successful life brings abundant blessing.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male compound name combining Hung (heroic) and Thinh (prosperous). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names expressing brave prosperity.

vietnamese.name: Bao Yen
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the precious and tender swallow, a treasure of gentle beauty that announces the spring.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Bao (precious) and Yen (swallow). Common in Vietnam; the swallow is a symbol of spring and renewal in Vietnamese poetic tradition.

vietnamese.name: Truc
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of bamboo standing straight and strong, bending without breaking in any storm.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female name meaning 'bamboo.' Common in Vietnam; bamboo is the most powerful symbol of resilience and uprightness in Vietnamese culture.

vietnamese.name: Kien
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the firm and steadfast determination of one who holds their course without wavering.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'firm' or 'steadfast.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese Confucian tradition of names expressing moral steadfastness.

vietnamese.name: Thu Hien
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the gentle autumn, that reflective season whose beauty comes from its quiet depth.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Thu (autumn) and Hien (gentle). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of seasonal compound names expressing emotional qualities.

vietnamese.name: Nhat
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the sun and the single brilliant source that gives light and warmth to everything.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese name meaning 'sun' or 'first,' used for both males and females. Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of solar names expressing primacy and illumination.

vietnamese.name: Tuyen Anh
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the peaceful light ray, a beam that travels gently and without disturbance.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Tuyen (light ray) and Anh (peaceful). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names joining light with peaceful qualities.

vietnamese.name: Bach
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the pure white quality of something immaculate, pristine and untouched by shadow.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese name meaning 'white' or 'pure,' used for both males and females. Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of color names expressing moral purity.

vietnamese.name: Phu
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the wealthy and prosperous one whose abundance comes from diligent and worthy effort.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'wealthy' or 'prosperous.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural aspiration for material and spiritual prosperity.

vietnamese.name: Kim Oanh
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the golden oriole, that brilliant yellow bird whose song and color enchant all who hear.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Kim (gold) and Oanh (oriole bird). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese poetic tradition of combining precious metals with bird imagery.

vietnamese.name: Tho Xuan
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the long spring life, the enduring freshness of one who lives in eternal youth.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Tho (longevity) and Xuan (spring). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names expressing the wish for long and fresh life.

vietnamese.name: Thanh Binh
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the peaceful purity and the serene clarity of a life undisturbed by conflict.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese name combining Thanh (pure) and Binh (peaceful), used for both males and females. Common in Vietnam; one of the most popular compound names reflecting Vietnamese aspirations for peaceful life.

vietnamese.name: Quynh
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the jade flower blooming at night, that rare and beautiful blossom of the dark hours.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female name meaning 'jade-like flower' or a flower that blooms at night. Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese poetic tradition of names evoking rare and mysterious beauty.

vietnamese.name: Duc Anh
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the peaceful virtue and the harmonious excellence of one who lives with moral grace.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male compound name combining Duc (virtue) and Anh (peaceful). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Confucian tradition of peaceful virtue as the highest ideal.

vietnamese.name: Thu Thuy
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the autumn water, that clear and reflective element in the season of beauty.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Thu (autumn) and Thuy (water). Common in Vietnam; autumn and water are paired in Vietnamese poetic tradition as symbols of reflective beauty.

vietnamese.name: Manh
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the strong and vigorous quality of one who moves through the world with physical power.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'strong' or 'vigorous.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural tradition of strength names expressing masculine vitality.

vietnamese.name: Hong Nhung
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the crimson velvet rose, the red flower whose rich texture makes it supremely beautiful.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Hong (rose/pink) and Nhung (velvet). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese poetic tradition of flower names with sensory texture imagery.

vietnamese.name: Phi
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the winged and soaring quality of one who rises above the ordinary in free flight.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese name meaning 'to fly' or 'nimble,' used for both males and females. Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of flight names expressing freedom and elevation.

vietnamese.name: Tuong
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the one who dreams and envisions what lies beyond the present moment.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'to imagine' or 'vision.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural tradition of thoughtful, visionary naming.

vietnamese.name: Lan Anh
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the peaceful orchid, that elegant flower whose quiet beauty endures in gentle solitude.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Lan (orchid) and Anh (peaceful). Very common in Vietnam; orchid and peace are two of the most cherished elements in Vietnamese naming culture.

vietnamese.name: Loc
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the good fortune and the auspicious prosperity that blesses a life from its beginning.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'good fortune' or 'prosperity.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural aspiration for auspicious luck in all endeavors.

vietnamese.name: Phuoc
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the happiness and the blessed good fortune that comes from living a virtuous life.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'happiness' or 'good fortune.' Common in Vietnam, especially in the south; reflects the Vietnamese cultural tradition of fortune-names.

vietnamese.name: Nhu Quynh
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the jade-like night flower, a blossom as smooth and precious as fine stone.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Nhu (gentle/smooth as jade) and Quynh (night-blooming flower). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese poetic tradition of compound flower names.

vietnamese.name: Hung Vuong
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the prosperous and flourishing royal one whose abundance and honor are beyond measure.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male compound name combining Hung (prosperous) and Vuong (king/royal). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of names expressing noble prosperity.

vietnamese.name: Bao Chau
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the precious pearl, the rare gem of great beauty formed in patience and held as treasure.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Bao (precious) and Chau (pearl). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names celebrating precious gem-like beauty.

vietnamese.name: Triet
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the philosophical wisdom and the penetrating understanding that sees to the heart of things.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'wisdom' or 'philosophical understanding.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese Confucian tradition of valuing deep intellectual comprehension.

vietnamese.name: My Hanh
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the beautiful and good one whose virtue and loveliness are equally fine.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining My (beautiful) and Hanh (good/moral). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names combining aesthetic and moral virtues.

vietnamese.name: Huy
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the brilliant and radiant quality of something that shines outward with its own light.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'brilliant' or 'radiant.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of light-names expressing intellectual and personal brilliance.

vietnamese.name: Bich Ngoc
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the jade-colored gem, that precious green stone combining two layers of rare beauty.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Bich (jade-green) and Ngoc (jade/precious gem). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound gem names for women.

vietnamese.name: Vu
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the rain and the heaven that brings nourishing water down upon all the earth.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name meaning 'rain' or 'universe.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural connection to the rain as both natural phenomenon and cosmic force.

vietnamese.name: Tuoi Xuan
vietnamese.meaning: Paints the image of the fresh and youthful spring, the season when everything is new and alive.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Tuoi (fresh/youthful) and Xuan (spring). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of compound names celebrating fresh springtime youth.

vietnamese.name: Ngan
vietnamese.meaning: Evokes the silver quality of something precious and gleaming with cool and enduring beauty.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female name meaning 'silver.' Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese tradition of precious metal names for women celebrating cool and enduring beauty.

vietnamese.name: Hung Cuong
vietnamese.meaning: Speaks of the heroic power and the brave strength of one whose force is outstanding.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male compound name combining Hung (heroic) and Cuong (powerful). Common in Vietnam; reflects the Vietnamese cultural tradition of compound names expressing heroic masculine strength.

vietnamese.name: Kim Loan
vietnamese.meaning: Carries the golden phoenix, that magnificent bird of renewal whose golden form is beyond compare.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese female compound name combining Kim (gold) and Loan (phoenix). Common in Vietnam; the phoenix combined with gold creates an image of supreme beauty and renewal.

vietnamese.name: Phat
vietnamese.meaning: Reflects the one who has achieved enlightenment and stands in the fullness of awakened understanding.
vietnamese.notes: A Vietnamese male name derived from the Buddhist concept of the Buddha, meaning 'enlightened one.' Common in Vietnam, particularly among Buddhist families; reflects the deep Buddhist tradition in Vietnamese culture.

khmer.name: Sreyleap
khmer.meaning: Carries the leaping joy of a young woman whose spirit rises with lively and bounding energy.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining srey (girl/woman) and leap (leap/jump). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound female names beginning with srey expressing feminine vitality.

khmer.name: Kolab
khmer.meaning: Evokes the rose in full bloom, fragrant and beautiful, offering its beauty freely to all.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name meaning 'rose.' Very common in Cambodia; one of the most beloved Khmer female names; reflects the Khmer tradition of floral names celebrating natural beauty.

khmer.name: Sombo
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the precious jewel and the bright gem whose quality enriches all around it.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name related to the Pali Sambho, meaning 'jewel' or 'treasure.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Theravada Buddhist gem-naming tradition in Khmer culture.

khmer.name: Chariya
khmer.meaning: Carries the excellent conduct and the fine behavior of one who lives with virtuous grace.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Pali Cariya, meaning 'good conduct.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Theravada Buddhist value of proper conduct as a central virtue.

khmer.name: Phal
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the fruit at its ripest, the full result of patient and devoted cultivation.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name meaning 'fruit' or 'result.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist concept of karma as producing fruits; one of the distinctively Khmer male names.

khmer.name: Channary
khmer.meaning: Evokes the moon woman, the lady whose luminous beauty belongs to the celestial night.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining chan (moon) and nary (woman). Very common in Cambodia; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Khmer tradition of lunar names for women.

khmer.name: Visna
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the special and extraordinary quality that sets one apart from all ordinary beings.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Pali Visesa, meaning 'special.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Theravada Buddhist Pali influence on Khmer male naming.

khmer.name: Sreymom
khmer.meaning: Carries the gentle and soft quality of a young woman whose manner is kind and yielding.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining srey (girl) and mom (soft/gentle). Very common in Cambodia; one of the most widely used Khmer female names; reflects the tradition of srey- compound names.

khmer.name: Bunna
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the merit accumulated through righteous living, the wealth of good deeds.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Pali Punna, meaning 'merit' or 'good deeds.' Very common in Cambodia; reflects the central Theravada Buddhist concept of merit in Khmer naming.

khmer.name: Sokunthea
khmer.meaning: Evokes the blooming precious gem, rare and beautiful in its full and magnificent flowering.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining sokun (precious/lucky) and thea (element). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound names combining precious and beautiful imagery.

khmer.name: Chankrisna
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the moonlit dark one, the moon casting its silver light on the deep beautiful night.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name combining chan (moon) and krisna (dark). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Hindu-Buddhist Sanskrit influence on Khmer male naming.

khmer.name: Pisey
khmer.meaning: Carries the special and precious quality of something rare and distinctly set apart.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Pali Pisesa, meaning 'special' or 'precious.' Very common in Cambodia; one of the most popular female names in the country.

khmer.name: Sopheak
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the peaceful and auspicious one who brings good fortune wherever they go.
khmer.notes: A Khmer name meaning 'good fortune' or 'auspicious peace,' used for both males and females. Common in Cambodia; reflects the Theravada Buddhist tradition of names expressing auspicious peace.

khmer.name: Chanraksmey
khmer.meaning: Evokes the moonbeam's ray, that silver thread of light connecting the moon to the earth below.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining chan (moon) and raksmey (ray of light). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound lunar-light names for women.

khmer.name: Vuthy
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the progress and the advancement of one who moves steadily forward in life.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Pali Vuddhi, meaning 'progress' or 'growth.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist concept of gradual development and advancement.

khmer.name: Botum
khmer.meaning: Carries the lotus flower, that sacred bloom which rises pure and beautiful from the murky depths.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Pali Paduma, meaning 'lotus.' Common in Cambodia; the lotus is the most sacred Buddhist flower symbol of purity rising from difficult conditions.

khmer.name: Sambath
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the precious wealth and the treasure accumulated through merit and virtue.
khmer.notes: A Khmer name derived from the Pali Sampatti, meaning 'wealth' or 'treasure,' used for both males and females. Common in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist concept of merit-based prosperity.

khmer.name: Chanda
khmer.meaning: Evokes the moon in its serene fullness, that luminous silver disc whose light is gentle and kind.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Pali Chanda, meaning 'moon.' Very common in Cambodia; reflects the central importance of the moon in Khmer Buddhist cosmology and calendar.

khmer.name: Raksa
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the protective guardian who keeps safe what is precious and prevents all harm.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Pali Rakkha, meaning 'protection' or 'guardian.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist tradition of protective naming.

khmer.name: Sovannapha
khmer.meaning: Carries the golden forest, that precious and abundant woodland whose trees are refined gold.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining sovanna (golden) and pha (forest/element). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound golden-imagery names.

khmer.name: Menghour
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the dragon's path, the powerful celestial creature making its majestic way.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name combining meng (dragon) and hour (path/way). Common in Cambodia; the dragon is an auspicious celestial creature in Khmer mythology and Buddhist tradition.

khmer.name: Sreyleak
khmer.meaning: Evokes the excellent young woman whose quality stands out above all others around her.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining srey (girl/woman) and leak (excellent/outstanding). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound srey- names for women.

khmer.name: Bora
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the ancient wisdom and the time-honored knowledge passed down through generations.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Sanskrit Bura, meaning 'ancient' or 'wise.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer cultural pride in ancient civilization and wisdom.

khmer.name: Sophy
khmer.meaning: Carries the good and virtuous character of one whose moral excellence is complete.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Pali Supi, meaning 'good woman.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Theravada Buddhist tradition of virtue names.

khmer.name: Pisach
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the powerful and magnificent one whose quality is beyond the ordinary.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from a Pali form meaning 'powerful' or 'magnificent.' Used in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist naming tradition.

khmer.name: Sreylin
khmer.meaning: Evokes the pure young woman whose character is clear and uncontaminated by anything base.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining srey (girl) and lin (pure/clear). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound srey- names emphasizing purity.

khmer.name: Rithy
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the prosperous and successful one whose achievements bring well-deserved abundance.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Pali Riddhi, meaning 'prosperity' or 'supernatural power.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist concept of merit-based success.

khmer.name: Sreynich
khmer.meaning: Carries the small and precious girl, delicate and dear to all who know her gentle nature.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining srey (girl) and nich (small/little). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of affectionate compound names using diminutive forms.

khmer.name: Vannak
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the colorful and brilliant quality of something beautiful and multifaceted.
khmer.notes: A Khmer name derived from the Pali Vanna, meaning 'color' or 'beautiful,' used for both males and females. Common in Cambodia; reflects the Buddhist tradition of beauty-names.

khmer.name: Borin
khmer.meaning: Evokes the ancient city and the civilized order of a well-established and cultured community.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name meaning 'ancient city' or related to old civilization. Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer cultural pride in the ancient Angkor civilization.

khmer.name: Somaly
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the garland of flowers worn as an offering, fragrant and beautifully arranged.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Sanskrit Mali, meaning 'garland.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Brahmanical tradition of garland-names and the importance of flower offerings in Khmer Buddhism.

khmer.name: Daro
khmer.meaning: Carries the star and its constant light, steady and clear in guiding those who look up.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name meaning 'star.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of celestial names for men.

khmer.name: Sinath
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the lion lord, that magnificent creature whose authority over all is natural.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name combining si (lion) and nath (lord). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Brahmanical tradition of lion-lord names in Khmer culture; the lion is a royal symbol.

khmer.name: Ratha
khmer.meaning: Evokes the chariot and the swift and powerful vehicle that carries one toward their destiny.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name derived from the Pali Ratha, meaning 'chariot.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the ancient Brahmanical and Buddhist imagery of the chariot as a symbol of purpose.

khmer.name: Sovattha
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the auspicious goodness and the blessed quality that marks a fortunate life.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name derived from the Pali Suvatthi, meaning 'good fortune' or 'auspicious.' Common in Cambodia; reflects the Theravada Buddhist tradition of auspicious naming.

khmer.name: Panha Reach
khmer.meaning: Carries the royal inquiry and the noble questioning that opens the path to highest understanding.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male compound name combining Panha (question/inquiry) and Reach (royal). Used in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer royal naming tradition.

khmer.name: Sokunthear
khmer.meaning: Paints the image of the precious garden where rare and beautiful things grow in abundance.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name combining sokun (precious) and thear (element). Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound names combining precious imagery with garden beauty.

khmer.name: Chandara
khmer.meaning: Evokes the moon star, a double celestial beauty combining the light of two heavenly bodies.
khmer.notes: A Khmer name combining chan (moon) and dara (star), used for both males and females in Cambodia. Common in Cambodia; reflects the Khmer tradition of compound celestial names.

khmer.name: Makara
khmer.meaning: Speaks of the mythical sea creature of the sacred waters, the cosmic being of January.
khmer.notes: A Khmer male name meaning 'January' or associated with the Makara water creature. Common in Cambodia; often given to boys born in January; reflects the Khmer lunar calendar tradition.

khmer.name: Davy
khmer.meaning: Carries the beloved quality of one who is deeply cherished and held dear by those who love.
khmer.notes: A Khmer female name used in Cambodia. Reflects the modern Khmer naming tradition that incorporates international names; common among younger generations in Phnom Penh.

burmese.name: Mi Mi
burmese.meaning: Carries the endearing and beloved quality of one who is cherished with deep familial affection.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication used as an affectionate term of address. Very common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplication in female names expressing endearment.

burmese.name: Kyaw Thu
burmese.meaning: Evokes the famous brightness of one whose renown and illumination spread widely.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Kyaw (famous) and Thu (bright). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining renown with luminous qualities.

burmese.name: Khin Moe
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the lovable sky above, the beloved expanse whose vastness fills the heart with wonder.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Khin (lovable) and Moe (sky/rain). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound female names combining affectionate and natural imagery.

burmese.name: Zaw Lin
burmese.meaning: Carries the brave and beautiful quality of one whose courage is matched by elegant grace.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Zaw (brave) and Lin (beautiful). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining martial and aesthetic virtues.

burmese.name: Thuzar
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the clever and sharp intelligence of one whose mind moves with quick precision.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name meaning 'clever' or 'intelligent.' Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of names expressing intellectual qualities.

burmese.name: Hein Htet
burmese.meaning: Evokes the supreme crystal clarity of one whose understanding is pure and completely transparent.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Hein (crystal) and Htet (supreme). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining material and superlative qualities.

burmese.name: Aye Myat
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the cool brilliance of one whose refreshing intelligence shines with clear and steady light.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Aye (cool) and Myat (brilliant). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound female names combining sensory and luminous imagery.

burmese.name: Thet Paing
burmese.meaning: Carries the certain and clear white purity of one whose character is beyond all doubt.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Thet (certain/clear) and Paing (white). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining certainty and purity.

burmese.name: Su Su
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the doubly serene one whose peace is reinforced and complete in all ways.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication of Su (serene). Very common in Myanmar; the reduplication emphasizes the quality of serenity; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated names.

burmese.name: Min Thu
burmese.meaning: Evokes the bright king whose radiant authority shines over all those in his realm.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Min (king) and Thu (bright). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese royal naming tradition and the connection between kingship and brilliance.

burmese.name: Wai Wai
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the doubly precious ruby quality, a gem whose value is reinforced and doubled.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication of Wai (ruby/precious). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of female reduplicated names; rubies are among Myanmar's most prized gems.

burmese.name: Sithu
burmese.meaning: Carries the courageous hero whose brave deeds distinguish them above all ordinary people.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male name derived from the Pali Siddhi, meaning 'hero' or 'brave warrior.' Common in Myanmar; reflects the Sanskrit and Pali influence on Burmese naming through Theravada Buddhism.

burmese.name: Myint Myint
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the doubly elevated one whose high quality is reinforced on every side.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication of Myint (elevated/high). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated female names emphasizing positive qualities.

burmese.name: Aung Naing
burmese.meaning: Evokes the victorious conqueror whose success through merit is complete and absolute.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Aung (success) and Naing (to conquer). Very common in Myanmar; one of the most recognizable Burmese male compound names.

burmese.name: Thin Zar
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the crystal-clear excellence of one whose purity and distinction are inseparable.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Thin (clear/crystal) and Zar (excellent). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound female names combining clarity and excellence.

burmese.name: Myo Zaw
burmese.meaning: Carries the brave people of the clan whose courage defines and sustains their community.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Myo (people/clan) and Zaw (brave). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining community and courage.

burmese.name: Khin Hnin
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the lovable and tender one whose gentle and soft nature wins all hearts.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Khin (lovable) and Hnin (tender/gentle). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound female names combining warmth with tenderness.

burmese.name: Nyan Win
burmese.meaning: Evokes the brilliantly wise one whose intelligence shines with the clarity of enlightened understanding.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Nyan (wisdom/knowledge) and Win (bright). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese Buddhist tradition of wisdom combined with brightness.

burmese.name: Shwe Yee
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the golden righteousness of one whose virtue has the warm and precious quality of gold.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Shwe (gold) and Yee (righteousness). Common in Myanmar; gold is associated with Buddhist merit in Burmese culture.

burmese.name: Pyae Sone
burmese.meaning: Carries the spreading wholeness and the expanding completeness of one full in every dimension.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Pyae (spread/expand) and Sone (whole/complete). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names expressing completeness.

burmese.name: Ei Ei
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the doubly radiant one whose brightness is reinforced and abundantly clear.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication used as an affectionate given name. Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated female names expressing doubled positive qualities.

burmese.name: Ko Gyi
burmese.meaning: Evokes the great elder brother, the respected elder who carries the weight of experience and wisdom.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male name combining Ko (elder brother) and Gyi (great). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of kinship and seniority terms used as personal names.

burmese.name: Yamon
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the beautiful woman of the forest, one whose natural grace belongs to the living world.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name meaning 'woman of the forest' or 'beautiful forest spirit.' Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of nature-spirit names for women.

burmese.name: Kyaw Kyaw
burmese.meaning: Carries the doubly famous quality of one whose renown is reinforced and spreads in all directions.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male name, a reduplication of Kyaw (famous). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated names for both men and women.

burmese.name: Aye Aye
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the doubly cool and refreshing one whose pleasant quality is fully complete.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication of Aye (cool/refreshing). Very common in Myanmar; the most commonly reduplicated Burmese female name element.

burmese.name: Zin Mar
burmese.meaning: Evokes the excellent coral jewel, that bright red gem formed in the sea with enduring beauty.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Zin (excellent) and Mar (coral). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of gem-names for women.

burmese.name: Nanda Win
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the bright joy of one whose happiness shines outward with luminous and radiant quality.
burmese.notes: A Burmese name combining Nanda (joy) and Win (bright), used for both males and females. Common in Myanmar; reflects the Pali Buddhist naming tradition combined with the Burmese light-naming tradition.

burmese.name: Pyithu
burmese.meaning: Carries the brave people's spirit and the collective courage of the community.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male name combining pyi (people/nation) and thu (bright/brave). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese national sentiment embedded in naming culture.

burmese.name: Moe Moe
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the doubly sky-like one whose vast and open quality encompasses all.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication of Moe (sky/rain). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated female names; sky imagery suggests vastness and openness.

burmese.name: Nay Win
burmese.meaning: Evokes the bright sunshine of one whose solar luminosity illuminates all that surrounds them.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Nay (sun) and Win (bright). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese solar naming tradition combining sun imagery with brilliance.

burmese.name: Kyi Kyi
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the doubly distinctive quality of one who stands out clearly from everything around.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication meaning 'distinctive.' Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated female names emphasizing notable qualities.

burmese.name: Thet Zin
burmese.meaning: Carries the certain excellence of one whose distinguished quality is beyond any question.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Thet (certain) and Zin (excellent). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese naming tradition of compound names expressing absolute excellence.

burmese.name: Wint Yee
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the righteous diamond, a gem whose purity and hardness embody moral virtue.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Wint (diamond) and Yee (righteousness). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining precious stones with virtue.

burmese.name: Aung Zaw
burmese.meaning: Evokes the brave success and the courageous achievement of one who triumphs through valor.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Aung (success) and Zaw (brave). Very common in Myanmar; one of the most frequently used Burmese male compound names.

burmese.name: Nandar Aung
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the joyfully moonlit success, the triumph that shines with the soft light of happiness.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Nandar (joyful moonlight) and Aung (success). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound names combining lunar joy with achievement.

burmese.name: Myat Noe
burmese.meaning: Carries the brilliant and fragrant quality of one whose radiance and sweetness are both outstanding.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Myat (brilliant) and Noe (fragrant). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound female names combining luminous and sensory qualities.

burmese.name: Zay Yar Lwin
burmese.meaning: Paints the image of the brilliant market of dawn, the bustling vitality of a life lit from within.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Zay (market/vitality), Yar (dawn), and Lwin (brilliant). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of three-element compound names.

burmese.name: Thu Thu
burmese.meaning: Evokes the doubly bright one whose luminosity is reinforced and unmistakably radiant.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female name, a reduplication of Thu (bright). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of reduplicated female names emphasizing luminous qualities.

burmese.name: Yan Aung
burmese.meaning: Speaks of the victorious conqueror whose success comes from deep within and spreads outward.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Yan (inner/deep) and Aung (success). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese Buddhist idea of inner achievement leading to outward success.

burmese.name: Thinzar Wint
burmese.meaning: Carries the crystal diamond, the union of two precious stones in one name of extraordinary beauty.
burmese.notes: A Burmese female compound name combining Thinzar (crystal) and Wint (diamond). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese tradition of compound gem-names for women.

burmese.name: Ye Naing
burmese.meaning: Carries the conquering warrior who succeeds through deep inner resolve and determination.
burmese.notes: A Burmese male compound name combining Ye (inner/deep) and Naing (conquer). Common in Myanmar; reflects the Burmese Buddhist tradition of inner power leading to external achievement.

lao.name: Chansamone
lao.meaning: Evokes the moon of a good person, the celestial quality that marks someone of true virtue.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining chan (moon) and samone (good person). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names joining celestial and virtuous imagery.

lao.name: Phouthone
lao.meaning: Carries the golden earth and the precious abundance of land that yields its riches generously.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining phou (earth) and thone (golden element). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao agricultural tradition and the cultural association of the earth with golden abundance.

lao.name: Khamchan
lao.meaning: Speaks of the golden moon, the precious celestial body whose radiance is as valuable as gold.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining kham (gold) and chan (moon). Very common in Laos; one of the most beautiful Lao compound names combining golden and lunar imagery.

lao.name: Sengthong
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the golden light that shines with precious radiance in all directions.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining seng (light) and thong (gold), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of compound names combining light with golden auspiciousness.

lao.name: Bouaphan
lao.meaning: Carries the flower of wisdom and the blossoming understanding that grows from devoted learning.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining boua (flower/lotus) and phan (wisdom). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of compound names joining lotus imagery with intellectual virtue.

lao.name: Vilaysack
lao.meaning: Evokes the powerful victory and the triumphant force of one who prevails through merit.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining vilay (victory) and sack (power). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao naming tradition of compound names expressing victorious strength.

lao.name: Thipkesone
lao.meaning: Speaks of the heavenly crystal gem, that precious stone of celestial clarity above all things.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining tip (heaven) and kesone (crystal/gem). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of compound names combining celestial and precious gem imagery.

lao.name: Sompong
lao.meaning: Carries the worthy vessel and the capable container that holds what is most valuable.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining som (worthy) and pong (vessel/capable). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of names expressing the worthy capacity to hold and preserve what matters.

lao.name: Daovone
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the star of heaven, a celestial light burning in the highest realm above.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining dao (star) and vone (heaven/element). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound celestial names for women.

lao.name: Bounmee
lao.meaning: Evokes the merit and the good fortune of one who is blessed with the luck of righteous living.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining boun (merit/blessing) and mee (to have), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the central Theravada Buddhist concept of bun (merit).

lao.name: Xayphone
lao.meaning: Speaks of the victorious forest growth and the flourishing abundance of life's natural expansion.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining xay (victory) and phone (forest/growth). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao naming tradition of compound names combining victory with natural growth.

lao.name: Phongsavanh
lao.meaning: Carries the heavenly forest and the celestial woodland where sacred growth is eternal.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining phone (forest) and savanh (heaven), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of heavenly imagery in naming.

lao.name: Khamsouk
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the golden happiness and the precious joy that enriches a fortunate life.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining kham (gold) and souk (happiness), used for both males and females. Very common in Laos; reflects the Lao cultural combination of material and emotional prosperity.

lao.name: Nalinthip
lao.meaning: Evokes the heavenly flower, the celestial lotus that blooms in the realm above all earthly things.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining nalin (lotus) and tip (heaven). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of heavenly lotus imagery in female naming.

lao.name: Bounthong
lao.meaning: Speaks of the golden blessing and the precious merit that adorns a fortunate and righteous life.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining boun (merit/blessing) and thong (gold), used for both males and females. Very common in Laos; one of the most popular names; reflects the combination of merit and golden blessing.

lao.name: Vongdala
lao.meaning: Carries the royal star lineage and the distinguished celestial heritage of a noble line.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining vong (dynasty/lineage) and dala (star). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao royal naming tradition and the importance of celestial lineage.

lao.name: Thavonsouk
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the heavenly happiness whose bliss is celestial and divine in its quality.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining thavon (heaven) and souk (happiness). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of celestial happiness names.

lao.name: Sidalay
lao.meaning: Evokes the victorious prosperity and the triumphant abundance of a life fully blessed.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining si (prosperity) and dalay (victorious). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names combining prosperity with victory.

lao.name: Khantaphon
lao.meaning: Speaks of the patient forest growth and the enduring expansion of one who develops through perseverance.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining khanta (patient) and phone (forest/growth). Common in Laos; reflects the Theravada Buddhist virtue of khanti (patience) combined with natural growth imagery.

lao.name: Phetsamone
lao.meaning: Carries the diamond of virtue and the precious hardness of one whose good character is unbreakable.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining phet (diamond) and samone (virtue/goodness). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of compound names combining precious gems with moral virtue.

lao.name: Somsanh
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the worthy peace and the deserved tranquility of one who has earned their rest.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining som (worthy) and sanh (peace), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist ideal of deserved peace as a goal of righteous living.

lao.name: Viengkham
lao.meaning: Evokes the golden fortress and the precious strong place that protects what is most valued.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining vieng (fortress/city) and kham (gold), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the historical Lao tradition of wiang (fortified city) names.

lao.name: Phommasone
lao.meaning: Speaks of the fullness of divine perfection, the complete and radiant presence of the highest celestial force.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining phomma (Brahma) and sone (complete). Common in Laos; reflects the Brahmanical Hindu influence on Lao naming through the Theravada Buddhist tradition.

lao.name: Keomone
lao.meaning: Carries the crystal of virtue and the jewel of moral goodness that is precious above all.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining keo (crystal/jewel) and mone (virtue). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names combining gem imagery with moral virtue.

lao.name: Sengphachanh
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the moonlit light, the radiance that combines celestial illumination.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining seng (light) and phachanh (moon). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of compound names drawing on both light and lunar imagery.

lao.name: Vilaythong
lao.meaning: Evokes the golden victory and the triumphant achievement adorned with precious brightness.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining vilay (victory) and thong (gold), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names combining victory with golden auspiciousness.

lao.name: Bouadanh
lao.meaning: Speaks of the flower of the land, the beautiful blossom that grows from and belongs to the earth.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining boua (flower) and danh (land/earth). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao agricultural tradition and the connection between feminine beauty and the fertile land.

lao.name: Phonkham
lao.meaning: Carries the golden forest and the precious abundant woodland of rich and beautiful growth.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining phone (forest) and kham (gold). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names joining natural forest imagery with precious golden quality.

lao.name: Sithixay
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the victorious establishment of something lasting and firmly built to endure.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining sithi (established/firm) and xay (victory). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao naming tradition of compound names expressing stable victory.

lao.name: Dalinthip
lao.meaning: Evokes the heavenly star, the celestial light burning bright in the highest realm above.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining dalin (star) and tip (heaven). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names combining stellar and heavenly imagery for women.

lao.name: Souphanphone
lao.meaning: Speaks of the beautiful forest growth and the lovely abundance that fills the natural world.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining souphan (beautiful) and phone (forest/growth). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names joining beauty with natural growth imagery.

lao.name: Vanthong
lao.meaning: Carries the golden blessing and the precious celestial favor of divine heaven's golden gift.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining van (heaven/blessing) and thong (gold), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of heavenly golden blessing names.

lao.name: Nithakhone
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the enduring prosperity and the lasting abundance that continues through time.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining nitha (enduring) and khone (prosperous). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao naming tradition of compound names expressing lasting prosperity.

lao.name: Inpong
lao.meaning: Evokes the great capable vessel and the worthy container that holds all that is most valuable.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining in (great) and pong (vessel/capable). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of names expressing the great capacity to hold and protect what matters.

lao.name: Phetmany
lao.meaning: Speaks of the diamond jewel, the most precious and hardest of all gems combined in beauty.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining phet (diamond) and many (jewel), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of compound gem-names celebrating precious beauty.

lao.name: Khouanvilay
lao.meaning: Carries the victorious heart and the triumphant spirit of one who prevails with inner strength.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining khouan (heart/spirit) and vilay (victory). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names combining inner qualities with outer achievement.

lao.name: Thongdee
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the good and golden one whose precious quality and goodness are united.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining thong (gold) and dee (good), used for both males and females. Very common in Laos; one of the simplest and most widely used Lao compound names.

lao.name: Souriya
lao.meaning: Evokes the sun and its radiant energy, the great celestial body that sustains all living things.
lao.notes: A Lao name derived from the Sanskrit Suriya, meaning 'sun,' used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Sanskrit and Buddhist solar influence on Lao naming culture.

lao.name: Nanthakone
lao.meaning: Speaks of the prosperous joy and the abundant happiness of one whose life overflows with blessing.
lao.notes: A Lao female name combining nantha (joy) and kone (prosperous). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao tradition of compound names expressing both happiness and material prosperity.

lao.name: Phengthip
lao.meaning: Carries the heavenly light and the celestial radiance that shines from the highest realm above.
lao.notes: A Lao name combining pheng (light) and tip (heaven), used for both males and females. Common in Laos; reflects the Lao Buddhist tradition of combining light with celestial imagery.

lao.name: Latsamee
lao.meaning: Paints the image of the auspicious goddess, the divine and fortunate one of blessed character.
lao.notes: A Lao female name derived from the Sanskrit Lakshmi, the goddess of prosperity. Common in Laos; reflects the Hindu-Buddhist influence on Lao naming through the Brahmanical tradition.

lao.name: Khampasith
lao.meaning: Evokes the golden achievement and the precious accomplishment of one who excels with merit.
lao.notes: A Lao male name combining kham (gold) and pasith (achievement/auspicious). Common in Laos; reflects the Lao naming tradition of compound names combining golden imagery with achievement.

chinese.name: Jiajia
chinese.meaning: Carries the doubly beautiful quality of one whose loveliness is reinforced and completely fine.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name, a reduplication of Jia (beautiful/fine). Common in China; the reduplication of single characters for female names is a popular trend in modern Chinese naming.

chinese.name: Wenbo
chinese.meaning: Evokes the broad culture and the extensive learning of one who has studied widely and deeply.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Wen (culture/literary) and Bo (broad/extensive). Common in China; reflects the Confucian ideal of broad cultural learning as the mark of a cultivated person.

chinese.name: Shuang
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the crisp and bright clarity of a cool clear morning when the air is clean and sharp.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name meaning 'crisp' or 'bright and clear.' Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of atmospheric clarity as a metaphor for feminine freshness.

chinese.name: Tianming
chinese.meaning: Carries the heaven-bestowed brilliance and the celestial radiance of one divinely illuminated.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Tian (heaven) and Ming (brilliant). Common in China; reflects the Confucian concept of the Mandate of Heaven and the celestial source of intelligence.

chinese.name: Ruochen
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the morning-like one, someone fresh and clear as the first light of day.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Ruo (like/as) and Chen (morning). Common in China; reflects the contemporary Chinese naming trend of poetic comparative names.

chinese.name: Jiahao
chinese.meaning: Evokes the fine and excellent quality of one whose goodness is outstanding in all dimensions.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Jia (fine/beautiful) and Hao (excellent). Very common in China; one of the most popular male names in modern China; reflects the tradition of compound virtue names.

chinese.name: Zixuan
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the self-selected and autonomous one who finds their own path with confident independence.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Zi (self/person) and Xuan (select/beautiful). Common in China; reflects the contemporary trend of names expressing personal autonomy.

chinese.name: Yongkang
chinese.meaning: Carries the eternally healthy and vigorous quality of one whose strength never diminishes.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Yong (eternal) and Kang (healthy). Common in China; reflects the Chinese cultural wish for enduring health and vigor.

chinese.name: Mengyao
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the dreaming charm and the enchanting quality of one who captivates like a dream.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Meng (dream) and Yao (charming/elegant). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of dream imagery for feminine enchantment.

chinese.name: Junhao
chinese.meaning: Evokes the excellent talented one whose outstanding ability is matched by their fine character.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Jun (talented) and Hao (excellent). Very common in China; one of the most popular male names; reflects the Chinese cultural ideal of combining talent with excellence.

chinese.name: Bingxin
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the pure heart that is clear as ice, untouched by anything that would cloud its clarity.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Bing (ice) and Xin (heart). Common in China; associated with the celebrated Chinese author Bing Xin; ice purity is a powerful metaphor in Chinese literature.

chinese.name: Chenyu
chinese.meaning: Carries the precious jade morning, that most beautiful and valued beginning of the day.
chinese.notes: A Chinese name combining Chen (morning) and Yu (jade), used for both males and females. Common in China; jade and morning together suggest precious new beginnings.

chinese.name: Yichen
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the gracious morning and the gentle beginning of a day touched by elegance.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Yi (gracious) and Chen (morning). Common in China; reflects the Chinese naming tradition of elegant morning imagery for women.

chinese.name: Zhenghao
chinese.meaning: Evokes the upright excellence of one whose honesty and goodness are both complete and admirable.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Zheng (upright/correct) and Hao (excellent). Common in China; reflects the Confucian value of rectitude combined with excellence.

chinese.name: Lingxiao
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the clever dawn and the agile intelligence that awakens with the first light of day.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Ling (clever/agile) and Xiao (dawn). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of combining intellectual qualities with natural phenomena.

chinese.name: Guanyu
chinese.meaning: Carries the jade crown of official excellence, the precious gem worn by those of distinguished service.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Guan (official/crown) and Yu (jade). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of official-distinction naming.

chinese.name: Yuxi
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the jade dawn, that precious moment when the first light of morning gleams.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Yu (jade) and Xi (dawn/morning glow). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of jade morning imagery for women.

chinese.name: Xincheng
chinese.meaning: Evokes the sincere and successful one whose honest character leads to genuine achievement.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Xin (sincere) and Cheng (successful). Common in China; reflects the Confucian ideal of sincerity as the foundation of all genuine success.

chinese.name: Ruiyao
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the charming sharp perception of one whose intelligence is as captivating as it is keen.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Rui (sharp/perceptive) and Yao (charming). Common in China; reflects the Chinese naming tradition of compound names expressing both intellectual and aesthetic qualities.

chinese.name: Haowen
chinese.meaning: Carries the excellent culture and the outstanding achievement in literary and intellectual arts.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Hao (excellent) and Wen (culture/literary). Common in China; reflects the Confucian literary tradition in Chinese naming culture.

chinese.name: Ziyao
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the self-illuminating charm, the inner beauty that radiates its own light.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Zi (self) and Yao (charming/elegant). Common in China; reflects the contemporary Chinese naming tradition of self-expressive names for women.

chinese.name: Jianping
chinese.meaning: Evokes the peaceful strength of one whose robust health is matched by their tranquil character.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Jian (strong/healthy) and Ping (peaceful). Common in China; reflects the Chinese cultural ideal of combining physical strength with inner peace.

chinese.name: Nuanxin
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the warm heart whose gentle heat brings comfort to all those who draw near.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Nuan (warm) and Xin (heart). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of heart-warmth as a metaphor for feminine compassion.

chinese.name: Zeyu
chinese.meaning: Carries the jade beneficence and the generous goodness that nourishes all around it.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Ze (beneficence/grace) and Yu (jade). Common in China; reflects the Chinese naming tradition of combining virtue with jade imagery.

chinese.name: Chunhua
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the spring flower blooming in its finest season of fragrant beauty.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Chun (spring) and Hua (flower). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of spring flowers as a symbol of feminine beauty at its peak.

chinese.name: Xiangming
chinese.meaning: Evokes the brilliant fragrance of something both beautifully scented and luminously bright.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Xiang (fragrant) and Ming (brilliant). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of combining sensory beauty with intellectual brilliance.

chinese.name: Yujie
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the outstanding jade quality of one whose excellent character shines like precious stone.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Yu (jade) and Jie (outstanding). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of jade as the ultimate symbol of virtue and excellence.

chinese.name: Zhiqiang
chinese.meaning: Carries the powerful wisdom of one whose strength and understanding are equally formidable.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Zhi (wisdom) and Qiang (powerful). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of combining intellectual and physical virtues in naming.

chinese.name: Xinyu
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the sincere jade quality, the precious stone whose authenticity is flawless.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Xin (sincere/heart) and Yu (jade). Very common in China; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Chinese ideal of sincere excellence.

chinese.name: Weijie
chinese.meaning: Evokes the magnificent outstanding one whose greatness stands above all ordinary distinction.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Wei (magnificent) and Jie (outstanding). Common in China; reflects the Chinese naming tradition of compound names expressing combined excellence.

chinese.name: Lingzhi
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the sacred mushroom of immortality whose properties bring wisdom and eternal life.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name meaning 'sacred mushroom of immortality.' Common in China; the lingzhi mushroom is a powerful symbol of longevity and wisdom in Chinese culture.

chinese.name: Haoming
chinese.meaning: Carries the excellent brightness of one whose intellectual radiance is outstanding and admirable.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Hao (excellent) and Ming (bright). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of compound names combining excellence with luminous intelligence.

chinese.name: Xueying
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the snow hero, the brave and graceful one who stands pure against winter's cold.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Xue (snow) and Ying (hero/gracious). Common in China; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of snow as purity and hero as graceful strength.

chinese.name: Wenhui
chinese.meaning: Evokes the cultural wisdom and the intelligent understanding that comes from deep literary cultivation.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Wen (culture) and Hui (wisdom/intelligent). Common in China; reflects the Confucian ideal of cultural cultivation leading to wisdom.

chinese.name: Jiayi
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the beautiful and righteous quality of one whose loveliness and virtue are perfectly combined.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Jia (beautiful) and Yi (righteousness). Very common in China; one of the most popular female names; reflects the Chinese tradition of combining aesthetic and moral virtues.

chinese.name: Mingzhen
chinese.meaning: Carries the brilliant and precious quality of one whose luminous excellence is genuine.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Ming (brilliant) and Zhen (precious/genuine). Common in China; reflects the Chinese naming tradition of compound names expressing authentic brilliance.

chinese.name: Yiqing
chinese.meaning: Reflects the gracious and pure quality of one whose elegant nature is both refined and unsullied.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Yi (gracious) and Qing (pure/clear). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of compound names combining elegance with purity.

chinese.name: Haolong
chinese.meaning: Paints the image of the excellent dragon, the magnificent creature of outstanding and powerful quality.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Hao (excellent) and Long (dragon). Common in China; the dragon is the most powerful and auspicious creature in Chinese mythology.

chinese.name: Meiqi
chinese.meaning: Evokes the beautiful and auspicious quality of something lovely and fortunate in equal measure.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Mei (beautiful) and Qi (auspicious/fine). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of compound female names combining beauty with good fortune.

chinese.name: Zhiwei
chinese.meaning: Speaks of the magnificent wisdom of one whose great understanding is matched by their presence.
chinese.notes: A Chinese male name combining Zhi (wisdom) and Wei (magnificent). Common in China; reflects the Confucian ideal of magnificent wisdom as the highest masculine virtue.

chinese.name: Shuhua
chinese.meaning: Carries the excellent flowering beauty and the fine blossoming quality of refined achievement.
chinese.notes: A Chinese female name combining Shu (excellent/graceful) and Hua (flower). Common in China; reflects the Chinese tradition of compound names combining graceful excellence with floral beauty.

chinese.name: Tianyi
chinese.meaning: Evokes the heaven-blessed righteousness and the celestially endowed virtue of one truly good.
chinese.notes: A Chinese name combining Tian (heaven) and Yi (righteousness), used for both males and females. Common in China; reflects the Confucian concept of heaven-bestowed moral virtue.

cantonese.name: Tsz Hin
cantonese.meaning: Carries the gracious and talented quality of one whose kindness and ability are equally fine.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Tsz (gracious) and Hin (talented), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong and Guangdong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound names expressing combined virtues.

cantonese.name: Ho Kei
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the fine and distinguished quality of one who stands out through genuine excellence.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Ho (good/fine) and Kei (distinguished). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound names combining simple goodness with distinction.

cantonese.name: Lok Yee
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the joyful righteousness of one whose happiness and virtue are permanently united.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Lok (joyful) and Yee (righteousness). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Confucian tradition of names combining happiness with moral virtue.

cantonese.name: Siu Chung
cantonese.meaning: Carries the small loyal one, the devoted and faithful person whose commitment never wavers.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Siu (small/modest) and Chung (loyal). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Confucian value of loyalty expressed through humble and faithful service.

cantonese.name: Yuen Ting
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the graceful and refined quality of one who is elegant in all they do.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Yuen (graceful) and Ting (elegant). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound female names expressing cultivated elegance.

cantonese.name: Chun Sing
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the glorious spring, the triumphant season when everything comes alive with abundance.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Chun (spring) and Sing (glorious). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of seasonal and glory names combined.

cantonese.name: Pui Lam
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the magnificent forest and the cultivated beauty of a woodland that is vast and splendid.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Pui (magnificent/cultivated) and Lam (forest). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of nature imagery in female naming.

cantonese.name: Yiu Ming
cantonese.meaning: Carries the outstanding brilliance of one whose radiant excellence shines without obstruction.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Yiu (outstanding) and Ming (brilliant). Common in Hong Kong and Guangdong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound names expressing luminous achievement.

cantonese.name: Ching Tung
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the pure and clear understanding of one whose comprehension is unmistakably clean.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Ching (pure/clear) and Tung (understand/bright), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of names combining purity with understanding.

cantonese.name: Ka Yin
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the beautiful and graceful quality of one whose aesthetic refinement is completely fine.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Ka (beautiful/fine) and Yin (graceful). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound names expressing cultivated beauty.

cantonese.name: Wai Lun
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the magnificent discourse and the great eloquence of one whose speech commands attention.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Wai (magnificent) and Lun (discourse/eloquent). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of names celebrating the power of eloquent speech.

cantonese.name: Sin Ying
cantonese.meaning: Carries the transcendent heroism of one who goes beyond the ordinary in both virtue and achievement.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Sin (transcendent/immortal) and Ying (hero/gracious). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Taoist influence on Cantonese naming through immortal imagery.

cantonese.name: Tak Keung
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the virtuous and strong one whose moral power and physical force are united.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Tak (virtue) and Keung (strong). Common in Hong Kong and Guangdong; reflects the Confucian ideal of virtue strengthened by physical power.

cantonese.name: Hiu Lam
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the wise forest and the understanding that grows deep and wide like trees in a vast wood.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Hiu (wise/kind) and Lam (forest). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of wisdom names joined with natural imagery.

cantonese.name: Kam Shing
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the golden glory and the precious triumph that shines with the warm radiance of success.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Kam (gold) and Shing (glorious), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of golden victory names.

cantonese.name: Wai Ying
cantonese.meaning: Carries the magnificent heroism of one whose great achievement combines nobility with brave excellence.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Wai (magnificent) and Ying (hero/gracious), used for both males and females. Very common in Hong Kong; one of the most widely used Cantonese name combinations.

cantonese.name: Oi Ling
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the beloved clever spirit whose intelligence is cherished by all who know them.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Oi (love/beloved) and Ling (clever/spirit). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound names combining affection with intellectual qualities.

cantonese.name: Bun Wai
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the magnificent cultural achievement of one whose literary excellence is outstanding.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Bun (literary/culture) and Wai (magnificent). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Confucian literary tradition expressed in Cantonese naming.

cantonese.name: Hei Tung
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the joyful understanding and the happy clarity of one who comprehends with delight.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Hei (joyful/happy) and Tung (understand), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese naming tradition combining joy with intellectual comprehension.

cantonese.name: Mei Yuk
cantonese.meaning: Carries the beautiful jade quality, the lovely and precious stone whose beauty is enduring.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Mei (beautiful) and Yuk (jade). Common in Hong Kong and Guangdong; reflects the Chinese tradition of jade as the symbol of beauty and virtue.

cantonese.name: Kwong Cheung
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the radiant flourishing and the wide expansion of brilliant achievement.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Kwong (radiant/wide) and Cheung (flourish). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of names expressing radiant expansion.

cantonese.name: Suet Yee
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the pure and righteous snow, the immaculate white that embodies both purity and virtue.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Suet (snow) and Yee (righteousness). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Chinese poetic tradition of snow as a metaphor for moral purity.

cantonese.name: Kin Wai
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the magnificent strength of one whose powerful force commands respect and admiration.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Kin (strong/healthy) and Wai (magnificent). Very common in Hong Kong and Guangdong; one of the most recognizable Cantonese male names.

cantonese.name: Pak Yin
cantonese.meaning: Carries the pure and graceful quality of something white and elegant, clean and beautifully refined.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Pak (white/pure) and Yin (graceful), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of purity names joined with elegance.

cantonese.name: Man Yiu
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the outstanding literary person whose cultural achievement shines with distinction.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Man (culture/literary) and Yiu (outstanding), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Confucian literary tradition in Cantonese culture.

cantonese.name: Ho Yin
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the good and graceful person of fine character whose virtue and elegance are harmonious.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Ho (good) and Yin (graceful), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of names combining simple goodness with elegance.

cantonese.name: Tsz Wai
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the gracious magnificence of one whose kindness is great and widely recognized.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Tsz (gracious) and Wai (magnificent), used for both males and females. Very common in Hong Kong; one of the most widely used Cantonese name combinations.

cantonese.name: Siu Kwan
cantonese.meaning: Carries the modest and distinguished bearing of one who wears their excellence with quiet dignity.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Siu (small/modest) and Kwan (distinguished), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese cultural value of modest distinction.

cantonese.name: Cheuk Yin
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the upright and graceful one whose rectitude and elegance are equally admirable.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Cheuk (upright/correct) and Yin (graceful), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Confucian tradition of uprightness in Cantonese culture.

cantonese.name: Yik Lun
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the brilliant discourse of one whose intellectual eloquence rises above all ordinary speech.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Yik (brilliant) and Lun (discourse). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese cultural tradition of valuing eloquent and brilliant expression.

cantonese.name: Ka Wai
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the fine and magnificent quality that comes from a combination of beauty and greatness.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Ka (beautiful/fine) and Wai (magnificent), used for both males and females. Very common in Hong Kong; one of the most popular Cantonese name combinations.

cantonese.name: Ming Kei
cantonese.meaning: Carries the brilliant and distinguished light of one whose excellence stands out unmistakably.
