cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Ming (brilliant) and Kei (distinguished), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of luminous distinction names.

cantonese.name: Tsz Yee
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the gracious and righteous person whose kindness and virtue are permanently united.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Tsz (gracious) and Yee (righteousness). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of compound names combining kindness with moral virtue.

cantonese.name: Cheung Ho
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the fine flourishing person of good character whose excellence spreads outward in all directions.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Cheung (flourish) and Ho (good). Common in Hong Kong and Guangdong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of names expressing flourishing goodness.

cantonese.name: Wai Tung
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the magnificent understanding whose great comprehension illuminates all that it touches.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Wai (magnificent) and Tung (understand/bright), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of combining greatness with understanding.

cantonese.name: Lok Shan
cantonese.meaning: Carries the joyful mountain, the happiness that is as stable and enduring as the ancient hills.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Lok (joyful) and Shan (mountain), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of natural imagery combined with emotional states.

cantonese.name: Siu Man
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the modestly cultured person whose refined learning is worn with quiet grace.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Siu (small/modest) and Man (culture), used for both males and females. Very common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of modest cultural refinement.

cantonese.name: Pak Hei
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the pure joy whose white and immaculate happiness is completely clean and freely expressed.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Pak (white/pure) and Hei (joyful), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese naming tradition combining purity with happiness.

cantonese.name: Ching Wan
cantonese.meaning: Speaks of the pure cloud drifting freely through the sky with clear and unmistakable grace.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Ching (pure) and Wan (cloud). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese poetic tradition of cloud imagery for women expressing pure freedom.

cantonese.name: Wai Shing
cantonese.meaning: Carries the magnificent glory and the great triumph that comes from combining achievement with renown.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese male name combining Wai (magnificent) and Shing (glorious). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese naming tradition of compound names expressing magnificent glory.

cantonese.name: Hiu Kwan
cantonese.meaning: Paints the image of the wise and distinguished one whose understanding elevates them above others.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese female name combining Hiu (wise) and Kwan (distinguished). Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of wisdom-distinction compound names for women.

cantonese.name: Cheuk Lam
cantonese.meaning: Evokes the upright forest and the rectitude that grows as strong and natural as deep woodland.
cantonese.notes: A Cantonese name combining Cheuk (upright) and Lam (forest), used for both males and females. Common in Hong Kong; reflects the Cantonese tradition of combining moral uprightness with natural imagery.

tibetan.name: Tenzin
tibetan.meaning: Holds the teachings and preserves what is most sacred in an unbroken lineage of wisdom.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'holder of teachings,' extremely common across Tibet, Nepal, and the Tibetan diaspora. Strongly associated with Tibetan Buddhism; the Dalai Lama bears this as his personal name, making it one of the most beloved names in Tibetan culture.

tibetan.name: Dolma
tibetan.meaning: Evokes the liberating goddess whose compassionate power rescues those who call upon her.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name referring to Tara, the female bodhisattva of compassion and liberation in Tibetan Buddhism. Very common across Tibet and among Tibetan communities in Nepal and India; one of the most popular female names.

tibetan.name: Norbu
tibetan.meaning: Shines with the radiance of a precious jewel whose worth cannot be measured or surpassed.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'precious jewel' or 'gem.' Common across Tibet and among Tibetan Buddhist communities; jewel imagery is deeply significant in Tibetan Buddhism, referring to the Three Jewels of Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha.

tibetan.name: Pema
tibetan.meaning: Blooms in pure beauty rising above the murky depths that gave it life, unstained and radiant.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'lotus,' used for both males and females. Very common in Tibet, Bhutan, and Tibetan Buddhist communities throughout the Himalayas; the lotus is the most sacred flower in Tibetan Buddhism, symbolizing enlightenment.

tibetan.name: Karma
tibetan.meaning: Carries the weight of all actions, the great moral ledger written by every deed across time.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name used for both males and females, meaning 'action' or 'karma' in the Buddhist sense. Very common in Tibet, Bhutan, and among Tibetan Buddhists; reflects the central Buddhist concept of karma as the force shaping one's destiny.

tibetan.name: Yangchen
tibetan.meaning: Pours forth melodious sound like the goddess of music who fills the world with divine song.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name associated with Saraswati, the goddess of music and learning in the Tibetan Buddhist tradition. Common in Tibet and among Himalayan Buddhist communities; reflects the Tibetan reverence for the arts and learning.

tibetan.name: Lobsang
tibetan.meaning: Possesses the kind and noble heart whose goodness opens wide to receive wisdom freely.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'kind-hearted' or 'noble mind.' Very common in Tibet and Tibetan Buddhist communities; frequently given to monks and laypeople alike; many prominent Tibetan lamas bear this name.

tibetan.name: Dawa
tibetan.meaning: Glows with the soft silver light of the moon whose gentle cycles mark the passage of time.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'moon' or 'Monday,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet and Bhutan; children born on Monday are often given this name; reflects the importance of the lunar calendar in Tibetan Buddhist tradition.

tibetan.name: Sangmo
tibetan.meaning: Breathes the sweetness of something wholly good and pleasing in its gentle and pure quality.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'sweet' or 'good.' Common in Tibet and among Tibetan communities in the Himalayas; reflects the Tibetan tradition of names expressing pleasant and virtuous qualities for women.

tibetan.name: Gyatso
tibetan.meaning: Stretches to every horizon with the boundless depth of the ocean that holds all things within.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'ocean.' Common in Tibet and Tibetan Buddhist communities; the ocean symbolizes the boundlessness of wisdom and compassion in Tibetan Buddhism; several Dalai Lamas have borne this name as part of their full title.

tibetan.name: Choden
tibetan.meaning: Holds the sacred teachings close and carries them forward with devoted and reverent care.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'devoted to the teachings' or 'religious.' Common in Tibet and among Tibetan Buddhist communities; reflects the deep religious identity of Tibetan culture.

tibetan.name: Rigzin
tibetan.meaning: Grasps the deepest knowledge and holds the awareness that illuminates the nature of all things.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'holder of awareness' or 'knowledge holder,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet and the broader Tibetan Buddhist world; in Tibetan Buddhism, a rigzin is a master who holds the highest tantric knowledge.

tibetan.name: Sonam
tibetan.meaning: Accumulates the merit of good deeds that bring fortune and ripens into worthy blessing.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'merit' or 'virtuous,' used for both males and females. Very common across Tibet, Bhutan, and Tibetan communities in India and Nepal; reflects the Buddhist concept of merit as the foundation of a good life.

tibetan.name: Palden
tibetan.meaning: Bears the glorious splendor of one whose brilliance adorns everything around them magnificently.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'glorious' or 'possessing splendor.' Common in Tibet and Bhutan; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist tradition of names expressing divine glory and the radiance of merit.

tibetan.name: Kunzang
tibetan.meaning: Spreads goodness in every direction, filling all of existence with its generous and all-encompassing quality.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'all good' or 'good in every way,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet and Bhutan; the name Samantabhadra in Sanskrit; one of the most revered figures in Tibetan Buddhism.

tibetan.name: Dechen
tibetan.meaning: Rests in the great bliss that transcends all ordinary happiness and cannot be disturbed by anything.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'great bliss.' Very common in Tibet and Bhutan; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist concept of mahasukha, the supreme bliss of enlightenment; one of the most beloved female names in Tibetan culture.

tibetan.name: Tsewang
tibetan.meaning: Commands the power of long life, the vital force that sustains existence through every season.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'long-life power.' Common in Tibet and among Tibetan Buddhists; Amitayus, the deity of longevity, is invoked in Tibetan prayers; this name reflects the deep Tibetan cultural wish for long and meaningful life.

tibetan.name: Kelsang
tibetan.meaning: Brings the auspicious happiness whose fortunate quality opens the way to all good things.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'auspicious happiness' or 'good fortune,' used for both males and females. Very common in Tibet and Tibetan Buddhist communities; frequently given to children born under auspicious circumstances.

tibetan.name: Ngawang
tibetan.meaning: Wields the power of speech and carries the commanding authority of the sacred word.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'powerful speech' or 'lord of speech,' used primarily for males. Very common in Tibet and among Tibetan monks; speech is deeply sacred in Tibetan Buddhism, where mantras hold transformative power.

tibetan.name: Lhamo
tibetan.meaning: Shines with the divine radiance of a goddess whose sacred quality sets her apart from the ordinary.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'goddess.' Very common in Tibet; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist tradition of feminine divine imagery; Palden Lhamo is the fierce protector goddess of Tibet, making this name deeply significant.

tibetan.name: Jigme
tibetan.meaning: Walks without fear through every challenge, unbowed by danger because nothing can shake this inner stillness.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'fearless.' Very common in Tibet and Bhutan; reflects the Buddhist ideal of courage arising from the absence of ego-based fear; widely used by both laypeople and monks.

tibetan.name: Rinchen
tibetan.meaning: Holds the precious quality of a rare gem whose value enriches all who come near it.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'precious' or 'jewel,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet and Bhutan; jewel symbolism is central to Tibetan Buddhism, evoking the preciousness of the enlightened mind.

tibetan.name: Wangmo
tibetan.meaning: Carries the power of a queen whose commanding presence orders the world with authority.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'powerful woman' or 'queen.' Common in Tibet and Bhutan; the suffix -mo marks the female form; reflects the Tibetan tradition of feminine names combining power with womanhood.

tibetan.name: Namgyal
tibetan.meaning: Rises victorious over all obstacles, triumphant in the highest realm beyond all limitation.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'victorious in the sky' or 'all-victorious,' used for both males and females. Very common in Tibet, Bhutan, and Ladakh; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist aspiration for spiritual victory over the forces of ignorance.

tibetan.name: Tsomo
tibetan.meaning: Flows with the abundance of a lake whose still and deep waters sustain all life around it.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name related to the word for lake. Common in Tibet; the great sacred lakes of Tibet are venerated in Buddhist and Bon traditions; this name connects a woman to the life-giving depth of these waters.

tibetan.name: Dorje
tibetan.meaning: Strikes with the indestructible clarity of the thunderbolt that cuts through all delusion at once.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'thunderbolt' or 'diamond scepter.' Very common in Tibet and Bhutan; the dorje (vajra) is the most important ritual object in Tibetan Buddhism, symbolizing indestructible truth and method.

tibetan.name: Chime
tibetan.meaning: Stretches beyond the reach of death into the boundless expanse of that which never ends.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'immortal' or 'deathless,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet and Bhutan; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist aspiration toward liberation from the cycle of birth and death.

tibetan.name: Pemba
tibetan.meaning: Shines with the steady golden glow of the planet that rules the day of its birth.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'Saturn' or 'Saturday,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet; children born on Saturday are often given this name; reflects the Tibetan tradition of naming children after the day of the week of their birth.

tibetan.name: Gonpo
tibetan.meaning: Protects with the fierce compassion of the guardian who shields all beings from harm.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name referring to Mahakala, the great protector deity of Tibetan Buddhism. Common in Tibet; Mahakala is one of the most important protector deities; this name places a child under his powerful protection.

tibetan.name: Nyima
tibetan.meaning: Radiates the warmth and life of the sun whose generous light sustains everything below.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'sun' or 'Sunday,' used for both males and females. Very common in Tibet; children born on Sunday are often given this name; the sun holds deep significance in Tibetan cosmology and daily life.

tibetan.name: Choekyi
tibetan.meaning: Embodies the teachings in their purest essence, being itself a vessel of the sacred truth.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'of the Dharma' or 'religious essence.' Common in Tibet and among Tibetan Buddhist communities; reflects the central role of the Dharma in Tibetan identity and culture.

tibetan.name: Gyurme
tibetan.meaning: Stands immovable and unchanging, fixed beyond all transformation and beyond the reach of time.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'unchanging' or 'immutable.' Common in Tibet and Bhutan; reflects the Buddhist concept of the unchanging nature of ultimate reality beneath all apparent transformation.

tibetan.name: Passang
tibetan.meaning: Favors its bearer with the auspicious quality of the day on which all good things begin.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'Friday' or 'auspicious,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet; children born on Friday are often given this name; reflects the Tibetan tradition of linking names to the days of the week.

tibetan.name: Wangchuk
tibetan.meaning: Commands great power and authority whose sovereign quality rules over all that surrounds it.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'powerful lord' or 'mighty sovereign.' Common in Tibet and Bhutan; the name of the royal dynasty of Bhutan; reflects the Tibetan tradition of kingly power names for men.

tibetan.name: Tsamchoe
tibetan.meaning: Consecrates the space of practice with the holy quality of one devoted to the highest truth.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name related to sacred practice and religious devotion. Used in Tibet and among Tibetan Buddhist communities; reflects the deep tradition of female religious practitioners in Tibetan Buddhism.

tibetan.name: Thinley
tibetan.meaning: Performs the enlightened activity that benefits all beings and fulfills the purpose of existence.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'enlightened activity' or 'Buddha activity,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet and Bhutan; refers to the spontaneous compassionate activity of a Buddha benefiting all sentient beings.

tibetan.name: Kunsang
tibetan.meaning: Fills every corner of existence with an excellent goodness that leaves nothing untouched.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'all excellent' or 'universally good,' used for both males and females. Common in Tibet; a variant spelling of Kunzang; reflects the Tibetan aspiration for complete and universal goodness.

tibetan.name: Zangmo
tibetan.meaning: Embodies the excellent and virtuous quality of a woman whose goodness is complete in every way.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'virtuous woman' or 'excellent woman.' Common in Tibet; the suffix -mo marks the feminine form; reflects the Tibetan tradition of compound names expressing complete feminine virtue.

tibetan.name: Tsultrim
tibetan.meaning: Lives within the discipline and moral code that shapes a life of genuine integrity.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'discipline' or 'ethics,' referring to monastic or lay ethical conduct. Used for both males and females in Tibet; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist emphasis on moral discipline as the foundation of the path to liberation.

tibetan.name: Mingyur
tibetan.meaning: Rests in the unchanging clarity of one whose nature remains steady through all of life's transformations.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'unchanging' or 'immovable mind.' Common in Tibet and Bhutan; reflects the Buddhist meditation ideal of a stable, unshakeable quality of awareness.

tibetan.name: Drukpa
tibetan.meaning: Roars with the thunderous power of the dragon whose voice shakes heaven and earth alike.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan male name meaning 'thunder dragon' or 'of the dragon people.' Used in Tibet and Bhutan; the dragon is the national symbol of Bhutan; this name is associated with the Drukpa Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism.

tibetan.name: Seldon
tibetan.meaning: Illuminates the path ahead with a clear and selfless light offered freely to guide all those who follow.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'clear light' or 'illuminating.' Common in Tibet and among Tibetan diaspora communities; clear light is a central concept in Tibetan Buddhist meditation and the nature of mind.

tibetan.name: Thubten
tibetan.meaning: Carries the teachings of the capable one whose doctrine has the power to transform all who receive it.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan name meaning 'of the Buddha's teachings' or 'doctrine of the capable one.' Common in Tibet; often given to children with strong religious blessings; reflects the central importance of Buddhist doctrine in Tibetan life.

tibetan.name: Khandro
tibetan.meaning: Moves through the sky with the freedom of a sky-dancer whose wisdom flies beyond all boundaries.
tibetan.notes: A Tibetan female name meaning 'sky dancer' or 'dakini.' Common in Tibet and Bhutan; the dakini is a female tantric deity representing the wisdom energy of enlightenment; a deeply auspicious name in the Vajrayana tradition.

uyghur.name: Abdulla
uyghur.meaning: Serves the divine with complete devotion, a willing servant before the source of all creation.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'servant of God.' Very common among Uyghurs in Xinjiang, China, and the Uyghur diaspora; reflects the deep Islamic identity of the Uyghur people.

uyghur.name: Gülnar
uyghur.meaning: Blooms with the fiery beauty of the pomegranate flower whose vivid color lights the garden.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name meaning 'pomegranate flower.' Very common among Uyghur women; reflects both Persian literary influence and the Central Asian cultural significance of the pomegranate as a symbol of fertility and beauty.

uyghur.name: Mämtimin
uyghur.meaning: Holds the trust and faithfulness of one who is completely reliable and honest in all dealings.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name derived from Arabic Mu'tamin, meaning 'trustworthy.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic naming tradition that emphasizes moral reliability.

uyghur.name: Zulpiya
uyghur.meaning: Gleams with the radiant beauty of the planet Venus as it burns bright in the evening sky.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name derived from Arabic Zulfa or the word for planet Venus. Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Persian and Arabic literary tradition of naming women after celestial beauty.

uyghur.name: Turghun
uyghur.meaning: Stands firm and settled in one place, stable as the earth that does not shift beneath one's feet.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name meaning 'settled' or 'stable.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Turkic naming tradition of names expressing steadfastness and reliable grounding.

uyghur.name: Mayram
uyghur.meaning: Carries the grace and purity of the revered mother whose blessed life became a model for all time.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name, the Uyghur form of Mary/Maryam. Common among Uyghur women; reflects both the Islamic veneration of the Virgin Mary as a prophet's mother and the broader Central Asian tradition of this beloved name.

uyghur.name: Ilham
uyghur.meaning: Receives the divine inspiration that arrives like a breath from beyond and quickens the spirit.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur name of Arabic origin meaning 'inspiration,' used for both males and females. Common among Uyghurs; reflects the Islamic concept of divine inspiration and the Sufi tradition of spiritual creativity.

uyghur.name: Nurgül
uyghur.meaning: Glows with the light of a luminous flower whose radiance brightens everything that surrounds it.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name combining Arabic nur (light) and Persian gül (rose/flower). Very common among Uyghur women; reflects the mixed Persian-Arabic linguistic heritage of Uyghur naming culture.

uyghur.name: Muhemmed
uyghur.meaning: Deserves the praise that rises from a character so admirable that all who see it give thanks.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name, the Uyghur form of Muhammad. Common among Uyghur men; reflects the deep Islamic identity of the Uyghur people and the universal Muslim practice of naming sons after the Prophet.

uyghur.name: Dilnoza
uyghur.meaning: Delights the heart with a sweet and charming quality that refreshes all who come near.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name combining Persian dil (heart) and noza (tender/sweet). Very common among Uyghur women; reflects the Persian literary influence on Uyghur female naming, where heart imagery is central.

uyghur.name: Tursun
uyghur.meaning: Endures with the strength of one whose life continues and persists through all that comes against it.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur name meaning 'may he/she live' or 'long-lived,' used for both males and females. Common among Uyghurs; reflects the Turkic tradition of names expressing the wish for survival and long life.

uyghur.name: Muniram
uyghur.meaning: Shines with the brilliance of the luminous one whose light cannot be hidden or contained.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic origin related to munira (luminous). Used among Uyghur women; reflects the Islamic and Persian literary tradition of light-names for women.

uyghur.name: Alim
uyghur.meaning: Possesses the knowledge of one who has learned deeply and comprehends the nature of things.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'learned' or 'scholar.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic tradition of honoring knowledge and scholarship; the religious scholar (alim) holds high status in Uyghur culture.

uyghur.name: Hulida
uyghur.meaning: Carries the eternal quality of something that endures beyond the ordinary span of time.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name derived from Arabic Khulud, meaning 'eternal' or 'immortal.' Used among Uyghur women; reflects the Arabic influence on Uyghur female naming through Islam.

uyghur.name: Muxtar
uyghur.meaning: Chooses its own course freely, selected and self-determining in its path through life.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'chosen' or 'autonomous.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic naming tradition emphasizing divine selection and individual determination.

uyghur.name: Feride
uyghur.meaning: Stands apart as unique and singular, a precious stone without equal or peer in all the world.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic origin meaning 'unique' or 'incomparable.' Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Persian and Arabic literary tradition of names celebrating singular, incomparable beauty.

uyghur.name: Gheyret
uyghur.meaning: Burns with the zeal and diligence of one whose passionate effort drives every endeavor forward.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name meaning 'zeal,' 'effort,' or 'diligence.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Central Asian cultural value of energetic striving and passionate commitment to one's work.

uyghur.name: Shirin
uyghur.meaning: Tastes as sweet as something that delights the senses with an irresistible and gentle pleasure.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Persian origin meaning 'sweet.' Common among Uyghur women; associated with the legendary beauty Shirin in classical Persian literature; reflects the deep Persian literary influence on Uyghur culture.

uyghur.name: Abdurehim
uyghur.meaning: Kneels before the merciful one, a devoted servant of the source of all compassion and grace.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'servant of the Merciful One.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic tradition of theophoric names combining abd (servant) with a divine attribute.

uyghur.name: Mahire
uyghur.meaning: Masters every skill with the expertise of one who has practiced until perfection comes naturally.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic origin meaning 'skilled' or 'expert.' Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Arabic influence on Uyghur naming through the long history of Islamic education in the region.

uyghur.name: Hamit
uyghur.meaning: Praises with an outpouring of gratitude and admiration directed toward the worthy and the great.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'praising' or 'praiseworthy.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic naming tradition honoring gratitude and praise as virtues.

uyghur.name: Güljahan
uyghur.meaning: Fills the entire world with the beauty of a flower whose fragrance spreads across every horizon.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name combining Persian gül (flower) and jahan (world). Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Persian cosmopolitan literary imagination that envisions feminine beauty as filling the whole world.

uyghur.name: Säyid
uyghur.meaning: Leads with the authority of a master whose presence commands respect and willing obedience.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'master' or 'lord.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic tradition of honorific names and the significance of noble lineage in Uyghur society.

uyghur.name: Patime
uyghur.meaning: Carries the weaning away from what was nourishing, marking the transition into a new and fuller life.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name, the Uyghur form of Fatima. Very common among Uyghur women; honors the daughter of the Prophet Muhammad, one of the most revered women in Islam; a deeply significant name throughout the Muslim world.

uyghur.name: Erkin
uyghur.meaning: Evokes freedom and boundlessness, like moving unhindered beneath an endless sky.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur name meaning 'free' or 'independent,' used for both males and females. Common among Uyghurs; reflects the Turkic nomadic heritage and the cultural value of freedom that runs deeply through Uyghur identity.

uyghur.name: Ziyoda
uyghur.meaning: Radiates an extra brightness that surpasses the ordinary light and adds something beyond the expected.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic origin meaning 'surplus of light' or 'extra radiance.' Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Arabic and Persian tradition of light-names celebrated in Islamic literary culture.

uyghur.name: Memet
uyghur.meaning: Carries the praise-worthy quality of one whose admirable nature merits the gratitude of all.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name, a colloquial Uyghur form of Muhammad. Very common among Uyghur men; reflects the widespread practice of naming children after the Prophet in various phonetic adaptations across Muslim Central Asia.

uyghur.name: Reyhan
uyghur.meaning: Breathes the sweet fragrance of basil, the blessed herb whose scent fills the air with pleasure.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic-Persian origin meaning 'basil' or 'sweet-smelling herb.' Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Persian poetic tradition of plant-names evoking fragrant beauty.

uyghur.name: Ötkür
uyghur.meaning: Cuts through obstacles with the sharpness of a blade that finds the direct path without hesitation.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name meaning 'sharp' or 'keen.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Turkic naming tradition of names expressing mental sharpness and decisive clarity; associated with the celebrated Uyghur novelist Abdurrehim Ötkür.

uyghur.name: Mewlude
uyghur.meaning: Celebrates the birth of the beloved one whose arrival into the world was a gift beyond measure.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name related to the Arabic mawlid, the celebration of the Prophet's birthday. Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Islamic tradition of the mawlid celebration as a central event in Muslim religious life.

uyghur.name: Perhat
uyghur.meaning: Chips away at the mountain with the tireless devotion of one whose love drives every stroke.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name, the Uyghur form of Farhad. Common among Uyghur men; associated with the legendary hero Farhad from Persian classical literature who carved through a mountain for love; deeply embedded in Uyghur literary culture.

uyghur.name: Zühra
uyghur.meaning: Blazes with the radiant brilliance of the morning star that outshines all others before the dawn.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic origin meaning 'Venus' or 'radiant.' Common among Uyghur women; the planet Venus as morning star is a powerful image of feminine beauty in classical Arabic and Persian poetry.

uyghur.name: Yüsüp
uyghur.meaning: Carries the beauty and virtue that made one man the most beloved of his generation.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name, the Uyghur form of Joseph/Yusuf. Common among Uyghur men; the Prophet Yusuf is celebrated in the Quran as the most beautiful of all men and a paragon of patience; this is among the most beloved names in Islamic culture.

uyghur.name: Nozugum
uyghur.meaning: Glows with the tender and delicate radiance of a gentle light that warms without dazzling.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name meaning 'tender light' or 'soft radiance.' Used among Uyghur women; reflects the Uyghur tradition of poetic feminine names drawing on light and delicacy imagery.

uyghur.name: Kamil
uyghur.meaning: Achieves the full and complete perfection of one whose character leaves nothing lacking or wanting.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'perfect' or 'complete.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Islamic ideal of striving toward completeness of character.

uyghur.name: Hüriyät
uyghur.meaning: Breathes the open air of freedom, moving through life unbound by any chain or constraint.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name meaning 'freedom' or 'liberty.' Used among Uyghur women; reflects the Uyghur cultural value of freedom, which carries profound significance in both the nomadic heritage and the modern political consciousness of the Uyghur people.

uyghur.name: Tohtison
uyghur.meaning: Stands completely settled and whole, a person who has arrived fully into their own being.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name meaning 'stand complete' or 'fully settled.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Turkic naming tradition of names expressing completeness and settled stability.

uyghur.name: Munire
uyghur.meaning: Shines with its own inner brilliance, radiating light outward from a source that cannot be dimmed.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name of Arabic origin meaning 'luminous' or 'radiant.' Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Islamic literary tradition of light-names expressing divine illumination.

uyghur.name: Bextiyar
uyghur.meaning: Walks in the bright light of fortune, a lucky and favored companion of all good things.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Persian origin meaning 'fortunate' or 'lucky.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Persian cultural influence on Uyghur naming through centuries of cultural exchange along the Silk Road.

uyghur.name: Qumri
uyghur.meaning: Calls out with the sweet cooing voice of the dove whose gentle song fills the air with longing.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name meaning 'turtledove.' Common among Uyghur women; the turtledove is a beloved bird in Central Asian and Persian poetry, associated with sweet-voiced feminine beauty and tender longing.

uyghur.name: Obul
uyghur.meaning: Receives the child as a gift and offering brought to gladden those who hoped and waited long.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur name meaning 'the child received' or 'offered child,' used for both males and females. Common among Uyghurs; reflects the Turkic Islamic tradition of names expressing gratitude for the arrival of a longed-for child.

uyghur.name: Tursungül
uyghur.meaning: Blooms and endures, the living flower that continues to blossom season after season without fading.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur female name combining tursun (may she live/endure) and gül (flower). Common among Uyghur women; reflects the Uyghur naming tradition of compound names combining wish-names with floral beauty imagery.

uyghur.name: Shewket
uyghur.meaning: Commands the splendor of great power whose magnificent authority inspires reverence in all who witness it.
uyghur.notes: A Uyghur male name of Arabic origin meaning 'power' or 'magnificence.' Common among Uyghur men; reflects the Arabic tradition of names expressing regal authority and splendor.

japanese.name: Haruto
japanese.meaning: Leaps upward like the sun at spring's arrival, a brightness that soars into the open sky.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining haru (spring/sunlight) and to (soar/person). One of the most popular male names in Japan in recent years; reflects the contemporary Japanese naming trend of nature-combined-with-aspiration names.

japanese.name: Yui
japanese.meaning: Ties together what was separate, binding hearts and lives in a single, unbreakable cord.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'bind' or 'connect.' Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese cultural value of connection and interdependence; consistently ranks among the top female names in Japan.

japanese.name: Sota
japanese.meaning: Sweeps across the landscape like a great wind whose vast breadth carries freshness everywhere.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining so (large/extensive) and ta (thick/great). Very popular in Japan; one of the most common male names for children born in recent decades.

japanese.name: Akari
japanese.meaning: Glows with a gentle luminosity that brightens the surrounding space without blinding anyone near.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'light' or 'brightness.' Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese tradition of light-names for women; consistently ranks among top female names.

japanese.name: Ren
japanese.meaning: Rises pure and beautiful from the water, a lotus whose perfection is born from patient stillness.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'lotus' or 'love,' used for both males and females. Very popular in Japan; the lotus carries Buddhist significance of purity and enlightenment; the name also carries connotations of romantic feeling.

japanese.name: Hana
japanese.meaning: Opens in a single perfect moment of blossoming beauty that fills the world around it with fragrance.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'flower.' Very popular in Japan; reflects the deep Japanese cultural love of flowers and the aesthetic of brief, beautiful moments; the cherry blossom is the most iconic expression of this feeling.

japanese.name: Kenji
japanese.meaning: Holds both the sharpness of the blade and the wisdom of the scholar in one disciplined being.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining ken (healthy/strong/sword) and ji (second son/rule). Common in Japan; reflects the traditional Japanese ideal of combining martial and intellectual virtues in one person.

japanese.name: Yuna
japanese.meaning: Embraces with the gentle warmth of something kind and nurturing whose touch brings quiet comfort.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'gentle' or 'bind together.' Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese naming tradition of soft, gentle names for women; also influenced by the trend of names ending in the feminine -na.

japanese.name: Ryota
japanese.meaning: Refreshes the spirit like a great cool stream whose abundance nourishes everything along its banks.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining ryo (cool/refreshing) and ta (big/great). Very popular in Japan; one of the most common male names for recent generations.

japanese.name: Sakura
japanese.meaning: Bursts into brief and breathtaking bloom, offering its full beauty openly and then releasing it without regret.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'cherry blossom.' Common in Japan; the cherry blossom is the national flower and the most powerful symbol of Japanese aesthetics, representing the beauty of transient things.

japanese.name: Kaito
japanese.meaning: Soars above the ocean on boundless wings, the sea and the sky joining in one endless expanse.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining kai (sea) and to (soar/person). Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese cultural connection to the ocean and the aspiration for freedom and height.

japanese.name: Aoi
japanese.meaning: Spreads the deep blue of the hollyhock across the eye, a color both vivid and serene.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'hollyhock' or 'blue,' used for both males and females. Common in Japan; the hollyhock is the symbol of the Aoi Festival in Kyoto; the name also evokes the calming depth of blue.

japanese.name: Takumi
japanese.meaning: Shapes things with the patient precision of a master craftsperson whose hands know exactly what is needed.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name meaning 'artisan' or 'skillful.' Common in Japan; reflects the deep Japanese cultural reverence for craftsmanship and the mastery that comes from devoted practice.

japanese.name: Miyu
japanese.meaning: Flows with the beautiful gentleness of a stream whose soft current carries something precious along.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name combining mi (beautiful) and yu (gentle/superior). Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese naming tradition of compound names combining beauty with gentle or superior qualities.

japanese.name: Hiroshi
japanese.meaning: Opens wide and generous like the great sky or sea that holds everything within its vast embrace.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name meaning 'generous' or 'tolerant.' Common in Japan, especially among older generations; reflects the traditional Japanese value of broad-mindedness and the virtue of a wide, inclusive heart.

japanese.name: Koharu
japanese.meaning: Brings the warm golden days of little summer when autumn holds its breath before the cold arrives.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'little spring' or 'Indian summer.' Popular in Japan; evokes the brief warm period in early autumn when summer lingers; reflects the Japanese sensitivity to seasonal transitions and fleeting beauty.

japanese.name: Daiki
japanese.meaning: Shines with the great and brilliant radiance of one whose noble light is unmistakable and generous.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining dai (great) and ki (radiance/noble). Common in Japan; reflects the Japanese tradition of compound names expressing great and brilliant qualities.

japanese.name: Nanami
japanese.meaning: Breathes with the living energy of seven seas whose combined vastness becomes something beautiful.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'seven seas' or combining na/nana (seven) and mi (sea/beauty). Popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese poetic tradition of combining numbers with natural imagery.

japanese.name: Riku
japanese.meaning: Stands on solid ground whose firm earth provides the stable foundation for everything built upon it.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'land' or 'shore,' used primarily for males. Popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese sense of groundedness and the importance of the land in an island culture.

japanese.name: Hinata
japanese.meaning: Turns always toward the warmth of the sun, a flower-like facing of the brightest and most nourishing light.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'sunny place' or 'facing the sun,' used for both males and females. Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese cultural appreciation of sunlight and warmth, especially after the cold months.

japanese.name: Shota
japanese.meaning: Rises up like a great flight into the wide open sky, soaring beyond what ordinary wings can reach.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining sho (soar/fly) and ta (thick/great). Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese tradition of aspiration names combining the idea of upward movement with greatness.

japanese.name: Satsuki
japanese.meaning: Opens in the month when rain falls most softly and the earth receives what it has long been waiting for.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'May' (the fifth month). Common in Japan; reflects the Japanese tradition of naming children after the month or season of their birth; May is associated with the fresh green of new growth.

japanese.name: Yusei
japanese.meaning: Carries the brightness of an entire star system, a light that comes from the distant heart of the universe.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining yu (gentleness/excellence) and sei (star/planet). Popular in Japan; reflects the contemporary Japanese trend of cosmic names drawing on astronomical imagery.

japanese.name: Kotone
japanese.meaning: Rings with the resonant sound of the plucked string, each note hanging in the air before it fades.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name combining koto (traditional Japanese harp) and ne (sound). Popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese cultural tradition of the koto as an instrument of refinement and classical elegance.

japanese.name: Makoto
japanese.meaning: Holds true to what is real and genuine, living with the sincerity that makes all words trustworthy.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'sincerity' or 'truth,' used for both males and females. Common in Japan; reflects the Japanese Confucian value of makoto (sincerity) as one of the foundational virtues of the ideal person.

japanese.name: Himari
japanese.meaning: Curves around its stem like the bright globe of a sunflower turning its open face toward the light.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'sunflower' or combining hi (sun) and mari (ball/circle). Very popular in Japan in recent years; reflects the Japanese aesthetic appreciation of flowers and the warming quality of sunlight.

japanese.name: Taiga
japanese.meaning: Rushes with the force of a great river whose current shapes the landscape around it over time.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining tai (big) and ga (river/elegant). Popular in Japan; the great river imagery evokes power and enduring flow; reflects the contemporary Japanese trend of nature-combined names for boys.

japanese.name: Noa
japanese.meaning: Rests in an inner stillness from which all movement and action arise without disturbance.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'love' or reflecting the sense of gentle harmony. Very popular in Japan in recent years; one of the top female names; its short, soft sound fits the Japanese preference for melodic simplicity.

japanese.name: Hayato
japanese.meaning: Strikes swiftly with the sudden precision of a falcon diving before the eye can follow its movement.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining haya (swift) and to (person/falcon). Popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese tradition of names expressing swiftness and decisive action, associated with the speed of the falcon.

japanese.name: Ichika
japanese.meaning: Opens into a single perfect flower, the one blossom whose complete beauty needs no companion.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name combining ichi (one) and ka (flower/fragrance). Popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese aesthetic of finding perfection in singular, concentrated beauty.

japanese.name: Kosuke
japanese.meaning: Lifts up its wings to help and support what rises, clearing the sky for what soars above.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining ko (assist/light) and suke (help/support). Common in Japan; reflects the Japanese cultural value of support and assisting others' rise.

japanese.name: Yuki
japanese.meaning: Falls in silent white abundance, covering the world in a pure and equal brightness from above.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'snow' or 'happiness/fortune,' used for both males and females. Very common in Japan; snow imagery evokes purity, silence, and the Japanese aesthetic of mono no aware—the beauty of transient things.

japanese.name: Asahi
japanese.meaning: Rises in the east with the first fire of morning, painting the sky before the world has fully woken.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'morning sun' or 'rising sun.' Common in Japan; the rising sun is the central symbol of Japanese national identity; this name evokes the energy and hope of new beginnings.

japanese.name: Kokoro
japanese.meaning: Beats at the center of everything felt and known, the source from which all true response arises.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'heart' or 'mind/spirit,' used for both males and females. Popular in Japan; the concept of kokoro is central to Japanese philosophy, encompassing the emotional, intellectual, and spiritual center of a person.

japanese.name: Ryusei
japanese.meaning: Blazes across the dark sky like a meteor whose brilliant streak leaves a line of wonder behind it.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining ryu (dragon/meteor) and sei (star). Popular in Japan; the meteor dragon imagery evokes dramatic, powerful, and fleeting brilliance; reflects the contemporary Japanese trend of cosmic and mythological names.

japanese.name: Kanna
japanese.meaning: Blooms in late summer with the vivid purple of the wild aster whose colors deepen as the light fades.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'summer flower' or associated with the summer aster. Common in Japan; reflects the Japanese sensitivity to seasonal flowers and the unique beauty of each period of the year.

japanese.name: Fumiya
japanese.meaning: Moves through the world with the grace and learning of one who has been shaped by culture and writing.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining fumi (writing/culture) and ya (person). Common in Japan; reflects the traditional Japanese respect for literary learning and the cultivation that comes from engaging with written culture.

japanese.name: Tsumugi
japanese.meaning: Draws out fine threads and weaves them together into a cloth of subtle and enduring beauty.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'spun silk' or 'pongee cloth.' Popular in Japan in recent years; reflects the Japanese cultural tradition of textile craftsmanship and the beauty of patient, careful making.

japanese.name: Naoki
japanese.meaning: Stands as straight and honest as a tree growing upward without twisting toward any easy advantage.
japanese.notes: A Japanese male name combining nao (honest/straight) and ki (tree/radiance). Common in Japan; reflects the Japanese traditional value of honesty and moral straightness as natural virtues.

japanese.name: Mizuki
japanese.meaning: Gleams with a cool and liquid beauty like moonlight touching the surface of still water at night.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'beautiful moon' or combining mizu (water) and ki (gleam). Very popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese poetic tradition of pairing water and moonlight as the ultimate image of serene beauty.

japanese.name: Ibuki
japanese.meaning: Exhales with the fresh vitality of the first breath taken on a cold mountain morning.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'breath of life' or 'vitality,' used for both males and females. Popular in Japan; evokes the feeling of cold, clean mountain air; reflects the Japanese connection between nature and living energy.

japanese.name: Towa
japanese.meaning: Stretches through all of time without diminishment, enduring beyond what ordinary things can hold.
japanese.notes: A Japanese name meaning 'eternal' or 'forever,' used for both males and females. Popular in Japan; reflects the Japanese tradition of names expressing enduring qualities that transcend the brevity of individual life.

japanese.name: Kanon
japanese.meaning: Sounds with the harmonic resonance of notes arranged in perfect relationship to one another.
japanese.notes: A Japanese female name meaning 'flower sound' or associated with musical harmony. Popular in Japan; also shares its sound with Kannon, the bodhisattva of compassion, adding a spiritual dimension to this musical name.

korean.name: Jisoo
korean.meaning: Gathers the wisdom of the earth and the clarity of still water into one focused and capable spirit.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining ji (wisdom/earth) and soo (water/excellent). Very popular in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names combining virtuous and natural elements.

korean.name: Minjun
korean.meaning: Possesses a quick and talented brilliance whose exceptional quality rises above the ordinary easily.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining min (quick/clever) and jun (talented/handsome). Very popular in Korea; one of the top male names; reflects the Korean Confucian tradition of valuing intellectual quickness and talent.

korean.name: Seoyeon
korean.meaning: Promises something auspicious and beautiful, like the good omen at the beginning of a worthy endeavor.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining seo (auspicious omen/beginning) and yeon (beautiful/lotus). Very popular in Korea; one of the top female names; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names combining auspicious beginnings with beauty.

korean.name: Hyunjae
korean.meaning: Shines with a bright and present wisdom that illuminates what is happening right now.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining hyun (bright/wise) and jae (talent/present). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean naming tradition of combining intellectual brightness with present ability.

korean.name: Chaeyeon
korean.meaning: Blooms with a colorful and delicate beauty whose graceful presence brightens everything around it.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining chae (colorful/vibrant) and yeon (graceful/beautiful). Popular in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound female names combining vivid color with elegant grace.

korean.name: Donghyun
korean.meaning: Illuminates the east with a bright wisdom that rises like the sun to light everything before it.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining dong (east) and hyun (bright/wise). Common in Korea; east is an auspicious direction in Korean cosmology, associated with sunrise and new beginnings.

korean.name: Yuna
korean.meaning: Holds the gentleness of the moon whose soft light nourishes without overwhelming those it touches.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining yu (gentle/moon) and na (elegant). Very popular in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of soft, melodic names for women with lunar and gentle imagery.

korean.name: Seungwon
korean.meaning: Lifts up what is wished for and carries it to completion through the force of persistent effort.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining seung (rise/win) and won (wish/desire). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean cultural aspiration to achieve what is desired through determined effort and rising above obstacles.

korean.name: Jiyeon
korean.meaning: Weaves wisdom and beauty together like a lotus growing in still water toward the light above.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining ji (wisdom) and yeon (lotus/beautiful). Popular in Korea; reflects the Korean Confucian and Buddhist tradition of combining wisdom with natural beauty.

korean.name: Taehyung
korean.meaning: Spreads great brightness in all directions, a vast luminosity that fills the entire sky.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining tae (great/big) and hyung (bright). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names combining greatness with luminous quality.

korean.name: Areum
korean.meaning: Captures the essence of beauty itself, the pure quality that makes something lovely to behold.
korean.notes: A Korean female name meaning 'beautiful' in native Korean. Common in Korea; reflects the modern trend of using native Korean words as names rather than Sino-Korean compounds; one of the most purely Korean-sounding names.

korean.name: Junhyuk
korean.meaning: Combines outstanding talent with the luminous strength whose combined brilliance commands respect.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining jun (talented/handsome) and hyuk (bright/magnificent). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean Confucian tradition of compound names expressing combined talents.

korean.name: Hayoon
korean.meaning: Breathes with the auspicious grace of something genuinely fine whose fortunate quality comes naturally.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining ha (auspicious/grand) and yoon (grace/allow). Popular in Korea; reflects the Korean naming tradition of names expressing fortunate and graceful qualities.

korean.name: Woojin
korean.meaning: Enriches what surrounds it like the fertile rain that falls on the precious gem of the earth.
korean.notes: A Korean name combining woo (rain/universe) and jin (precious/true), used for both males and females. Very common in Korea; one of the most popular name combinations reflecting the Korean value of precious things nurtured by nature.

korean.name: Narae
korean.meaning: Opens out like wings spreading wide and free, ready to rise and move through the open air.
korean.notes: A Korean female name meaning 'wings' in native Korean. Popular in Korea; reflects the modern trend of native Korean names expressing freedom and the aspiration to soar; a distinctly poetic and natural sound.

korean.name: Jihoon
korean.meaning: Holds a brilliant and learned virtue whose cultivated wisdom shines with the marks of long study.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining ji (wisdom/virtue) and hoon (cultivated/learned). Very common in Korea; reflects the Confucian Korean tradition of valuing cultivation through learning as the highest form of virtue.

korean.name: Somi
korean.meaning: Carries the small and lovely beauty of something delicate and charming in its gentle entirety.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining so (small/plain) and mi (beautiful). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean aesthetic tradition of appreciating subtle, understated beauty rather than dramatic grandeur.

korean.name: Kyungmin
korean.meaning: Gathers in the brightness of an auspicious intelligence that moves quickly through what it touches.
korean.notes: A Korean name combining kyung (auspicious/bright) and min (quick/clever), used for both males and females. Common in Korea; reflects the Confucian Korean tradition of names combining good omens with intellectual quickness.

korean.name: Boreum
korean.meaning: Rises full and luminous above the horizon like the spring moon that fills the night with soft white light.
korean.notes: A Korean female name meaning 'full moon of spring' in native Korean. Common in Korea; evokes the first full moon of the lunar new year, which is one of the most celebrated moments in the Korean calendar.

korean.name: Taeyang
korean.meaning: Burns with the great solar energy of the sun whose heat and light sustain all living things.
korean.notes: A Korean male name meaning 'great sun' or combining tae (great) and yang (sun). Common in Korea; solar imagery represents life-giving power and the highest masculine aspiration in Korean naming tradition.

korean.name: Dahye
korean.meaning: Carries a generous and reaching virtue whose goodness extends toward everyone it encounters.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining da (much/reaching) and hye (wisdom/virtue). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean Confucian tradition of names expressing abundant moral virtue.

korean.name: Insu
korean.meaning: Leads with the benevolence of the exemplary person whose kind authority earns the trust of all.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining in (benevolence/person) and su (water/excellent). Common in Korea; reflects the Confucian concept of in (ren), benevolent humanity, as the central virtue of the ideal person.

korean.name: Sohyun
korean.meaning: Illuminates with a modest and refined wisdom whose gentle brightness never overwhelms its surroundings.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining so (small/plain) and hyun (bright/wise). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean aesthetic of combining quiet modesty with genuine intellectual brightness.

korean.name: Yunseo
korean.meaning: Begins under a gracious and auspicious sign, its very origin marked by harmony and good fortune.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining yoon (grace/allow) and seo (auspicious omen). Very popular in Korea; one of the top female names; reflects the Korean tradition of names expressing gracious and fortunate beginnings.

korean.name: Chanwoo
korean.meaning: Shines with praise and the great cosmic abundance that falls like nourishing rain on what is worthy.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining chan (praise/brilliant) and woo (rain/universe). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean naming tradition of compound names joining acknowledgment of excellence with natural abundance.

korean.name: Haerin
korean.meaning: Gathers the grandeur of a forest into itself, a dignified and auspicious depth of nature.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining hae (sea/grand) and rin (forest/neighbor). Popular in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names drawing on natural imagery from both sea and land.

korean.name: Yunho
korean.meaning: Spreads a gracious grandeur outward like a great lake reflecting the sky above it clearly.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining yun (grace) and ho (lake/grand). Common in Korea; the great lake imagery suggests expansive, calm grandeur; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names evoking natural majesty.

korean.name: Naeun
korean.meaning: Unfolds into an elegant and delicate grace like a petal opening in its own time without being forced.
korean.notes: A Korean female name meaning 'graceful' or combining na/nae (I/gentle) and eun (grace/silver). Popular in Korea; reflects the Korean aesthetic tradition of names expressing gentle, unforced elegance.

korean.name: Sejun
korean.meaning: Establishes the brilliance of an outstanding talent that rises above its generation with distinction.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining se (generation/world) and jun (talented/handsome). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean naming tradition of names connecting individual talent to generational achievement.

korean.name: Miso
korean.meaning: Blooms with a quiet smile whose gentle expression opens up the space between two people warmly.
korean.notes: A Korean female name meaning 'smile' in native Korean. Popular in Korea; reflects the modern trend of native Korean word-names expressing emotional warmth and the simple beauty of human connection.

korean.name: Hyoshin
korean.meaning: Carries the filial trust that shines within a family as its most sincere and deeply felt bond.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining hyo (filial piety) and shin (trust/sincerity). Common in Korea; reflects the Confucian Korean tradition of filial piety as a cardinal virtue, the foundation of all other moral qualities.

korean.name: Soyeon
korean.meaning: Opens into a plain and beautiful grace like an unadorned flower whose simplicity is its finest quality.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining so (plain/small) and yeon (beautiful/graceful). Very common in Korea; reflects the Korean aesthetic tradition of appreciating modest, unadorned beauty.

korean.name: Dongwook
korean.meaning: Builds a great and upright strength in the east, a pillar of virtue whose solidity never shifts.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining dong (east) and wook (upright/beautiful). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names combining auspicious direction with moral uprightness.

korean.name: Hayoung
korean.meaning: Blooms with the grand and verdant abundance of a luxuriant growth under an auspicious sky.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining ha (grand/auspicious) and young (flourish/flower). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names combining auspicious grandeur with flourishing growth.

korean.name: Inho
korean.meaning: Expands the benevolent spirit into the grandeur of a great lake whose depth holds all within it.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining in (benevolence) and ho (lake/grand). Common in Korea; reflects the Confucian Korean tradition of names combining the virtue of benevolence with images of expansive natural grandeur.

korean.name: Jieun
korean.meaning: Holds the wisdom of a gracious and silvery gift whose value grows deeper with every passing year.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining ji (wisdom) and eun (grace/silver). Very common in Korea; reflects the Korean Confucian tradition of compound names combining intellectual virtue with graceful giving.

korean.name: Minhyeok
korean.meaning: Strikes with a quick and magnificent power whose swift brilliance leaves an impression that endures.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining min (quick/clever) and hyeok (magnificent/bright). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean naming tradition of compound names expressing swift and magnificent qualities.

korean.name: Saerom
korean.meaning: Refreshes like the first new rain that falls on dry earth and makes everything feel clean and alive.
korean.notes: A Korean female name meaning 'new and refreshing' in native Korean. Popular in Korea; reflects the modern trend of native Korean compound names expressing newness and renewal.

korean.name: Hyunwoo
korean.meaning: Illuminates the vast universe with a bright wisdom whose light reaches into every dark corner.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining hyun (bright/wise) and woo (universe/rain). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean naming tradition of compound names combining personal wisdom with cosmic scale.

korean.name: Eunjin
korean.meaning: Offers the precious gift of grace to all who come near, a silvery generosity freely bestowed.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining eun (grace/silver) and jin (precious/true). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean tradition of compound names combining gracious giving with preciousness.

korean.name: Junseo
korean.meaning: Steps forward with talent at the auspicious beginning, gifted from the very first moment of setting out.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining jun (talented/handsome) and seo (auspicious). Very popular in Korea; one of the top male names in recent years; reflects the Korean tradition of combining talent with auspicious beginnings.

korean.name: Minji
korean.meaning: Moves with the quick intelligence of the earth's wisdom, sharp and grounded in what is real.
korean.notes: A Korean female name combining min (clever/quick) and ji (wisdom/earth). Very common in Korea; one of the most popular female names across multiple generations; reflects the Korean Confucian tradition of combined intellectual virtues.

korean.name: Gyuwon
korean.meaning: Reaches back to the original source and draws from it the standard by which all things are measured.
korean.notes: A Korean male name combining gyu (standard/rule) and won (origin/source). Common in Korea; reflects the Korean Confucian tradition of names expressing the value of standards derived from original and foundational principles.

mongolian.name: Batbold
mongolian.meaning: Stands with the strength of steel, firm and unyielding as forged metal that holds its shape through all force.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining bat (strong/firm) and bold (steel/sword). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian naming tradition of compound strength names; steel imagery evokes the warrior heritage of the steppe.

mongolian.name: Oyunbileg
mongolian.meaning: Marks the intelligent mind with a sign of wisdom that cannot be hidden or overlooked by anyone.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining oyun (mind/intelligence) and bileg (mark/sign). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of compound female names combining intellectual qualities with symbolic marking.

mongolian.name: Ganbaatar
mongolian.meaning: Holds the strength of a steel hero whose unbreakable power has earned the greatest heroic title.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining gan (steel) and baatar (hero/brave warrior). Common in Mongolia; the Mongolian word baatar (hero) is famous worldwide through the capital Ulaanbaatar; this name evokes the ideal of the indomitable hero.

mongolian.name: Enkhjargal
mongolian.meaning: Rests in the peacefully joyful abundance of a life blessed with happiness that comes without struggle.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining enkh (peace) and jargal (happiness/joy). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian naming tradition of compound names combining peace with happiness as the ultimate aspiration.

mongolian.name: Tserenpuntsag
mongolian.meaning: Carries the long life of a lotus whose endurance holds the purity of a flower that outlasts all seasons.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian name combining tseren (long life, from Tibetan) and puntsag (lotus, from Tibetan). Common in Mongolia; reflects the deep Tibetan Buddhist influence on Mongolian naming culture through the strong historical ties between Mongolia and Tibet.

mongolian.name: Narantsetseg
mongolian.meaning: Blooms like a sunflower whose petals open wide to face the great warmth of the golden light above.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining naran (sun) and tsetseg (flower). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of solar flower compound names; the sunflower image connects celestial and earthly beauty.

mongolian.name: Munkh-Erdene
mongolian.meaning: Shines forever with the radiance of the eternal jewel whose brilliant worth never diminishes.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian name combining munkh (eternal) and erdene (jewel/treasure). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian Buddhist tradition of names combining eternity with precious gem imagery.

mongolian.name: Solongo
mongolian.meaning: Arches across the sky in brilliant multicolored glory after the rain has washed everything clean.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name meaning 'rainbow.' Common in Mongolia; the rainbow holds auspicious significance in Mongolian shamanic and Buddhist traditions as a bridge between heaven and earth.

mongolian.name: Baasanjav
mongolian.meaning: Carries the Friday victory, the triumph achieved at the auspicious moment when fortune favors.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining baasan (Friday, from Tibetan) and jav (victory). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist influence on Mongolian naming; children born on Fridays are often given names that include the day.

mongolian.name: Ariunaa
mongolian.meaning: Remains untouched by anything impure, holding its pristine quality like fresh snow on a still morning.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name meaning 'pure' or 'holy.' Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian Buddhist tradition of names expressing spiritual purity; one of the most beloved female names for its combination of clarity and beauty.

mongolian.name: Dorjpalam
mongolian.meaning: Holds the path illuminated by the indestructible clarity of the thunderbolt that cuts through all delusion.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining dorj (thunderbolt/vajra, from Tibetan) and palam (path). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Vajrayana Buddhist influence on Mongolian male naming; the vajra is the symbol of indestructible truth.

mongolian.name: Nominchimeg
mongolian.meaning: Adorns the turquoise jewel with a decorative beauty that makes the precious stone even more magnificent.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining nomin (turquoise/lapis lazuli) and chimeg (ornament/decoration). Common in Mongolia; turquoise is deeply significant in Mongolian culture as a protective stone; the addition of ornament elevates its value.

mongolian.name: Gantulga
mongolian.meaning: Stands as steadfast and unyielding as a fireplace of steel whose frame holds the flame through every storm.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining gan (steel) and tulga (fireplace/tripod). Common in Mongolia; the tulga is the central fireplace of the ger (yurt), the sacred heart of Mongolian domestic life; this name evokes the steel-strong protector of the home.

mongolian.name: Enkhmaa
mongolian.meaning: Bestows the mother's peace, the deep and encompassing calm that shelters all who rest within it.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining enkh (peace) and maa (mother). Common in Mongolia; one of the most warmly regarded female names; reflects the Mongolian cultural reverence for motherhood and the peace that a nurturing presence brings.

mongolian.name: Otgonbayar
mongolian.meaning: Brings the joyful gift of the youngest child, the last and most cherished one whose arrival is celebrated.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian name combining otgon (youngest child) and bayar (joy/celebration), used for both males and females. Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of naming the youngest child with a name that acknowledges their special place in the family.

mongolian.name: Tsetsegmaa
mongolian.meaning: Becomes the flowering mother whose blossoming beauty gives rise to everything beautiful around her.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining tsetseg (flower) and maa (mother). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of mother-flower compound names celebrating the nurturing and beautiful quality of femininity.

mongolian.name: Batsaikhan
mongolian.meaning: Combines the firm strength of enduring character with the noble beauty that distinguishes true excellence.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining bat (firm/strong) and saikhan (beautiful/excellent). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian ideal of combining physical and moral strength with genuine beauty of character.

mongolian.name: Uyanga
mongolian.meaning: Rings with the melodious sweetness of music whose harmonic beauty lingers long after the sound fades.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name meaning 'melody' or 'sweet-sounding.' Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian cultural tradition of musical names for women; the great Mongolian tradition of throat singing and folk music gives this name deep resonance.

mongolian.name: Tserenpurev
mongolian.meaning: Lives long under the watchful light of the sacred day, with life sustained by its auspicious protection.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining tseren (long life) and purev (Thursday, from Tibetan). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist influence on Mongolian naming through the tradition of associating names with the day of the week.

mongolian.name: Bolormaa
mongolian.meaning: Shines with the crystal clarity of the mother whose transparent and brilliant nature reflects the truth.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining bolor (crystal) and maa (mother). Common in Mongolia; crystal imagery suggests purity and transparency; one of the most beautiful and popular Mongolian female names.

mongolian.name: Khürelbaatar
mongolian.meaning: Stands as the bronze hero whose ancient and enduring strength has proven itself across all generations.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining khürel (bronze) and baatar (hero). Common in Mongolia; bronze evokes the ancient steppe civilizations predating steel; this name connects the heroic ideal with an older and more enduring strength.

mongolian.name: Oyuntsetseg
mongolian.meaning: Blooms like the flower of intelligence, a blossom whose beauty is the beauty of the mind itself.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining oyun (intelligence/mind) and tsetseg (flower). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of compound female names combining intellectual qualities with floral imagery.

mongolian.name: Sodnomdorj
mongolian.meaning: Carries the fortunate merit of the indestructible one whose accumulated goodness is as hard as diamond.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining sodnom (merit/fortune, from Tibetan) and dorj (thunderbolt/vajra). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Vajrayana influence on Mongolian Buddhist naming culture.

mongolian.name: Davaasuren
mongolian.meaning: Brings the moonlit strength of a Monday that rises powerful and reliable at the start of the week.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining davaa (Monday/moon, from Tibetan) and suren (strength/power). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist calendar influence on Mongolian naming; children born on Monday often receive davaa in their names.

mongolian.name: Möngöntuya
mongolian.meaning: Radiates with the silver light-ray whose precious cool gleam illuminates the surrounding darkness gently.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining möngön (silver) and tuya (ray of light). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of compound female names combining precious metals with luminous imagery.

mongolian.name: Amartuvshin
mongolian.meaning: Rests in the peaceful awakening of one whose tranquil clarity arises naturally from inner stillness.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining amar (peaceful/at rest) and tuvshin (level/awakening). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian Buddhist tradition of names combining peace with the concept of spiritual awakening or attainment.

mongolian.name: Altantsetseg
mongolian.meaning: Blossoms with the golden beauty of a flower whose color is as precious as the metal it evokes.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining altan (golden) and tsetseg (flower). Very common in Mongolia; one of the most beloved female names; the golden flower is the most classic combination in Mongolian female naming tradition.

mongolian.name: Tогтох
mongolian.meaning: Remains fixed and settled in one place, stable as the ancient earth that holds everything together.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name meaning 'to stay' or 'stable.' Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian nomadic tradition in which the concept of settled stability carries special weight; the name expresses a wish for a child's enduring life.

mongolian.name: Lkhagvasuren
mongolian.meaning: Draws power from the midweek abundance of the Wednesday that holds the week's finest strength.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining lkhagva (Wednesday, from Tibetan) and suren (strength). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist influence on Mongolian naming through the sacred calendar.

mongolian.name: Gantsogt
mongolian.meaning: Shines with the radiant brightness of the steel flame whose light and strength are one and the same.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining gan (steel) and tsogt (bright/flame). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian naming tradition of combining steel strength with luminous energy.

mongolian.name: Nomin-Erdene
mongolian.meaning: Holds the turquoise jewel in safekeeping, a precious treasure of deep blue whose value is beyond price.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining nomin (turquoise) and erdene (jewel). Common in Mongolia; turquoise combined with jewel creates a layered gem image; reflects the Mongolian Buddhist tradition of precious stone names.

mongolian.name: Bayanjargal
mongolian.meaning: Lives in the wealthy joy of abundance whose happiness comes from the fullness of having more than enough.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining bayan (wealthy/rich) and jargal (happiness). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian cultural aspiration for material abundance combined with genuine happiness.

mongolian.name: Delgermaa
mongolian.meaning: Expands like the vast and generous mother whose abundance spreads out to fill every horizon.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining delger (expansive/prosperous) and maa (mother). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian steppe landscape in which the image of vast open expansion is deeply embedded in cultural identity.

mongolian.name: Sangijav
mongolian.meaning: Claims the holy victory that arises from the sacred and purified quality of a life well lived.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining sangi (holy/pure, from Tibetan) and jav (victory). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Tibetan Buddhist influence on Mongolian male naming through the combination of holiness with victory.

mongolian.name: Uranbileg
mongolian.meaning: Bears the sign of artistic mastery, a mark that identifies one gifted in the making of beautiful things.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining uran (artistic/skillful) and bileg (sign/mark). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian cultural tradition of artistic craftsmanship in felt-making, metalwork, and other traditional arts.

mongolian.name: Gombosuren
mongolian.meaning: Draws the protection and strength of the great guardian whose power shields those placed in its care.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian male name combining gombo (protector/Mahakala, from Tibetan) and suren (strength). Common in Mongolia; Mahakala (Gomboo) is the great protector deity of Tibetan and Mongolian Buddhism; this name invokes his protective power.

mongolian.name: Erdenechimeg
mongolian.meaning: Adorns itself with the jewel's own ornamentation, the precious stone made even more magnificent by decoration.
mongolian.notes: A Mongolian female name combining erdene (jewel) and chimeg (ornament/decoration). Common in Mongolia; reflects the Mongolian tradition of compound names in which the jewel is further adorned, suggesting layered and elaborated beauty.

mongolian.name: Khongorzul
mongolian.meaning: Blooms like the lotus of the beloved one whose sweet and cherished nature opens into pure beauty.
